title: Revenue Insight Center - Assistant Manager
description:
Who we are looking for

The Senior Associate – Revenue Insight Center provides subject matter expertise in Financial Planning & Analysis, with a strong focus on software-based revenue, lending related revenue and other fee revenue models for complex custody bank revenue streams. The role requires developing a deep understanding of State Street's business practices across multiple Business Units.

We are looking for a technically strong, forward-thinking analyst who can not only perform complex reporting and analytics with a strong level of independence but also modernize and automate how that analysis is built and delivered. The individual will support ad-hoc requirements of the Revenue IC Lead and Business Unit Chief Financial Officers (CFOs) for Investment Services, while bringing a continuous-improvement mindset to reporting tools and processes.

What you will be responsible for

  • Monthly revenue waterfalls including variance analysis, along with preparation of management presentations describing key insights
  • Month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close and forecast, as well as the Annual Operational Planning process (budget) and Medium-Term Outlook (MTO) forecasting
  • Conduct detailed variance analysis and highlight key insights and drivers for the Investment Services Business
  • Provide a holistic view of business performance, including new business, pipeline, backlog installations and revenue backlog for Front Office Businesses (leveraging Salesforce CRM data), and retention metrics
  • Build, automate, and maintain interactive dashboards and reporting (Power BI / Tableau) to streamline recurring reporting and improve the speed and quality of insights
  • Manage multiple stakeholders and deliverables simultaneously across Outlook (forecast) cycles and month-end reporting cycles
  • May be required to participate in or conduct a wide range of ad-hoc financial analyses on critical and complex issues
  • May develop and/or update various financial models and automated workflows to generate analyses
  • Collaborate across geographies to promote consistency, knowledge sharing, and accuracy in reporting

Job Requirements

Education & Experience

  • 6–9 years of experience in Financial Planning & Analysis
  • Experience supporting a software-based business OR investment services custody businesses in general, or product line management / senior executives
  • Proven experience in financial statement preparation and analysis within a global environment
  • Demonstrated experience building automated reporting solutions and data visualizations that reduce manual effort and improve reporting cycle times

What we value

  • Advanced proficiency with data visualization and finance automation tools – Power BI and/or Tableau (data modeling, DAX/measures, dashboard design and maintenance) and workflow automation tools such as Alteryx
  • Working knowledge of Hyperion Essbase, SmartView, and Salesforce 360 / CRM data
  • Knowledge of US GAAP, particularly ASC 606, is an added advantage
  • Knowledge & experience in a software revenue business
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite:
    • Excel (pivot tables, advanced formulas, workbook maintenance best practices)
    • Power BI dashboards (power queries, measures, maintenance of dashboards)
    • Copilot, Access, PowerPoint, Word
  • Ability to operate effectively under pressure in a fast-paced, dynamic, and evolving delivery environment
  • Strong communication skills with high attention to detail and strong organizational skills
  • Ability to prioritize workload and manage stakeholder expectations across concurrent cycles
  • A positive, proactive, and forward-thinking mindset – someone who anticipates needs, drives process improvement, and looks for smarter ways to work
  • Strong problem-solving capability

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

title: Registered Nurse
description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Utilises advanced nephrology nursing skills in the design, planning delivery and evaluation of patient care within the haemodialysis clinical setting.
  • Protects the rights of patients by adhering to Privacy Principles and providing an excellent standard of care.
  • Functions as the primary co-ordinator of care for a group of dialysis patients, collaborating with other health care providers in developing and implementing individualised plans of care.
  • Responsible for own professional development. Participates in professional development activities, attending both internal and external education programs, to ensure maintenance and updating of professional knowledge and skills for clinical competence.
  • Completes clinical and mandatory competencies annually
  • Provides education and support to the patient and patient’s carers, to facilitate the attainment, maintenance and restoration of optimal health and well-being.
  • Provides support and guidance to the level 1 registered nurses, enrolled nurses and PSA’s working within the facility and delivers care as part of a nursing team.
  • Performs staff education and assessment, acting as a preceptor and role model as required.
  • Provides education, support and guidance to any visitor in the clinic.
  • Ensures/promotes a safe, secure and healthy working environment to staff and a comfortable, safe environment for visitors and patients as detailed in the Workplace Health and Safety policy (FMEAU-28).
  • Understanding of the Quality improvement process and risk management obligations
  • Participates in clinic management and business development by adopting a portfolio of responsibilities additional to direct patient care. This may include, but is not limited to the following;
    • quality improvement co-ordination
    • infection control promotion / monitoring
    • occupational health and safety promotion / monitoring
  • Co-ordination / delivery of staff education and professional development programs
  • Clinical research projects
  • Actively participates in the quality improvement process by reporting all incidents, hazards and opportunities for improvement.
  • Participates in the review of organisational policies and procedures as required.
  • Deputises in a higher-level position as appropriate and when requested.
  • Liaise with Nephrologists and other members of the Allied Health Team.
  • Maintains accurate patient information in medical records, including Therapy Data Management System (TDMS) and abides by confidentiality and Privacy Policy.

Essential Competencies:

  • Registered as a general nurse with AHPRA (Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency)
  • Current practising certificate.
  • Minimum three years current relevant nursing experience
  • Holds / working towards an approved nephrology nursing qualification
  • Ability to continuously reflect on nursing practice and apply learning as appropriate.
  • Demonstrated organisational, decision-making and problem-solving skills, combined with the ability to be self-directed.
  • Computer literacy
  • Demonstrated commitment to quality improvement initiatives.
  • Demonstrated advanced clinical competence and expertise in nephrology nursing
  • Demonstrated ability to effectively perform staff and patient education
  • Effective communication and interpersonal skills including the ability to operate within a multidisciplinary team
  • Self-motivated with an ability to work independently
  • Role Model

title: Front-end Software Engineer
description:
Twoday is the premier digital transformation partner in Northern Europe with a global presence. With approximately 3,000 technologists, we collaborate with the most admired private and public sector organizations to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions. Our deep industry expertise includes Data & AI, software engineering, digital experiences and business applications. We serve over 8,000 customers and facilitate their digital transformation journeys.

Technology stack:

Angular, TypeScript, HTML / CSS, Tailwind CSS, RESTful API, C# / .NET (nice to have)

In this role you will:

  • Build modern, responsive user interfaces using the latest versions of Angular and TypeScript;

  • Develop clean, scalable, and accessible HTML/CSS structures for complex layouts;

  • Integrate front-end features with RESTful APIs and collaborate closely with backend engineers;

  • Apply robust state-management practices to ensure predictable and maintainable UI behavior;

  • Use established UI component libraries to accelerate development and maintain visual consistency;

  • Write and maintain front-end tests to ensure quality, reliability, and prevent regressions;

  • Contribute to UX and UI enhancements, performance optimizations, and key architectural decisions;

  • Partner with designers, QA engineers, and product owners to deliver polished, intuitive user experiences.

What we do expect:

  • Solid experience with Angular (modern versions);

  • Strong understanding of TypeScript, browser APIs, and frontend fundamentals;

  • Proficiency in HTML/CSS, Tailwind CSS, responsive design, and clean UI architecture;

  • Experience integrating with RESTful APIs;

  • Understanding of state management — RxJS patterns, signals, or NgRx;

  • Experience using UI component libraries (Ant Design / ng-zorro or similar);

  • Ability to write and maintain front-end tests (unit/integration/e2e);

  • Clear communication, a collaborative mindset, and attention to detail.

Nice to have:

  • Familiarity with C# / .NET backend code for reading API contracts and tracing issues across layers.

What you should expect:

  • Committees: from the conferences we attend to ideas like workplace design - you’ll have the chance to create an organisation you dream to work in by joining 1 of 10 our committees.

  • Healthcare: health insurance and mental & emotional support from third-party consultants.

  • Pension accumulation: the state encourages participation in pension accumulation programs by applying tax benefits.

  • Flextime: flexible working hours, flexible work from home policy, office workplace and free parking.

  • Leisure together: excursions, team buildings, pizza Fridays, techie club, sports training or board games nights. We also have a sauna!

  • Gross salary: 3500-6000 EUR. Your salary is based on your competence and experience.

title: Senior Pattern Cutter
description:
About TOTEME

TOTEME is a fashion house rooted in Swedish sensibilities, modern aspirations and the notion of style. With an appreciation for women’s many roles and how pieces are worn in practice, we create emblematic designs with an emphasis on materiality and shape. Representing a direct and decisive way of dressing, the collections are contextualized in curated edits, visuals and spaces.

TOTEME was founded in 2014 by Elin Kling and Karl Lindman. From the atelier in Stockholm, we create collections spanning ready-to-wear, shoes, bags, accessories and jewelry.

The Role

TOTEME is looking for a Senior Pattern Cutter to join our team in Stockholm full-time. You will work closely with Product Development and Design in creating our collections. As a part of the Quality & Technical team, you will report to the Head of Quality & Technical.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and refine patterns across multiple product categories, ensuring precision, consistency, and alignment with brand standards.

  • Own and maintain measurement charts within Product Lifecycle Management (PLM), driving accuracy throughout the product lifecycle.

  • Oversee the end-to-end pattern process, from initial concept and prototype through fittings to final production, ensuring the highest standards of fit, quality, and manufacturability.

  • Drive and execute fittings, translating feedback into technical improvements and scalable production outcomes.

  • Leverage 3D design tools (CLO) to create and evaluate virtual samples, enhancing efficiency, reducing physical sampling, and supporting sustainable product development.

  • Serve as a mentor within the team encouraging craftsmanship and innovation.

  • Act as a key expert and advisor within the organization, providing strategic input, and technical guidance across departments.

  • Participate in and contribute to TOTEME's social and environmental sustainability strategy.

Your Profile

  • Minimum 10 years of working experience within pattern making, preferably within luxury brands, with extensive experience in a wide range product groups.

  • Excellent knowledge of garment construction and tailoring.

  • Skilled user of Lectra Expert, CLO, and Illustrator.

  • Technical diploma or degree in related fields.

  • Fluent in spoken and written English.

About You

  • A TOTEME ambassador who lives and breathes our culture and values.

  • Great attention to detail and a penchant for accuracy.

  • An understanding of luxury business, customers, competitors and products.

  • Self-motivated, solution-oriented and hands-on.

  • A strong team player with a positive outlook and collaborative approach.

  • An organized multitasker with strong problem-solving skills.

This is a permanent position based at TOTEME's Headquarters in Stockholm with a start in September 2026.

TOTEME has teams in Stockholm, London, New York, and Shanghai, as well as retail spaces across Europe, North America, and Asia. As a member of our dynamic, creative, and highly collaborative team, you will take part in exciting projects and milestones, evolving within your role as you contribute to the company’s growth.

TOTEME stands for inclusivity and a sense of belonging in the workplace. We welcome all applicants and recruit on the basis of skills and experience.

By applying to this position I agree with TOTEME’s privacy policy and to be contacted about future job opportunities for up to 2 years from the submission date. Find out more about our privacy policy here.

title: Controls Transformation and Automation - Vice President
description:
Who we are looking for:

Global Technology is transforming the controls function to enhance control rationalization and incorporate robust system integrations and automations. The position will support this transformation to drive increased efficiency and effectiveness across State Street’s cybersecurity and technology risk and control framework.

As the Technology Controls Transformation and Automation Vice President, you will:

  • Lead and/or support key automation areas, such as: Automation Opportunity Identification, Source Data and System Integration, Artificial Intelligence Integration, Automated Control Implementation

  • Lead control rationalization initiatives, including enhancing the controls library framework and collaborating with stakeholders to align the existing controls library to this framework

  • Support the end-to-end control transformation process, including adapting and strategizing to optimize control designing, rationalization, automation, and controls alignment to risks, processes, and standards

  • Ensure that the risk and control assessment activities are delivered accurately and timely, in line with the State Street framework and methodology.

  • Drive enhancement of leadership reporting around the status and outcomes of control automation and rationalization activities

  • Reinforce an inherent culture of accountability and ownership for implementation and execution of controls across all levels and functions within Global Technology Services.

  • Collaborate closely with several stakeholder groups to support control automation and control rationalization, including system owners, risk & control functions, and all three lines of defense

  • Work in conjunction with other Technology Risk and Controls departments as well as business partners and external parties as needed to resolve issues and support adherence to applicable policies, standards, and regulations.


These skills will help you succeed in this role:

  • Must have the ability to operate in a timely and deadline-oriented environment, while delivering results in an efficient and effective manner

  • Strong understanding of the integration of risks, standards, controls, findings, industry frameworks, and regulations across technology domains

  • Strong technical skills and problem solving, including a solid understanding of how to use common enterprise technologies to effectively automate control testing

  • Proven experience with IRM Solutions such as Archer is required

  • Familiarity with programming, scripting, and/or command line interfaces such as VBA, Python, PowerShell, etc. is a plus

  • Experience with automation, artificial intelligence, or data analytics tools (e.g. Alteryx, Blue Prism, Databricks, etc.) is a plus

  • Advanced Microsoft Office, particularly Excel is required

  • Ability to interact with and communicate professionally with multiple levels of management in multiple regions

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, ability to express ideas and understand workflows

  • Strong time management skills, problem-solving and critical thinking skills.

  • Must be detail-oriented

  • Experience working in the Financial industry preferred

  • Prior knowledge of the State Street technology and control environment is a plus

Education & Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science / Information Technology, IT Security, Risk Management, or a related field; Masters degree a plus

  • Overall experience of 15 – 18 + years in Industry

  • Minimum 12 years of experience working in cybersecurity, technology operations, technology automation or artificial intelligence, technology control management, technology audit, and/or technology consulting. Minimum of 6 years of leadership experience in these areas.

  • Preference for: Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Information Security Auditor (CISA), Certified Risk & Information Systems Controls (CRISC), and/or technical certifications supporting technology automation or artificial intelligence

Why this role is important to us:

Our technology function, Global Technology Services (GTS), is vital to State Street and is the key enabler for our business to deliver data and insights to our clients. We’re driving the company’s digital transformation and expanding business capabilities using industry best practices and advanced technologies such as cloud, artificial intelligence and robotics process automation.

We offer a collaborative environment where technology skills and innovation are valued in a global organization. We’re looking for top technical talent to join our team and deliver creative technology solutions that help us become an end-to-end, next-generation financial services company.

Join us if you want to grow your technical skills, solve real problems and make your mark on our industry.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

title: Senior Managing Counsel, Blockchain & Digital Assets
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Managing Counsel, Blockchain & Digital Assets

Job Description, Senior Managing Counsel, Digital Assets & Strategic Product Integration

Who is Mastercard?
Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.
Overview
Mastercard is looking for an experienced lawyer to serve as a key legal advisor supporting the integration of BVNK and the development of next-generation digital asset, stablecoin, cross-border payments, and embedded finance solutions. This role will partner closely with multiple teams and stakeholders across Mastercard and BVNK including Product, Business Development, Privacy, Compliance, and Regulatory teams to enable strategic revenue synergies, accelerate go-to-market readiness, and support Mastercard's evolving digital assets ecosystem.

The position requires strong judgment, strategic thinking, and the ability to navigate complex regulatory and commercial issues in a rapidly evolving environment. The ideal candidate is highly motivated, intellectually curious and analytical, dedicated to delivering strategic and hands-on legal support and partnership to develop the next generation of Mastercard product solutions focused on enabling the future of payments.
Role
As Senior Managing Counsel, you will be a member of the Law Department supporting Blockchain & Digital Assets team under Mastercard’s Core Payments business. You will handle product development and support matters for the business on a day-to-day basis, identifying and analyzing relevant issues and helping the business effectively navigate legal and regulatory risks and implement appropriate controls, policies and processes. You will:
• Lead legal support for the strategic development and daily operations of Mastercard’s global digital assets products and services, reporting into the Assistant General Counsel for Core Payments.
• Manage tactical and strategic legal support related to products and solutions to advance Mastercard’s corporate objectives, including Mastercard’s stablecoin, digital assets, and blockchain strategy.
• Develop strong relationships with senior management, product development and business and sales clients.
• Collaborate with product, technology, regulatory, sales, franchise, marketing, communications and others to develop and launch innovative products and solutions that differentiate Mastercard and meet our customers’ and cardholders’ rapidly changing needs.
• Assist executive level clients, and others, in managing and mitigating legal, regulatory and commercial risks by providing solution oriented guidance.
• Assist in the monitoring and evaluation of applicable laws and government regulations affecting the operation and product development of Mastercard’s digital assets business.
• Inform policy and regulatory positions related to Mastercard’s products and innovation with relevant internal and external stakeholders.
• Advise from the concept through development to launch stage and beyond of various solutions and services:
o ensuring alignment and compliance of products and services with Mastercard’s policies, procedures and processes and with local and global regulatory and commercial laws;
o covering a broad range of commercial and contractual matters, including product and marketing legal review, non-disclosure agreements, pilot agreements, and bid process support;
o draft, review and negotiate a wide range of contracts (including complex technology agreements, customer and vendor agreements).
• Identify needed process improvements and efficiencies; own and execute solutions to support Mastercard’s transformation strategy and streamline processes to increase ease of doing business.
• Take responsibility for special projects while continuing to handle a steady stream of day-to-day matters.
• Instruct and manage external counsel within agreed policy and budget.

Please note that roles within Mastercard evolve with the needs of the business and department. Therefore, the above is a guideline and by no means comprehensive.

Location
This role will be based in Purchase, NY. As our team supports the business globally, the role may require some travel and ability to support other time zones.

All About You
• A qualified lawyer with a solid academic and training background, licensed to practice in New York.
• Experience in the financial services industry, emerging technology, stablecoin and other digital currencies, and the use of public and private blockchains.
• Experience working on products and solutions involving digital assets and advising on related legal issues. Good working knowledge of the regulatory landscape and legal issues relating to digital assets.
• Experience across a range of commercial and technology contracts and the ability to analyze and identify the core legal issues, skilled at explaining technical problems succinctly and clearly.
• A working knowledge of data privacy and intellectual property issues and how they relate to emerging technology such as digital assets.
• A fast learner who can gain rapid familiarity with complex products, services and legal frameworks and maintain knowledge of those changing products, services and legal frameworks.
• Solutions oriented with excellent time management, planning and organizational skills.
• Agile and flexible while working under pressure and with tight deadlines.
• Strong drafting, writing, oral communication and negotiating skills, able to communicate your vision and roadmap effectively to a wide variety of stakeholders.
• Exceptional interpersonal skills with proven experience in relationship building and partnering. Must work well in both team and individual settings.
• Strong business acumen.
• Language: Fluent English.

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

Purchase, New York: $239,000 - $383,000 USD

title: Dar Clases de Piano- Sin Experiencia
description:
¿Tocas el piano y quieres ganar dinero enseñando lo que te apasiona? En Tusclasesparticulares, portal líder de clases particulares en España, tenemos alumnos de todos los niveles buscando profesor de piano ahora mismo — y queremos contar contigo.

  • Impartir clases personalizadas adaptadas al nivel y objetivos de cada alumno, desde iniciación hasta nivel avanzado.

Requisitos:

Qué ofrecemos:

  • Retribución competitiva de 15 a 30€/hora según experiencia y dedicación.

  • Visibilidad ante miles de alumnos y familias activos en nuestra plataforma cada mes.

💡 Con o sin experiencia docente. Si tocas el piano y tienes ganas de enseñar, este es tu sitio.

title: Principal, Platform Architecture
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Principal, Platform Architecture

Overview

The Distributed Platforms team is looking for a Senior Platform Architect to own and develop a cohesive architecture across our various on-premise compute, storage and runtime products and services, along with owning our hardware standards ensuring we are optimally placed to provide our customers with the best possible experience today and in the future across storage, CPU compute and GPU compute platforms. The ideal candidate must have an infrastructure architecture background with a passion for understanding how various compute and storage products and services interact with each other and how they fit together to provide a common architecture across our global datacenters.

Role & Responsibilities
In this architecture role, you will:
• Own and evolve end to end compute and storage architectural patterns and designs for the Distributed Platforms program.
• Partner closely with subject matter experts (SMEs) across engineering teams, as well as cross domain pattern teams (networking, databases, datacenter engineering), to define and execute strategic platform roadmaps.
• Define and lead the GPU and AI infrastructure strategy spanning compute and storage platforms.
• Establish, maintain, and govern architecture standards, reference designs, and best practices to ensure consistency, scalability, and operational excellence.
• Influence platform direction by balancing technical rigor, operational feasibility, and customer needs.
• Act as a trusted architectural authority across infrastructure, platform, and product teams.

All About You
The ideal candidate for this position will demonstrate:
• More than 10 years of proven experience owning and designing large scale compute and storage architectures in enterprise or datacenter environments.
• Strong hands-on knowledge across core infrastructure technologies, including:
o Linux and Windows
o VMware
o Storage platforms (file, object, and block)
o Kubernetes, Tanzu / PCF, and modern container platforms
• Experience designing infrastructure to support GPU accelerated AI/ML workloads.
• A strong foundational understanding of networking and datacenter infrastructure.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, high quality architectural documentation.
• A product and service-oriented mindset, with the ability to understand and anticipate customer and platform consumer needs.
• A collaborative leadership style, with a passion for bringing together SMEs across multiple domains to define holistic, scalable architectural standards.

Corporate Security Responsibility
Every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security. All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and therefore, it is expected that the successful candidate for this position must:

• Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices.
• Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed.
• Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
• Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Clinical Education Manager
description:
Monday - Friday schedule, no nights/weekends/holidays/on call
Rewarding development of direct patient care staff
Structured professional development to enhance personal growth
Collaboration with operational and clinical leaders to ensure quality patient care is provided to our dialysis patients
Facilitation and implementation of our world-class dialysis training program
Opportunity for professional development by participating in national projects related to the development of our staff Ability to support all dialysis modalities

PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Supports the training and education of employees based in company facilities and programs within an assigned geographical area. Delivers standardized, competency based, training and development programs including new employee orientation, preceptor training, facility specific in- services, and clinical, quality, and customer service training programs to promote quality patient care, and achieve Area, Regional and/or Enterprise wide initiatives & goals.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Aligns with regional, group, and corporate, business-related goals through all activities and actions.
  • Adheres to standards and scope of nursing practice, company policy, procedures, compliance, regulatory, legal, and ethical, requirements.
  • Focuses all programs towards achieving a culture of patient safety, clinical quality goals, and improving patient outcomes, using company and industry guidelines and standards.
  • Develops competency and skills to educate personnel in all chronic kidney disease treatment programs.
  • Empowers and promotes a culture of lifelong learning through professional programs, coaching, and available technologies.
  • Collaborates with relevant partners at all levels to schedule, provide, and implement education programs, technologies, and processes for new and current personnel.
  • Utilizes appropriate educational venues and generational learning techniques to deliver, support, and evaluate training.
  • Partners in the implementation of action plans through to resolution, as appropriate.
  • Uses facilitation techniques and the variety of tools and resources available to promote immersive, engaging, learning experiences.
  • Develops and maintains open, effective, and timely communication with internal and external customers such as, but not limited to, company management and facility team, government agencies, physicians, hospitals, and contracted travel personnel.
  • Demonstrates leadership competencies, excellent communication, customer service, continuous quality improvement, relationship development, critical thinking, team building, motivating employees, performance management, and decision- making skills.
  • Supports metric / KPI programs, with timely and complete data documentation, reporting, and ongoing quality improvement processes.
  • Maintains knowledge of products, services, and strategies to support and train staff appropriately in all modalities.
  • Perform other related duties as assigned.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
    • Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, Regional and facility/program employees and physicians. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, departmental, and corporate meetings may be required.
    • The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases / materials.
    • This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over. The employee may occasionally be required to move, with assistance, machines, and equipment of up to 200 lbs., and may lift chemical and water solutions of up to 30 lbs. as high as 5 feet.
    • May be asked to provide essential functions of this position in other locations which may include overnight stays, with the same physical demands and working conditions as described above.
  • This position requires between approximately 35-80% travel

SUPERVISION:

  • None

EDUCATION AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

  • Associate or Bachelor’s degree from an accredited School of Nursing.
  • Advanced degree BSN or MSN (preferred)
  • Current appropriate RN state licensure in primary state of residence
  • Obtain and maintain RN state licensure in assigned states as directed
  • Must meet the practice requirements in the state of employment
  • Minimum 1-year dialysis experience as a Registered Nurse
  • CDN or CNN (preferred) or must attain within 18 months of employment
  • Successful completion and maintenance of CPR BLS (Basic Life Safety) Certification, all annual and ongoing required education / training / competencies.

The rate of pay for this position will depend on the successful candidate’s work location and qualifications, including relevant education, work experience, skills, and competencies.

Annual Rate: $79,000.00 - $133,000.00

Non-Bonus Eligible Positions: include language below.
Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave.

Bonus Eligible Positions – include language below.
Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave and potential for performance-based bonuses depending on company and individual performance.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Senior Global Roaming Coordinator
description:
Position Title: Senior Global Roaming Coordinator

Location: Islamabad

Company: Telenor Shared Services (TSSPK)

Department: Roaming Delivery / Telenor Linx

Why you should join Telenor Shared Services Pakistan

At Telenor Shared Services Pakistan, you get the opportunity to grow your expertise in an inclusive and supportive environment. You will work with international teams, contribute to meaningful projects, and develop skills that help you move forward in your career.

About Telenor Shared Services Pakistan

Telenor Shared Services Pakistan delivers transactional business services in Finance & Accounting and IT. Together with Telenor Shared Services Norway, we support employees across Telenor globally by making everyday work simpler and more efficient.

Position Related

This position is placed in Telenor Linx (TLX) which is a new global unit within Telenor Group. TLX is a trusted global partner for Mobile Network Operators and Digital Service Providers, for market leading software to enable digital authentication, payment, and third-party sales & distribution. We uniquely combine the traditional world of telecom with software flexibility. For more information about Telenor Linx and our operations, visit telenorlinx.com.

We are seeking an experienced Global Roaming Coordinator. Primarily, the Global Roaming Coordinator will be responsible for coordinating the operational execution of the roll-out plan provided by the Roaming Coverage Manager(s).

Goals

  • Establish contact with Roaming Partners

  • Negotiate International Roaming Agreements (IRAs)

  • Drive and coordinate testing and launches according to roaming roll-out plan

  • Try to solve and if necessary, escalate potential issues that may jeopardize the targets

  • Ensure all documentation and status is updated and kept in our Roaming Management Tool

Actively collaborate with the other members of the team and strive towards making the rollout of roaming services as effective as possible

Key responsibilities

• Establish and maintain communication with roaming partners
• Support negotiation and coordination of International Roaming Agreements (IRAs)
• Drive testing, implementation, and launch of roaming services
• Monitor progress against rollout plans and highlight risks or delays
• Resolve operational challenges and escalate issues when required
• Maintain accurate documentation and reporting in relevant systems
• Collaborate closely with internal teams across multiple regions

Eligibility Criteria

Experience
• 7+ years of experience in mobile roaming or telecom operations
• Experience in coordination roles within roaming rollout or similar functions

Skills:
• Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
• Effective communication and stakeholder management
• Ability to work in a structured and systematic manner
• Collaborative mindset with cross-cultural awareness

Education:
• Masters/bachelor’s degree and must have basic technical understanding on mobile or mobile roaming

Functional Skills / Knowledge Areas

  • A collaborative and goal-oriented approach

  • Proactive and having a structured approach – ability to break down problem into its parts

  • Having excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with clients and stakeholders at all levels and among different countries and culture

  • Insight into IREG- and/or TADIG is valuable) either from working with mobile operator or vendor (Huawei, Wipro, or other)

  • Due diligence abilities and time management skills

  • A persistent attitude that addresses challenges and seeks effective solutions

  • Commitment to achieving shared goals

  • Strong knowledge of the roaming roll-out process

  • Ability to communicate effectively with partners and colleagues using the English language (read, write, talk)

  • Additional language skills will be regarded as a plus

  • Experienced in working with Microsoft Office (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint and Word) as well as utilizing virtual workspaces (Teams, Sharepoint, etc.)

  • Potential Career Path

• Opportunities to move into roles such as Roaming Manager or Service Delivery Lead
• Exposure to broader telecom operations and international project management

title: Manager, Product Management
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Manager, Product Management

Overview
The Global Authentication Product team is looking for a Manager, Product Management to help drive the execution, integration, and adoption of Digital Payment Credentials (DPC), with a primary focus on digital identity wallet ecosystems & integration initiatives.

The role serves as a key bridge between Global Product, Engineering, Regional Product teams, wallet providers, issuers, and external identity ecosystem stakeholders.

The ideal candidate combines strong product management discipline, technical understanding, and ecosystem engagement capabilities. The individual can drive execution across multiple initiatives while translating complex technical and regulatory requirements into practical, scalable solutions.

Role
In this role, you will:

Product Delivery & Program Execution
• Drive delivery of Digital Payment Credential capabilities across priority initiatives and markets.
• Manage delivery milestones, dependencies, risks, and stakeholder communications.
• Coordinate cross-functional teams to ensure successful execution and market readiness.

Identity Wallets Integration
• Lead product initiatives related to Identity Wallets integration and related digital identity ecosystems.
• Collaborate with wallet providers, issuers, ecosystem partners, and engineering teams to enable interoperability.
• Define integration approaches, requirements, and implementation guidance.
• Support pilot execution and solution validation activities.

Product Development & Execution
• Translate business opportunities into structured product requirements and technical specifications.
• Partner closely with Engineering, Architecture, Legal, Security, and CX&D teams.
• Support Studio processes and product development frameworks.
• Contribute to business case development and prioritization exercises.

Go-To-Market & Launch
• Partner with Commercialization, Marketing, Sales, and Regional Product organizations to develop customer-facing materials.
• Contribute to product documentation, implementation guides, training assets, and solution briefs.

Research & Innovation
• Monitor developments related to eIDAS, EUDI Wallets, OpenID, FIDO Alliance, EMVCo, W3C, and adjacent industry initiatives.
• Evaluate ecosystem trends and emerging identity technologies.

Performance Monitoring & Optimization
• Track product adoption, pilot outcomes, customer feedback, and operational performance metrics.
• Ensure feedback loops are established between customers, regions, and product teams.

All About You
• Bachelor's degree required; Master's degree preferred.
• Experience in Product Management, Program Management, payments, authentication, digital identity, or adjacent technology domains.
• Demonstrated ability to manage complex cross-functional initiatives.
• Understanding of authentication technologies, digital identity ecosystems, wallet architectures, or payment platforms.
• Experience working with external partners and ecosystem stakeholders.
• Ability to synthesize technical, regulatory, and business requirements into actionable product direction.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• Comfortable operating in a dynamic environment with multiple concurrent priorities.
• Familiarity with digital identity standards, Verifiable Credentials, OpenID technologies, FIDO standards, eIDAS, EUDI Wallets, or EMVCo specifications is a strong plus.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Personlig assistent sökes till kvinna i Uppsala
description:
Är du min nästa assistent och vår nästa kollega? Vill du tillhöra en assistentgrupp som tillsammans arbetar för att sätta guldkant på vår kunds tillvaro?

Vi på Vår Omtanke söker nu flera personliga assistenter som vill arbeta med vår kvinnliga kund i Uppsala!
I rollen kommer du göra skillnad i människors liv och tillsammans med oss arbeta efter vår vision – Alla har rätt till ett bra liv.

Om tjänsten
Som assistent i det här uppdraget kommer du att assistera kunden, hjälpa till med matlagning, hjälpa kunden med hennes personliga hygien och vara behjälplig vid förflyttning.
Talar du persiska (farsi), är duktig på att laga mat är det ett plus men absolut inte något krav.

Vem är du?
Du är en ärlig och pålitlig person som genuint tycker om att arbeta och du äger förmågan att kunna skapa en harmonisk atmosfär omkring dig. Du bör ha stor empati, ha tålamod samt kunna sätta dig in i en annan människas situation och vara mycket lyhörd. Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av att arbeta som personlig assistent eller har annan erfarenhet som kan vara likvärdig. Som person har du en god social förmåga, ser till hela kundens behov, är noggrann och ansvarstagande samt har ett gott bemötande. Endast kvinnliga sökande efter önskemål från kund.

Vad får du?

Friskvård, utbildning och väl introduktion.

Arbetstid och tjänstgöringsgrad:

Ett stort plus om du kan arbeta extra vid behov.

Lön: Fast timlön enligt överenskommelse, övriga ersättningar sker enligt kollektivavtal.

Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse

Vad händer nu?
Vi ser gärna att du ansöker snarast då vi går igenom urvalet löpande och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.

Din ansökan läses av personal på Vår Omtanke.

Vill du veta mer om tjänsten eller rekryteringsprocessen är du varmt välkommen att kontakta oss!

Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Grid Solutions Engineer
description:
About Ameren Services (B&CS)

Ameren Services provides administrative support and services to Ameren Corporation and its operating companies, subsidiaries and affiliates. Ameren Services includes a wide range of skill sets and roles, from finance and legal experts to digital and cyber specialists, plus those charged with ensuring environmental compliance and operational safety. Together, we help execute a strategy that enables Ameren to deliver superior long-term value to customers, shareholders and the environment.

Our benefits include:

  • Medical coverage on date of hire

  • 100% employer paid cash balance pension plan

  • 401(k) with company match fully vested on date of hire

  • Minimum of 15 days paid vacation and 12 paid holidays

  • Paid parental leave and family caregiver leave

Visit our Benefits & Perks Page for more information on benefits provided to regular full-time employees. ​

About The Position

It has never been a more exciting time to work in the energy industry. The electric distribution grid is in transformation to a smarter, more resilient, cleaner, and greener (solar, wind) grid. Advanced systems for grid monitoring and control will be at the forefront of these efforts. This position requires an Engineer to collaborate with a talented team within Digital and Operations to design, implement, and maintain our leading-edge technology suite known as the Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Our ADMS platform is utilized within our Distribution Control Center and provides operators with real time awareness and control of the Ameren Distribution electric grid serving over 2.4 million customers. From a more technical perspective the ADMS platform is comprised of an industry leading vendor solution including SCADA – Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition, Outage Management, Distribution Management (maps and switching), Voltage Optimization, and other advanced functions. In addition, we have integrated the platform with many other corporate systems and technologies to enable interaction with our Customers reporting and checking power outages, and field service/mobile workers responding to those situations.

In this position you will be involved in crafting and building the future of electric utility operations.

The position leverages a flexible time work schedule that is completely remote with occasional visits to the Ameren Headquarters in Missouri and field locations across the service territory. The ADMS system has a 24x7x365 availability requirement and so applicant will be required to provide off hours support as needed.

A diverse technology portfolio keeps the ADMS system operational, technologies include: Virtual VxRail Linux and Windows Servers, Multiple Database technologies (Oracle, Sql Server, Cassandra), Palo Alto Firewalls, Mulesoft Enterprise Service Bus, Splunk SIEM, Dynatrace Application Monitoring, GIS Mapping of electrical components to their physical location in ERSI based map systems.

Key responsibilities include:

  • ​​Provide technical and user support to system operators, division office workers, division engineering, and information technology personnel

  • ​Provide training and documentation when needed/requested

  • ​Aggregate and model data to create reports and dashboards in various BI tools. (PowerBI, SSRS, OBIEE)

  • ​ Analyze, recommend, design, plan, test, implement and support ADMS

  • ​Perform system/data analysis, design software enhancements, test system functionality, and maintain application configuration settings of the ADMS applications

​

​May assist senior staff with the following:

  • ​Engage with ADMS vendor, user groups, and utility peers to ensure Ameren's requirements are incorporated into future software releases

  • ​Provide input into technological process/standards/ methodology for cloud-based configuration; identifies gaps and develops training plans

  • ​Enable smart grid with applications including SCADA, Power Flow, Fault Locating, Switching, Outage Management

  • ​Perform root cause analysis and develop action plans and priorities for backlogs (identifying short- and long-term solutions); may lead implementation of plans

​​

Qualifications - Grid Solutions Engineer

  • ​​​​Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in engineering, mathematics or computer science

  • ​Three or more years of directly related experience required

  • ​Experience with Electric Power Distribution systems and applications such as Advanced Distribution Management System, Outage Analysis System, GIS Geographic Information Systems, and Electric Circuit Analysis systems preferred

  • ​Experience with software applications development, relational databases, and server / network infrastructure preferred​​​

Qualifications - Senior Grid Solutions Engineer

  • Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in engineering, mathematics or computer science

  • ​Five or more years of relevant experience required

​Experience with two or more of the following systems or technologies is required:

  • ​Electric Power Distribution,

  • ​Advanced Distribution Management System,

  • ​Outage Analysis System,

  • ​GIS Geographic Information System,

  • ​Electric Circuit Analysis,

  • ​Software applications development,

  • ​Relational databases, and

  • ​Server / network infrastructure

Qualifications - Lead Grid Solutions Engineer

  • ​​​​Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in engineering, mathematics or computer science

  • ​Seven or more years of relevant experience required

​Experience with three or more of the following systems or technologies is required:

  • ​Electric Power Distribution,

  • ​Advanced Distribution Management System,

  • ​Outage Analysis System,

  • ​GIS Geographic Information System,

  • ​Electric Circuit Analysis,

  • ​Software applications development,

  • ​Relational databases, and

  • ​Server / network infrastructure

Additional Information

Ameren’s selection process includes a series of interviews and may include a leadership assessment process. Specific details will be provided to qualified candidates.

#LI-Hybrid

Compensation Range:

$84,400.00 - $204,400.00

* This pay range encompasses multiple levels of the role. Career level and compensation depends upon applicant’s credentials.

At Ameren, base salary is one component of a competitive compensation package for employees. Our pay ranges are broad to allow for movement within our organization and to accommodate different skill sets and levels of expertise. We take into consideration a variety of factors including, but not limited to, skills, abilities, experience, education, credentials, and internal equity when determining the base salary offered. Roles are eligible for additional rewards including annual incentive payments based on individual and company performance.

If end date is listed, the posting will come down at 12:00 am on that date:

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, military service or status, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other class, trait, or status protected by law.

title: Market Research Manager
description:
Market Research Manager

Pipeline Ownership | Strategic Prospecting | Deal Qualification
Salary: Starting at $75,000 + Incentive Plan (based on experience)

Role Overview

This is not a high-volume calling role. This is a pipeline ownership role for someone who can identify, qualify, and advance real revenue opportunities.

The Market Research Manager at TCI is responsible for building qualified, revenue-ready pipeline across Brokerage, Dedicated, Leasing, Rental, and Maintenance. You will drive top-of-funnel execution through targeted prospecting, structured discovery, and market intelligence.

Location: North Richland Hills, TX (On-site)
Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM

What “Market Research” Means Here

  • Identifying high-probability accounts within our ICP

  • Engaging those accounts through structured outbound

  • Running qualifying discovery that confirms real buying intent

  • Advancing opportunities to proposal-ready

  • Research is not the output. | Qualified pipeline is.

What You’ll Own

  • Own outbound execution against a defined ICP

  • Build and qualify pipeline that meets strict deal criteria

  • Run discovery that separates interest from intent

  • Advance opportunities with clear next steps and deal logic

Key Responsibilities

  • Execute outbound prospecting aligned with TCI’s Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)

  • Identify and prioritize target accounts across all business units

  • Schedule and confirm meetings with decision-makers and key influencers

  • Conduct structured discovery to validate business need, current providers, and decision processes

  • Maintain detailed and accurate CRM documentation for all activities and opportunities

  • Build and manage account intelligence including fleet size, growth signals, and competitive landscape

  • Progress opportunities from initial outreach through proposal readiness

  • Partner with Business Development Managers and Directors to support opportunity advancement

  • Participate in pipeline reviews and provide clear updates on opportunity status and next steps

  • Ensure all opportunities meet qualification standards prior to proposal stage

  • Utilize tools such as LinkedIn Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, and CRM platforms to support prospecting efforts

  • Represent TCI’s values of professionalism, accountability, and preparation in all interactions

Who Thrives in This Role

  • You don’t care about activity metrics if they don’t produce pipeline

  • You think in deals, not tasks

  • You’d rather disqualify early than waste time late

  • You want ownership before the close, not after

Qualifications

  • 2+ years of experience in B2B prospecting, business development, or sales development

  • Experience conducting discovery conversations and qualifying opportunities

  • Ability to work on-site in North Richland Hills, TX, Monday–Friday (8:00 AM – 5:00 PM)

  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

  • High attention to detail and commitment to process

  • Bachelor’s degree preferred OR equivalent relevant experience

Preferred Skills

  • Experience in logistics, transportation, or asset-based services

  • Proficiency with CRM systems (Salesforce preferred)

  • Experience using Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, or similar prospecting tools

  • Strong analytical mindset with ability to interpret market or operational data

  • Proven ability to manage multiple accounts and priorities simultaneously

  • Demonstrated ability to work within a structured sales process

About TCI

We are a family-owned company proudly serving our customers since 1978.

At TCI, we are:

  • Dedicated to safety, each other, and our customers

  • Positive and passionate, showing up each day with a “Can Do” attitude

  • Innovative problem solvers, always looking for better ways to serve

  • Ethical, transparent, and accountable in how we operate

We’re looking for individuals who align with our core values and want to grow with a team that truly lives them.

title: Supervisor Service Delivery-Logistics
description:
C.H. Robinson is seeking a Supervisor Service Delivery to ensure the execution of the Service Delivery strategy for an assigned customer or customer portfolio, in line with customer SLAs and expectations. In this key position, you will be responsible for Service Delivery Excellence and Customer Satisfaction, Personnel Development and Management, and driving Operational Excellence through a Continuous Improvement approach.

At C.H. Robinson, we believe that in-person collaboration is key to driving innovation and achieving success. In this role, you will work on-site with your peers five days a week, fostering creativity and producing impactful results through face-to-face interactions. Experience the full benefits of teamwork and immediate collaboration in a vibrant and engaging workplace.

Responsibilities:

The duties and responsibilities of this position consists of, but are not limited to, the following:

Business Strategy:

  • Drive consistent execution that meets Service Delivery requirements and expectations
  • Leverage Operations Dashboards and internal analytics to understand areas of service excellence & service gaps, and work with teams on actions to address identified gaps
  • Learning and utilization of continuous improvement methodology in management of KPIs for the service delivery team
  • Identify recurring service delivery interruptions & customer issues, drive root cause analysis and resolve via appropriate corrective & preventive actions
  • Key customer contact for Service Delivery (Operational) needs
  • Collaborate with multiple areas of the business to drive standardization of leading practices

Team Leadership:

  • Lead direct reports by providing regular feedback, coaching and guidance based on performance, dashboards, and Personal Development Plan
  • Ensure assigned team-members have a solid understanding of customer business objectives and the plans to attain those objectives
  • Ensure service delivery team has resources required to execute day-to-day operations
  • Coordinate schedules to ensure proper account coverage. Examples of but not limited to PTO, meals, breaks
  • Maintain and utilize Account Specific Onboarding for new team members
  • Other duties or responsibilities as assigned according to the team and/or country specific requirements

Required Qualifications:

Required:

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in operations and/or customer/client service facing role
  • Experience mentoring and or coaching small project teams
  • Ability to travel up to 7.5% (domestically and international)

Preferred Qualifications:

Preferred:

  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university
  • Previous supervisory experience
  • Experience executing businesses through Standard Practices and Operational Excellence methodologies
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite of programs
  • Strong problem-solving and conflict resolution skills
  • Ability to adapt and lead in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong interpersonal/communication and presentation skills, with ability to communicate effectively in a highly responsive and professional manner
  • Ability to develop strong working relationships, both internally and externally
  • High level of integrity where personal and professional values are aligned with the company's mission, vision, and leadership framework
  • Ability to work collaboratively within the business setting
  • Values a diverse and inclusive work environment

Equal Opportunity

C.H. Robinson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to a workplace and performance culture that reflects the strengths of our worldwide marketplace. title: Senior Technical Program Manager
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Technical Program Manager

Technical Program Manager Role Overview
Who is Mastercard?
We work to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships, and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. We cultivate a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects their individual strengths, views, and experiences. We believe our differences enable us to be a better team — one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results.
Technology at Mastercard
What we create today will define tomorrow: revolutionary technologies that reshape the digital economy to be more connected and inclusive than ever before — safer, faster, and more sustainable.
We need technologists who are energized by the challenges of a truly global network, with the talent and vision to create the critical systems and products that power global commerce and connect people everywhere to the vital goods and services they need every day.
Working at Mastercard means being part of a unique culture that is inclusive, diverse, and built on collaboration. It is a place that celebrates your strengths, values your experiences, and offers the flexibility to shape a career across disciplines and continents — while working alongside experts and leaders at every level of the business to improve what exists and invent what’s next.
All About You
Are you a technical program manager who wants to work for a company that offers above-and-beyond benefits, including paid parental leave, flexible work hours, gift matching, and volunteer incentives?
Do you want to work in an inclusive and nurturing culture that encourages your professional learning and development?
The ideal candidate is passionate about the customer experience journey, highly motivated, intellectually curious, analytical, and possesses an entrepreneurial mindset.
Knowledge / Experience
• 8+ years leading large-scale, end-to-end technical programs across multiple teams with complex dependencies in enterprise environments.
• Strong expertise in portfolio- and initiative-level program management, including governance, execution tracking, reporting, and operational readiness.
• Hands-on experience with Agile Scrum, SAFe, and blended delivery models, with strengths in work decomposition, sprint planning, backlog management, and delivery acceleration.
• Solid understanding of software engineering, system design, and architecture, with the ability to engage engineers on trade-offs, scalability, and constraints.
• Proven ability to drive business and customer outcomes through technical execution, measured through metrics and results.
• Demonstrated strength in risk management, ambiguity handling, stakeholder influence, and executive communication.
• Experience building and leading TPM organizations, mentoring leaders, shaping practices, and influencing technology roadmaps.
About the Role
• Drive transformational change in end-to-end product delivery by applying technical program management and engineering best practices across agile and hybrid environments.
• Lead large enterprise technical programs and products from inception to delivery, operating autonomously in ambiguous problem spaces.
• Maintain a deep understanding of business domains, systems, and architectural decisions, translating complexity into clarity and execution.
• Provide thought leadership, drive problem decomposition, accelerate delivery, and ensure predictable outcomes under tight deadlines.
• Establish trusted partnerships with engineering, business, and executive stakeholders, influencing decisions and aligning execution.
• Build, lead, and mature a high-performing TPM function, fostering strong delivery culture, empathy, and accountability.
Major Accountabilities
• Own program and portfolio execution, optimizing processes, aligning teams and technologies, and ensuring efficient use of resources.
• Drive delivery from planning through production, proactively managing risks, dependencies, escalations, and blockers.
• Establish clear governance, metrics, and reporting to enable transparency, timely decisions, and continuous improvement.
• Foster cross-team collaboration, accountability for results, and organizational learning.
• Act as the primary program representative, providing clear communication, accurate status, and stakeholder alignment.
• Stay current on technology trends, evaluate designs, and influence long-term organizational and technical direction.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Outpatient Registered Nurse - RN
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

The professional registered nurse Outpatient RN CAP 1 is an entry level designation into the Clinical Advancement Program (CAP). This position is accountable and responsible for the provision and coordination of clinically competent care including assessment, planning, intervention and evaluation for an assigned group of patients. This may include delegation of appropriate tasks to direct patient care staff including but not limited to RNs, LVN/LPNs and Patient Care Technicians. As a member of the End Stage Kidney Disease (ESKD) health care team, this position participates in decision-making, teaching, leadership functions, and quality improvement activities that enhance patient care outcomes and facility operations.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

All duties and responsibilities are expected to be performed in accordance with Fresenius Kidney Care policy, procedures, standards of nursing practice, state and federal regulations.

· Performs all essential functions under the direction of the Supervisor and with guidance from the Clinical Educator, Preceptor or in collaboration with other Registered Nurses.

· Performs ongoing, systematic collection and analysis of patient data pre – during – post hemodialysis treatment for assigned patients and documents in the patient medical record, makes adjustments or modifications to treatment plan as indicated and notifies Team Leader, Charge Nurse, Supervisor or Physician as needed.

· Assesses, collaborates, and documents patient/family’s basic learning needs to provide initial and ongoing education to patients and family.

· Directs and provides, in collaboration with direct and ancillary patient care staff, all aspects of the daily provision of safe and effective delivery of hemodialysis therapy to assigned patients.

· Administers medications as prescribed or in accordance with approved algorithm(s), and documents appropriate medical justification and effectiveness.

· Initiates or assists with emergency response measures.

· Serves as a resource for health care team, participates in staff training and orientation of new staff as assigned.

· Ensures correct laboratory collection, processing and shipping procedures are performed and reschedules missed or insufficient laboratory collections.

· Identifies expected outcomes, documents and updates the nursing assessment and plan of care for assigned patients through collaboration with the Interdisciplinary Team.

· Ensures patient awareness related to transplant and treatment modality options.

· Required to complete CAP requirements to advance.

· Performs all other duties as assigned by Supervisor.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

· The position provides direct patient care that regularly involves heavy lifting, moving of patients and assisting with ambulation. Equipment aids and/or coworkers may provide assistance.

· This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over.

· The employee may occasionally be required to move, with assistance, machines and equipment of up to 200 lbs., and may lift chemical and water solutions of up to 30 lbs. as high as 5 feet.

· The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels.

· May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials.

· Day to day work includes desk work, computer work, interaction with patients, facility/hospital staff and physicians.

· The position may require travel to training sites or other facilities.

· May be asked to provide essential functions of this position in other locations with the same physical demands and working conditions as described above.

SUPERVISION:

Assigned oversight of Patient Care Technicians/LPNs/LVNs/RNs as a Team Leader or designated Nurse in Charge, after meeting all the following:

· Successful completion of all FKC education and training requirements for new employees.

· Must have a minimum of 9 months experience as a RN.

· Must have a minimum of 3 months experience in chronic/acute hemodialysis as a RN.

EDUCATION and LICENSURE:

· Graduate of an accredited School of Nursing.

· Current appropriate state licensure.

· Current or successful completion of CPR BLS Certification.

· Must meet the practice requirements in the state in which he or she is employed.

EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

· Entry level for RNs with less than 2 years of Nephrology Nursing experience as a Registered Nurse.

· Chronic/acute hemodialysis experience (preferred).

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors
