title: Stödpedagog sökes till Unika i Kvicksund
description:
Om Unika

Verksamheten ligger belägen i Kvicksund och vänder sig till LSS personkrets 1.

Om rollen som Stödpedagog

I rollen som stödpedagog kommer du att ge stöd och service utifrån de boendes önskemål, både i hemmet och i fritidsaktiviteter. Du ska bidra till att den boende ges möjlighet till en aktiv och meningsfull tillvaro. Vidare ska du kunna se till varje persons individuella behov och kunna bedriva pedagogiskt arbete utifrån detta. Då behovet är olika bland brukarna är ingen dag sig lik vilket bidrar till ett utmanande och spännande jobb.

Du kommer att arbeta brukarnära tillsammans med dina kollegor och har ett stort ansvar att, i samråd med verksamhetschef, driva och utveckla det pedagogiska arbetet på verksamheten. Vissa delegerade hälso- och sjukvårdsuppgifter ingår i arbetet.

Om rollen som Stödassistent

Som stödassistent kommer du att arbeta med att ge individuellt anpassat stöd till personer med funktionsvariationer, med målet att främja deras självständighet och välbefinnande i vardagen. Du kommer att stödja brukarna i deras dagliga rutiner och aktiviteter, utifrån deras behov och förmågor, och alltid ha deras personliga genomförandeplan som utgångspunkt. Som stödassistent arbetar du utifrån tydliggörande pedagogik och lågaffektivt bemötande för att skapa trygghet och förutsägbarhet i brukarnas vardag. Samtidigt har vi ett starkt fokus på att arbeta professionellt och effektivt inom verksamhetens resurser och budget.

Kvalifikationer

Du är relationsskapande, lugn, stabil och kan hålla ett realistiskt perspektiv på situationer som uppkommer i det dagliga arbetet. Du har en god samarbetsförmåga och relaterar till andra på ett lyhört sätt samt trivs med att samverka med andra. Du är en självständig, ödmjuk och ansvarsfull person som brinner för omsorgsarbete och kan se individens behov.

Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.

Krav stödpedagog:

  • Examen från stödpedagogutbildningen 200 YH poäng eller 60 HP inom pedagogik.

  • Erfarenhet samt kunskap om målgruppen LSS vuxna personkrets 1.

  • Grundläggande kunskaper om hushållsarbete såsom kost och hygien.

  • Datorvana samt goda kunskaper i svenska språket, i tal och skrift.

Krav stödassistent:

  • Mycket god förmåga i svenska i tal såväl som skrift.

  • God datorvana, erfarenhet av dokumentation.

  • Erfarenhet av arbete inom vård och omsorg

  • Gymnasial utbildning som tex barn-och fritidsprogram, vård-och omsorgsprogram, eller eftergymnasial utbildning tex stödpedagog, socialpedagog eller högskoleutbildning inom relevant område.

Meriterande om du därtill har:

  • Erfarenhet av arbetsledning

  • Erfarenhet av att ha jobbat med människor med autism och utmanande beteende.

  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta med individer med komplex psykosocial beteendeproblematik.

  • Erfarenhet av tydliggörande kommunikation och lågaffektivt bemötande.

Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.

Anställningsvillkor

Anställningsform:
Tjänstgöringsgrad: 98%
Arbetstid: Dag, kväll och helg
Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse, vi ser positivt på om du kan tillträda inom kort.
Kollektivavtal: Vi är kollektivavtalsbundna till Vårdföretagarna/Kommunal, Bransch E.

Vad kan vi erbjuda dig?

Attendo är en trygg arbetsgivare som kan erbjuda dig som vill goda möjligheter att ta mer ansvar eller att gå vidare till nya roller. Vi erbjuder dig som medarbetare utbildningsmöjligheter, kollektivavtal, friskvård samt flera andra förmåner och personalrabatter via vår förmånsportal AttendoPlus, i samarbete med Benifex.

Om rekryteringsprocessen

Före eventuell anställning ska utdrag ur belastningsregistret tillsammans med giltig fotolegitimation visas upp. Om du blir kallad till en intervju behöver du kunna styrka att du har rätt att arbeta i Sverige, genom att uppvisa att du har medborgarskap inom EU/EES eller ett giltigt arbetstillstånd. Sista ansökningsdag 2026-02-28 men urval och intervjuer sker löpande, så vänta inte med din ansökan.

Om Attendo/Unika

Unika är en del av Attendo, som är ett av Nordens största omsorgsföretag och startades 1985. Vi är verksamma inom LSS. Våra värderingar omtanke, engagemang och kompetens vägleder oss dagligen i vårt arbete för att alla ska uppleva trivsel, delaktighet, gemenskap och arbetsglädje.

Välkommen med din ansökan!
Tillsammans för en meningsfull vardag!

Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta: (Herash.azizi@unika.se)

title: Sales Development Representative (German speaker)
description:
Ready to convert cold leads to hot opportunities?

At tamigo, we help retailers and hospitality brands across Europe simplify and streamline workforce management. With already a solid customer base in the DACH market, we’re now growing fast - and we’re looking for a Sales Development Representative to help us accelerate.

You’ll team up with our Account Executive to turn curiosity into conversations and conversations into customers. You’ll dive into outreach, spark great first impressions, and spot real opportunities. Your drive, structure, and clear communication will help you handle objections, run sharp discovery calls, and close smaller deals along the way.

If you love asking smart questions, uncovering needs, and making a real impact, this role puts you right at the heart of our growth - and the success of our customers in the DACH market.

Core responsibilities

· Handle small deals (inbound small and mid-size business leads).

· Book qualified meetings with enterprise prospects for the Account Executive. You’re responsible for research, outreach and the first discovery call.

· Keep our CRM system (HubSpot) up to date.

Once the above is under control

· Outbound outreach to midsize leads.

· Support the Account Executive during larger deals.

· Learn to close midsize deals.

You bring: Native level German, excellent English and sales drive. We’ll teach the rest.

Start in inbound, learn to prospect, present and close smaller deals – grow fast with us. Experience is a plus, not a prerequisite.

As a person you are

  • Structured

  • Proactive

  • Eager to learn and grow

What tamigo offers you

  • An exciting role where you take ownership for your sales pipeline and make a difference for customers.

  • A growing SaaS company, where initiative and an entrepreneurial spirit are welcomed.

  • Close collaboration with colleagues across Europe.

  • A central and well-connected office near Østerport station.

  • Flexibility to work from home up to two days a week.

Please submit your application and CV in English – it’s our company language.

Try the product you'll be supporting

Take a look at the solution you'll become an expert in: https://tamigo.com/free-trial Sign up using 'Candidate' as your company name — and tell us what we could do better.

Questions about the role? Reach out to our recruitment team directly at julio.garcia@tamigo.com.

Please note that you must have a clean criminal record and a valid work permit for Denmark (or the right to work in Denmark as an EU/EEA or Swiss citizen) to be considered for this position.

title: Dental Assistant Instructor (63880)
location: California West Covina UEI - West Covina, CA 91791
description:
We're Looking For: Someone with tenacity, passion, discipline and grit to join our team as an Dental Assistant Instructor at our campus.

To Do What: In this position, you will be responsible for the delivery of quality educational instruction by helping develop the technical and soft skills needed for our students to secure a job in their new career.

Who Are We: United Education Institute is a leader in post-secondary career education and we are obsessed with student success. Our company is fast-growing and has opportunities for growth and advancement.

What We Offer: We are a Certified Employee-Owned Company and offer a variety of benefits for full-time and part-time colleagues including a generously matched 401(k) plan!

Some of the great work you'll do includes:

  • Helping students determine their educational goals

**Teaching experience is not required if you are an expert in the field. We will train***

  • California Applicants: An applicant must be an RDA licensee for 3 years and maintain valid, current RDA license throughout employment in California. A Minimum of 3 years of experience in the dental field and RDA License is required for California schools. Must have X-Ray, Coronal Polishing, and Pit and Fissure certifications.

  • Florida Applicants: EFDA is required for Florida based schools, unless the individual graduated from a Commission on Dental Accreditation (CODA) accredited school. A Minimum of 5 years of experience is required for Florida schools.

  • Georgia Applicants: CDA/EDDA/EFDA certificate or 3 years of experience is required for Georgia schools.

  • Arizona Applicants: Must possess an AZ Board-approved certificate in Radiology.

  • Thirty-six months combined related industry and/or teaching experience.

title: HID Interaction Algorithm Engineer
description:
Join the engineering team that fuses multimodal input to enable seamless interactions on human-computer interfaces like  Vision Pro. Human Interface Devices (HID) is looking for an Interaction Algorithm Engineer to help build the next generation of computing.

HID operates at the intersection of hardware, software, and design. The problems you solve will be multidisciplinary and require strong collaboration and communication skills. You’ll prototype ideas at the early stages of development and develop robust solutions through production.

In this role, you will:

  • Prototype and define interactions end-to-end, in collaboration with HW, SW, and design partners
  • Design user studies and leverage prototypes to define success criteria
  • Develop processes for measuring key performance indicators
  • Analyze data to identify and drive algorithm improvements
  • Create and maintain core algorithms for new interactions and root-cause issues throughout the stack
  • Communicate user impacts of technical challenges to cross-functional stakeholders

Minimum Qualifications

  • BS in Computer Science (CS), Electrical Engineering (EE), Human Computer Interaction (HCI), or a related field / equivalent experience

Preferred Qualifications

  • 2+ years SW development experience (or equivalent internship / research experience)
  • Proficiency in C++ and Python
  • Proficiency in signal processing and/or machine learning
  • Comfortable with 3D mathematics
  • Experience developing interactions or input technologies
  • Experience using genAI tools for SW development (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.)
  • Able to communicate clearly and collaborate with cross-functional teams
  • Excellent problem solving and root cause analysis skills
  • Experience developing software with agentic workflows
  • Experience developing software for Augmented Reality (AR) / Virtual Reality (VR) (ex. ARKit)
  • Experience with Objective-C and/or Swift
  • MS/PhD in Computer Science (CS), Electrical Engineering (EE), Human Computer Interaction (HCI), or a related field / equivalent experience

title: Enterprise Risk Management , Assistant Vice President
description:
Who we are looking for

We are looking for an Enterprise Risk Management Audit, Assistant Vice President to lead audit engagements focused in the Financial Risk areas. Enterprise Risk Management provides independent risk oversight over State Street’s business activities. The Financial Risks include Market, Credit (including Trading Credit ), Interest Rate Risk and Liquidity. In this role, under the direction of senior audit management, you will oversee individual audit engagement and lead assigned teams through all aspects of the audit (i.e., planning, assessments, testing and reporting). You will also play a critical role in the ongoing development of audit staff and support various departmental initiatives.

Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining plays an important role in the overall success of the organization. Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability; regulators rely upon us to provide assurance that the bank operates in a manner that is compliant with key banking regulations to properly manage risks to the company and the global financial systems. To make that happen we need teams like yours to help navigate employees and the organization as a whole. In your role, you will help us build resilience and execute day to day deliverables at our best. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for.

What you will be responsible for

As an Enterprise Risk Management Audit, Assistant Vice President you will:

  • Plan, oversee and execute audits according to the Division’s risk-based audit methodology, other internal standards and industry practices.
  • Supervise and direct the execution of risk and control assessments, along with the effective and efficient testing of key controls.
  • Review audit workpapers to ensure execution in line with internal standards and that conclusions are properly supported.
  • Draft audit reports that clearly communicate overall conclusion, including: key risks, identified issues and their root cause, and impact on overall business strategy.
  • Work in partnership with business area management to communicate expectations and manage any project issues that may arise during the audit engagement.
  • Escalate potential and identified issues to senior management timely and work with business management to develop and negotiate solutions.
  • Develop and maintain a thorough understanding of assigned areas of responsibility, including key technology, products, organizational activities, regulatory environment and global footprint.
  • Establish and maintain strong and effective client relationships with business management across the lines of defense and assist management in understanding and implementing / maintaining internal control principles.
  • Contribute to change initiatives through active participation in working groups and adoption of practices in day-to-day responsibilities.
  • Coach and mentor audit staff as part of their ongoing development.

What we value

These skills will help you succeeding this role

  • A high degree of professionalism with proven history of providing effective leadership to diverse teams.
  • The ability to manage complexity, effectively prioritize multiple tasks and work independently in non-routine situations and in a fast-paced environment.
  • Knowledge and understanding of financial risks particularly Liquidity, Interest Rate, Market/Trading and Credit risk.
  • Excellent analytical, problem resolving, communication (written and verbal), interpersonal, organizational and presentation skills.
  • Demonstrated ability to synthesize understanding of business processes into value added feedback on risk and control environment.
  • Proven ability to research, interpret and apply regulatory requirements.
  • Fluency in English – Written and spoken.

Education & Preferred Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in Market/Credit/Interest Rate/Liquidity risks in the first or second line.
  • Public Accounting, Internal Auditing.
  • Bachelor’s degree, preferable in finance, accounting or related field.
  • Advanced degree or certification (CFA, FRM, CPA) preferred.
  • Experience executing or auditing Financial Risk.
  • Experience in auditing information technology general controls.

Salary Range:

$91,000 - $140,500 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

title: Sr. Business Analyst, Advisors & Consulting Services (12 months fixed term)
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Sr. Business Analyst, Advisors & Consulting Services (12 months fixed term)

Role Context

This is a hands-on fixed term role based in Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea to program manage a large card migration program with one of Mastercard's key clients.

The role sits within a large, complex transformation initiative and is focused on delivering client-facing transformation and change initiatives. You will work across strategy, process, data, and technology to translate business needs into clear requirements and actionable solutions that drive tangible business value.

You will support a portfolio conversion project, partnering closely with the client, product owners, delivery teams, and leadership to define problems, shape solutions, and ensure successful implementation across complex environments.

The role will work closely with cross functional program teams and delivery partners to ensure customer expectations are effectively managed, and communications are executed in a regulated environment.

Past experience in developing markets would be prioritised.

Role Purpose

The Senior Business Analyst will be an experienced self-starter who likes taking initiative and is comfortable working with a variety of stakeholders to determine business requirements.

All About You:
• Experience with customer segmentation and campaign analytics for banking / finance
• Collaborate with business and client stakeholders to understand objectives, challenges, and requirements.
• Lead requirements gathering and analysis for new and existing processes, systems, and data solutions.
• Define and document business requirements, functional specifications, and user stories.
• Analyse business processes, identify gaps and inefficiencies, and design future state solutions.
• Consume data analysis and identify trends, insights, and opportunities that inform decision-making.
• Develop process maps, current-state and future-state models, and supporting documentation.
• Work closely with technical and delivery teams to support solution design, configuration, and implementation.
• Facilitate workshops and working sessions to align stakeholders and prioritise requirements.
• Support testing, validation, and implementation to ensure solutions meet business needs.
• Prepare clear, executive-ready communications, including analysis, recommendations, and status updates.
• Provide post-implementation support to ensure smooth transition into business as usual.
• Strong experience as a Business Analyst or similar role within complex, change driven environments.
• Proven ability to analyse business processes, systems, and interpret data to drive meaningful outcomes.
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills.
• Able to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and adapt in fast-paced environments.
• Good communication skills to present findings effectively
• Payments and card industry experience is highly preferred.

Qualifications & Skills:
• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification. Advanced degree or MBA preferred
• Certification in Business Analysis (e.g., CBAP, PMI-PBA) or related certifications preferred.
• 7+ years of experience in business analysis or a similar role, with a proven track record of delivering successful projects.
• Strong proficiency in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint; experience creating executive-level materials.
• Experience with business analysis and delivery tools such as JIRA, Confluence, and Visio.
• Experience with data analytical tools such as SQL, Python, Tableau and/or Power BI.

Note:
• Must be open to travelling extensively and be on-site for a period of time.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Director, Software Engineering
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Director, Software Engineering

Director, Software Engineering
Mastercard's Business Experimentation & Optimization (BEO) program is seeking a Director, Software Engineering to lead the design, delivery, and evolution of our data platforms, analytics capabilities, and customer-facing solutions. This leader will be responsible for a global engineering organization that develops and operates the end-to-end data and analytics ecosystem powering customer insights and decision-making.
The role will initially focus on leading teams responsible for data ingestion, data engineering and data pipelines, custom analytics, and insight delivery, partnering closely with Product, Data Science, Analytics, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, high-quality solutions that create measurable customer and business value. Over time, this leader will expand their scope to assume ownership of key customer-facing products and experiences, helping shape the future vision and technology strategy for the BEO platform.
As part of the Services Technology organization, BEO is undergoing a significant technology transformation to modernize how we generate and deliver insights to our customers. This is a highly visible technical leadership opportunity to drive innovation through cloud-native platforms, modern data architectures, AI-enabled capabilities, and emerging technologies including agentic workflows. The successful candidate will play a critical role in shaping the next generation of experimentation, analytics, and insight delivery capabilities across the platform.
This hybrid role is based in Arlington, VA and requires three days per week onsite. The leader will manage a global organization of approximately 25 engineers and engineering leaders, including 4–5 direct reports across the United States, India, and Europe. Success in this role requires strong technical leadership, working with ambiguity in a rapidly changing ecosystem, stakeholder management, and the ability to drive execution across globally distributed teams.

Role:
• Lead engineering teams responsible for ingesting, transforming, and managing large-scale data assets that power analytics and customer-facing insights.
• Build and scale modern data platforms, APIs, and applications that support experimentation, measurement, reporting, and advanced analytics use cases.
• Partner closely with Product, Data Science, and Analytics teams to translate business needs into scalable technical solutions.
• Lead modernization efforts leveraging cloud technologies, AI-enabled workflows, and automation to improve speed, quality, and customer outcomes.
• Champion improvements in developer experience, engineering tooling, and best practices, including the adoption of AI to improve productivity and engineering effectiveness.
• Lead, coach, and develop engineers and engineering managers across global, cross-functional teams.
• Identify, assess, and mitigate technical risks in collaboration with senior technology leaders.
• Foster a culture of innovation, experimentation, and continuous technical learning.
• Set high standards for engineering excellence and elevate team performance.
• Navigate complex cross-team dependencies and drive alignment across stakeholders.
• Promote agile ways of working and enable teams to adapt quickly to changing priorities and insights.
• Engage and influence senior stakeholders on technology direction and product decisions.
• Anticipate and plan for future scaling needs aligned with business growth and customer demand.

All about you:
• Proven experience as an Engineering leader in an agile, production-scale environment.
• Experience leading multiple engineering teams and developing engineering leaders in a global, distributed setup.
• Strong track record of defining architecture, design, and delivery of large-scale, multi-team systems.
• Hands-on familiarity with full-stack, data, and cloud technologies such as Databricks, AWS (Data Services + Agentic/ML Services including EMR, Agent Core, Bedrock etc), .NET/C#, Java, Spring Boot, React, Python, Spark, or similar.
• Strong technical foundation with ability to quickly learn and adopt new technologies.
• Relentless focus on customer value, business outcomes, and delivery excellence.
• Experience improving engineering organizations, structures, and delivery processes at scale.
• Ability to influence across teams, functions, and geographies to drive technical alignment.
• Proven success leading distributed global teams across multiple regions.
• Strong understanding of SDLC methodologies (Scrum, Kanban, SAFe) and when to apply them.
• Proven ability to build, mentor, and retain high-performing engineering leaders
• Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence executive, business, and technical stakeholders.
• Strong analytical thinking and decision-making capabilities in ambiguous and complex environments.
• Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field, or equivalent practical experience.

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

Arlington, Virginia: $195,000 - $323,000 USD

title: HR Manager - Manufacturing
description:
Our client, a leading global company in the advanced medical manufacturing industry, is seeking an experienced HR Manager to lead the site's human resources strategy and operations.

This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in establishing a new manufacturing facility while partnering with regional and global leaders to shape culture, attract top talent, and support business growth across Latin America.

What You'll Do

  • Lead the HR strategy and day-to-day operations for a new manufacturing site in Cartago.
  • Build and scale HR processes, policies, and systems aligned with business objectives.
  • Lead high-volume recruitment initiatives to support rapid workforce growth.
  • Partner with leaders on workforce planning, organizational design, performance management, and employee development.
  • Drive employee engagement, culture, and retention initiatives.
  • Provide expert guidance on employee relations, labor compliance, and HR best practices.
  • Collaborate with regional and global HR teams on strategic talent initiatives across the Americas.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
  • 5–8+ years of progressive HR experience, ideally within manufacturing, medical devices, or other industrial environments.
  • Experience supporting talent acquisition, employee relations, HR operations, and organizational development.
  • Strong knowledge of Costa Rican labor legislation.
  • Experience implementing HR systems, payroll, or HR infrastructure is highly valued.
  • Proven ability to influence leaders, manage change, and work effectively in a dynamic, evolving environment.
  • English proficiency required.

title: Profesor/a de lengua y literatura para Selectividad
description:
¿Te apasiona la lengua castellana y quieres ayudar a estudiantes a superar la EBAU/EVAU?

En Tusclasesparticulares, la plataforma educativa líder en España, buscamos profesores de lengua y literatura especializados en preparación para la Selectividad.

Lo que harás:

  • Impartir clases particulares de lengua castellana y literatura orientadas a la EBAU/EVAU (Selectividad).

  • Preparar al estudiante en los bloques clave del examen: comentario de texto, comprensión lectora, gramática, ortografía y historia de la literatura española.

  • Trabajar la expresión escrita y el análisis literario, dos de los puntos más determinantes en la nota final.

Lo que necesitamos:

  • Conocimiento del Idioma o estudios relacionados.

  • No es necesario, pero se valora experiencia previa dando clases particulares o preparando alumnos para la Selectividad.

  • Capacidad para explicar con claridad y motivar en momentos de alta presión académica.

Lo que te ofrecemos:

  • Flexibilidad total: tú decides tus horarios y el número de alumnos.

  • Ingresos de 15 € a 35 €/hora según perfil y experiencia.

  • Modalidad presencial, online o combinada.

title: 전략기획
description:
About

[21년 연속 영업이익 달성, MarTech를 이끄는 마케팅 플랫폼]
휴머스온은 1998년 설립 이후 디지털 마케팅 분야를 선도해온 기업입니다. 자체 개발한 빅데이터 마케팅 플랫폼 ‘TasOn’과 통합 메시징 솔루션 ‘TMS’를 통해 국내 시장 점유율 1위를 기록하며, 고객 맞춤형 마케팅을 지원하고 있습니다.
삼성전자, 현대자동차, 대한항공, 스타벅스 등 국내외 대표 기업들이 휴머스온과 함께하고 있으며, 국내 대기업 고객 점유율은 80% 이상에 달합니다. AI 기반 메시지 발송은 연간 300억 건 이상을 처리하고 있으며, 고객사 재계약률은 95% 이상으로 업계 최고 수준의 신뢰도를 보여주고 있습니다.
휴머스온의 강점은 기술력에 있습니다. 빅데이터 엔진과 AI 기반 마케팅 자동화 기술을 결합해 고객 행동을 정밀하게 분석하고, 개인화된 마케팅을 자동으로 실행할 수 있는 플랫폼을 구축했습니다. 이러한 기술 혁신은 고객사의 마케팅 효율을 극대화하고, 실제 영업 성과로 이어지고 있습니다.
지속적인 R&D 투자와 안정적인 매출 성장은 휴머스온을 국내를 넘어 글로벌 시장에서도 경쟁력 있는 기업으로 자리매김하게 했습니다. 앞으로도 휴머스온은 “글로벌 빅데이터 마케팅 테크놀로지 컴퍼니”로 도약하며, 데이터와 기술을 기반으로 새로운 가치를 창출해 나갈 것입니다.

Responsibilities

• 연간/분기 사업계획 수립 지원 및 실적 대비 진척 관리, 경영진 보고
• 부서별 예산 및 과제 집행 현황 모니터링, 이슈 파악 및 대응 방안 제시
• 기존 사업 성과 분석 및 데이터 기반 인사이트 도출, 개선 방향 제시
• 신규 사업 기회 발굴 및 타당성 검토(시장 조사, 사업성 분석, 리스크 진단)
• 기존·신규 사업 포트폴리오 전반에 대한 전략 방향 수립 지원
• 대표이사 직속 특별 과제(TF) 기획 및 수행

Requirements

• 4년제 대학교 졸업 이상
• 경영전략/기획 분야 실무 경력 5년 이상
• 사업계획 수립 및 관리 경험
• 신규 사업 검토(시장조사, 사업성 분석) 경험
• 데이터 기반 사업분석 및 인사이트 도출 역량
• C-Level 대상 보고서 작성 및 커뮤니케이션 역량

Preferred

• 경영·경제·산업공학 등 관련 전공자
• 컨설팅펌 또는 전략기획실 출신 (사업 구조화, 논리적 문제해결 훈련 경험)
• 재무모델링 능력 (NPV/IRR 등 사업타당성 분석, 손익·현금흐름 추정)
• 데이터 분석 툴 활용 역량 (SQL, Excel 고급, Tableau/Power BI 등)
• IT·플랫폼 또는 CRM/마케팅솔루션 등 유사 업종 이해
• 다부서 협업 조율 경험

Benefits

• 선물 : 명절선물/귀향비, 생일선물/파티
• 리프레시 : 반차, 휴양시설 제공, 경조휴가제
• 근무 환경 : 카페테리아
• 교육/생활 : 교육비 지원, 도서구입비지원

title: Application, Infrastructure & Service Management - Senior Associate
description:
Who we are looking for

As a Production Support Engineer you will help build a meaningful engineering discipline, combining software and systems to develop creative engineering solutions to operations problems. Much of support and development will focus on existing systems, building infrastructure and reducing work through automation. You’ll join a team of curious problem solvers with diverse set of perspectives who are thinking big and taking risks. In this environment you’ll work on the relevant projects, supported by an organization that provides the support and mentorship you need to learn and grow. As engineer, you’ll be focused on running better production applications and systems.

What you will be responsible for

As a Production Support Engineer you will be responsible for:

  • Onboarding applications on to Production management with the highest onboarding standards
  • Troubleshoot priority incidents, facilitate blameless post-mortems and ensure permanent closure of incidents
  • Perform analytics on previous incidents and usage patterns to better predict issues and take proactive actions
  • Build and drive adoption for greater self-healing and resiliency patterns.
  • Participate in performance tests; identify bottlenecks, opportunities for optimization and capacity demands.
  • Engage with development team throughout the life cycle to help develop software for reliability and scale, ensuring minimal refactoring or changes
  • Design automated software and product upgrades, change management, and release management solutions
  • Participate in the 24x7 support coverage as needed

Education & Preferred Qualifications

  • B.S/M.S.Degree
  • 2 years as L3 Production Support analyst
  • SQL Knowledge, Shell scripting, Python, Ansible
  • ITIL

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. title: Core Ledger Specialist
description:
We're looking for a skilled Core Ledger Specialist to join our platform team. Our team does not develop the core ledger systems ourselves — they are built and delivered by external suppliers. Your role is to understand the business and financial logic deeply, define and write clear requirements, verify and regression test what our suppliers deliver, and support internal configuration of the platform. A significant part of this role is providing hands-on internal support to colleagues across the business who rely on the core ledger day to day — this is something all specialists in the team spend a lot of time on. This role calls for strong analytical and technical skills combined with a good understanding of how financial ledgers work.

You will join a small, close-knit team: today the Core Ledger team consists of a Head of Core Ledger IT, a Product Manager, and two Core Ledger Specialists (one of whom also carries delivery management responsibilities). This is a great opportunity to make a direct impact early on and help shape the team as it grows.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and document clear, detailed requirements for transaction processing, balance management, and posting logic, to be delivered by external suppliers

  • Verify and regression test supplier deliveries to ensure data integrity, accuracy, and consistency across all ledger operations

  • Collaborate with the Head of Core Ledger IT and suppliers on solution design and technical decisions

  • Investigate and resolve complex ledger-related incidents and data discrepancies, working with suppliers when needed

  • Maintain clear documentation, including requirements, data flows, test results, and runbooks

  • Support internal configuration of the core ledger platform

  • Provide hands-on internal support to other teams (finance, customer service, operations, and others) on day-to-day ledger questions and issues — this is a significant and ongoing part of the role

  • Support integration of new products and financial instruments into the ledger framework, working with suppliers and Product Management

  • Work closely with QA and the Delivery Manager to ensure supplier deliveries are well tested before approval

Qualifications and Experience

Desired Skills

  • 4+ years of experience working with financial or transactional systems, in a requirements, analysis, or configuration role

  • Strong understanding of bookkeeping, financial transaction processing, or core banking systems

  • Experience writing clear, detailed requirements and acceptance chriteria for systems delivered by external suppliers

  • Familiarity with relational databases and ability to query and analyse data (SQL)

  • Solid understanding of how to verify and test complex system deliveries

Nice to Have

  • Experience working in a regulated financial environment (banking, payments, BNPL)

  • Knowledge of accounting standards and financial reporting requirements

  • Experience working with externally delivered or outsourced systems

  • Familiarity with testing frameworks and structured test methodologies

Behaviours

  • Precision-minded — takes accuracy and correctness seriously, especially in financial contexts

  • Service-minded and approachable — enjoys helping colleagues understand and resolve ledger-related questions

  • Curious and continuous learner who stays current with industry practices

  • Collaborative team member who shares knowledge and supports colleagues

  • Takes ownership of problems and follows through to resolution

  • Constructive and clear in requirements discussions and supplier reviews

Education
BSc or MSc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.

Languages
Fluent English is required for this role.

We Offer

  • A key role in building and shaping a modern, cloud-native core ledger platform

  • A collaborative, cross-functional team environment with short decision paths

  • Competitive salary and benefits package

  • Flexible working arrangements with hybrid options

  • Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning

  • A chance to make a real impact in the fintech space across the Nordic market

  • Modern office in Hamburg with a strong team culture

title: Clinical Education Manager
description:
Monday - Friday schedule, no nights/weekends/holidays/on call
Rewarding development of direct patient care staff
Structured professional development to enhance personal growth
Collaboration with operational and clinical leaders to ensure quality patient care is provided to our dialysis patients
Facilitation and implementation of our world-class dialysis training program
Opportunity for professional development by participating in national projects related to the development of our staff Ability to support all dialysis modalities

PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Supports the training and education of employees based in company facilities and programs within an assigned geographical area. Delivers standardized, competency based, training and development programs including new employee orientation, preceptor training, facility specific in- services, and clinical, quality, and customer service training programs to promote quality patient care, and achieve Area, Regional and/or Enterprise wide initiatives & goals.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Aligns with regional, group, and corporate, business-related goals through all activities and actions.
  • Adheres to standards and scope of nursing practice, company policy, procedures, compliance, regulatory, legal, and ethical, requirements.
  • Focuses all programs towards achieving a culture of patient safety, clinical quality goals, and improving patient outcomes, using company and industry guidelines and standards.
  • Develops competency and skills to educate personnel in all chronic kidney disease treatment programs.
  • Empowers and promotes a culture of lifelong learning through professional programs, coaching, and available technologies.
  • Collaborates with relevant partners at all levels to schedule, provide, and implement education programs, technologies, and processes for new and current personnel.
  • Utilizes appropriate educational venues and generational learning techniques to deliver, support, and evaluate training.
  • Partners in the implementation of action plans through to resolution, as appropriate.
  • Uses facilitation techniques and the variety of tools and resources available to promote immersive, engaging, learning experiences.
  • Develops and maintains open, effective, and timely communication with internal and external customers such as, but not limited to, company management and facility team, government agencies, physicians, hospitals, and contracted travel personnel.
  • Demonstrates leadership competencies, excellent communication, customer service, continuous quality improvement, relationship development, critical thinking, team building, motivating employees, performance management, and decision- making skills.
  • Supports metric / KPI programs, with timely and complete data documentation, reporting, and ongoing quality improvement processes.
  • Maintains knowledge of products, services, and strategies to support and train staff appropriately in all modalities.
  • Perform other related duties as assigned.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
    • Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, Regional and facility/program employees and physicians. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, departmental, and corporate meetings may be required.
    • The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases / materials.
    • This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over. The employee may occasionally be required to move, with assistance, machines, and equipment of up to 200 lbs., and may lift chemical and water solutions of up to 30 lbs. as high as 5 feet.
    • May be asked to provide essential functions of this position in other locations which may include overnight stays, with the same physical demands and working conditions as described above.
  • This position requires between approximately 35-80% travel

SUPERVISION:

  • None

EDUCATION AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

  • Associate or Bachelor’s degree from an accredited School of Nursing.
  • Advanced degree BSN or MSN (preferred)
  • Current appropriate RN state licensure in primary state of residence
  • Obtain and maintain RN state licensure in assigned states as directed
  • Must meet the practice requirements in the state of employment
  • Minimum 1-year dialysis experience as a Registered Nurse
  • CDN or CNN (preferred) or must attain within 18 months of employment
  • Successful completion and maintenance of CPR BLS (Basic Life Safety) Certification, all annual and ongoing required education / training / competencies.

The rate of pay for this position will depend on the successful candidate’s work location and qualifications, including relevant education, work experience, skills, and competencies.

Annual Rate: $71,000.00 - $151,000.00


Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave and potential for performance-based bonuses depending on company and individual performance.

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Data and AI Program Manager, Vice President
description:
Who we are looking for

We are looking for a Data and AI Program Manager reporting directly to global Data and AI Chief of Staff. You will serve as an enterprise leader for Data and AI, overseeing the planning, execution, and delivery of enterprise wide Data and AI initiatives.

Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining is a part of Data and AI organization, a function that is vital to the company. The Data and AI Program Manager will ensure the disciplined execution and value realization while delivering clear, compelling communication to internal and external stakeholders. The role operates at the intersection of strategy, program management, and executive communication.

What you will be responsible for

As a Data and AI Program Manager, you will:

  • Coordinate and lead global Data and AI Programs by partnering with cross‑regional technology teams, business stakeholders, and vendors on the planning, execution, and implementation of IT initiatives. Own day‑to‑day management of assigned programs to ensure delivery against agreed scope, schedule, budget, and quality.

  • Own program documentation and governance, including program plans, status reports, and key decision records. Ensure full alignment with program and project governance, risk management, and audit requirements, and provide proactive support during internal or external reviews.

  • Identify, track, and manage program and operational risks, maintaining up‑to‑date mitigation and contingency plans. Proactively resolve issues where possible and escalate critical risks and dependencies in a timely and structured manner to ensure informed decision‑making by leadership.

  • Act as a key stakeholder interface, ensuring alignment across technology, business, and management teams, and delivering clear, accurate, and audience‑appropriate program communications.

What we value

These skills will help you succeed in this role

  • Experience in Enterprise wide program management with demonstrated project management capability in technology‑enabled environments.

  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with global, cross‑functional stakeholders in both verbal and written contexts.

  • High proficiency in project and productivity tools, including Microsoft Office, MS Project, Jira, Clarity, Confluence, and similar platforms. Experience with analytics tools such as Power BI is a plus.

  • Experience in IT Compliance, Data Management, or AI project management is a plus.

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure, demonstrating resilience, adaptability, and sound judgment in fast‑paced and high‑pressure situations.

Education & Preferred Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Management, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related discipline.

  • Minimum of 10 years of relevant experience in program management, vendor management, capacity planning, or PMO related roles. Experience working on large-scale programs is preferred.

  • Strong knowledge of SDLC, Agile and Waterfall methodologies.

  • Demonstrated technical understanding of software engineering concepts (e.g., system architecture, APIs/integrations, data flows, cloud basics).

  • PMP certification, Data Governance, or any additional project management–related certifications are highly desirable.

Salary Range:

$120,000 - $202,500 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

title: Software Engineer I
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Software Engineer I

Overview
The Software Development Engineer is an early-career software engineer specializing in Java development with a focus on building microservices that support Mastercard's high-throughput, low-latency transaction processing platforms. This role provides opportunities to learn modern software engineering practices while contributing to the development, testing, deployment, and support of business-critical applications.

What You Will Do
•Develop software solutions by translating product requirements into technical implementations with guidance from senior engineers.
•Contribute to software design discussions and propose scalable, maintainable solutions.
•Write modular, readable, and performant code following engineering best practices.
•Develop and maintain unit tests and automated test coverage to validate software quality and reliability.
•Participate in functional and integration testing activities across applications and services.
•Follow secure coding standards and software engineering best practices throughout the development lifecycle.
•Participate in peer code reviews and incorporate feedback to improve code quality and maintainability.
•Assist with prototyping and proof-of-concept initiatives.
•Support cloud-ready microservices and distributed applications.
•Assist in troubleshooting and resolving software defects and production issues.
•Build applications with maintainability, reliability, and operational readiness in mind.
•Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams using Agile Scrum methodologies.

All About You
•Good understanding of Java, J2EE, Spring, Spring Boot, Kafka, RESTful Web Services, and JPA.
•Foundational understanding of software design and architecture concepts.
•Familiarity with unit, functional, and integration testing practices.
•Understanding of secure coding principles.
•Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
•Strong understanding of data structures and their impact on application performance.
•Exposure to microservices and cloud-native application development.
•Knowledge or experience with AI-assisted applications, AI agents, or agentic workflows is a plus.
•Exposure to CI/CD practices and Test-Driven Development.
•Strong communication skills and willingness to learn from experienced engineers.

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

O'Fallon, Missouri: $72,000 - $115,000 USD

title: Junior Network Engineer
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Junior Network Engineer

Our Purpose
We work to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible.
Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company.
We cultivate a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects their individual strengths, views, and experiences.
We believe that our differences enable us to be a better team – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results.

Who is Mastercard?
Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry.
Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible.
Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company.
With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.

Overview
The Global Provisioning Team is providing consumers with a single point of contact to request infrastructure changes, reduce hand-offs and SLA wait time.
The Platform Engineer drives efficiency and provides value to our internal customers and stakeholders. The Platform Engineer II drives efficiency and provides value to our internal customers and stakeholders.
The ideal candidate is highly motivated, intellectually curious, is curious on how to improve processes and possesses an entrepreneurial mindset.


Role
In this position, you will:
• Manage Smart PDU monitoring and infrastructure health.
• Support port capacity tracking and utilization reporting.
• Maintain DCIM systems, asset inventories, and operational data.
• Provide operational support for data centre infrastructure services.
• Troubleshoot and resolve infrastructure incidents and service requests.
• Support infrastructure deployments, upgrades, and lifecycle activities.
• Contribute to automation and operational efficiency initiatives.
• Develop technical documentation and operational procedures.
• Collaborate with engineering teams on infrastructure improvements.
• Ensure compliance with operational and security standards.


All About You
• Platform engineering expertise with exposure to two or more of the following areas: LAN, firewalls, Routing and Switching technologies while adhering to standard design methodologies and best practices.
• Mastery of TCP/IP protocol communications, addressing & subnetting, knowledgeable with IPV6 addressing.
• Knowledge of Cisco’s product portfolio: routers, switches, ACI.
• Knowledge of Aruba product portfolio: switches, ClearPass, APs.
• Knowledge of Arista product portfolio: Spine leaf architecture, Cloud Vision.
• Cisco certifications such as CCNA required, DCCOR preferred.
• Familiarity with scripting tools such as Python, Ansible and Automation to improve process efficiencies and remove errors due to manual inputs.
• Knowledge of Infoblox (inc. DHCP, DNS).
• Datacenter support experience including racking and cabling of IT equipment is a plus.
• Ability to follow process - Evaluate and propose process enhancements to drive efficiencies.


Desirable or additional capabilities
• Strong verbal and written communications skills, and excellent customer support capabilities.
• Self-motivated demonstrating strong organizational and listening skills; and very detail oriented with the ability to carry out assigned tasks with minimal supervision.
• Ability to consistently act in a professional manner and communicate with all levels of the organization, both internally and externally.
• Methodical problem resolving skills.
• Implementation of Cisco ACI.
• Understanding of spine-leaf technologies and Cloud Vision is good to have.
• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Math, Engineering or equivalent combination of education and experience.

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

O'Fallon, Missouri: $76,000 - $127,000 USD

title: School meal worker (Hot Drop) - Nenagh HQ
description:
A little bit about us

Looking for a job that actually makes a difference - and fits into your day-to-day routine?

At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help children build healthy habits for life.

We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are!

So, what’s the job?

We’re on the hunt for School Lunch Operatives – reliable, cheerful people who take pride in doing things right and bring a bit of warmth to every school day.

Think of yourself as the final link in the lunch chain. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the Hot Drop Locations. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven, loading them into vans and driving them to the schools.

If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you:

  • Load pre-packaged school meals into ovens.

  • Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines.

  • Unload ovens and load into vans.

  • Drive the lunches in a company vehicle to the local schools.

  • Ensure adequate stock levels.

  • Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule.

  • Maintain temperature and cleaning records.

  • HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided.

What we’re looking for

Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers - people who are sound, good-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal.

We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every facility in Ireland has a lift

Working for children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly.

All you need is the ability to read English, have full clean Irish driving license, and heat each lunch with care.

We’re looking for cheerful people who like being out and about. Teleportation would make life easier, but until that arrives, we need drivers who are happy to take a roughly one-hour trip to deliver meals to local schools.

I still have questions actually…

No problem! We’re here to answer them.

When will I work?

This job is offered on a part-time basis, 6 hours a day, Monday through Friday! Typical hours of work are 08:00 to 14:00 with some flexibility required as start and finish times may be a little earlier or later.

Will I have to work all year long?

No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well-deserved rest! Our staff sign on to Social Welfare as Educational Sector Workers during the summer months.

What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag?

Well, for starters - a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back.

The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms.

How much is it paid?

It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn €14.65 per hour – 50c above minimum wage.

title: Senior Solutions Consultant, Personalization
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Solutions Consultant, Personalization

About the Role

As a Senior Solutions Consultant, you will act as a strategic partner to Tier 0–1 customers, designing and delivering data-driven personalization programs that drive measurable uplift. You will shape multi-quarter campaign roadmaps, translate insights into innovative concepts, and own campaign performance across your portfolio, working cross-functionally to execute a cohesive personalization vision. This role requires advanced personalization expertise and the ability to lead complex customer initiatives from end to end.



Key Responsibilities



Advanced Personalization Strategy & Innovation

Design and oversee complex, insight-driven personalization roadmaps that span multiple channels, targeting strategies, and KPIs.

Own the health of the campaign portfolio across accounts, proactively managing risk, pacing, and value creation.

Develop executive-level narratives that tie campaign performance to strategic business outcomes.

Value Planning & Measurement

Build and maintain account-level and vertical-level value frameworks, surfacing the ROI of use cases and campaigns.

Lead quarterly roadmap planning cycles, define campaign sequencing models, and contribute to renewal strategies through measurable impact narratives.

Own and evolve value scorecards across your book of business and support internal enablement with case studies and impact reporting.

Commercial Strategy & Renewals

Partner with Customer Success and Sales to shape renewal conversations and identify upsell opportunities.

Support commercial discussions with strategic roadmap data, performance benchmarks, and use case expansion strategies.

Subject Matter Expertise

Act as a go-to expert in a defined domain (e.g., AI, financial institutions, retail strategy, analytics, segmentation).

Provide guidance and thought leadership internally and externally to advance customer success and innovation.

Cross-Functional Influence & Stakeholder Management

Act as a senior point of contact for customer executives and cross-functional internal teams (Sales, CS, ITPM, TAM).

Drive alignment on prioritization, campaign planning, and delivery timelines through structured collaboration frameworks.

Operational Excellence & Escalation Ownership

Define standards for personalization innovation, roadmap deliverables, and solutions commercial success

Partner with internal delivery leads to streamline operational workflows and improve efficiency across pods

Mentorship & Team Enablement

Coach Consultants and Associates on personalization, planning and customer communication

Contribute to training materials, onboarding resources, and reusable playbooks to raise team-wide delivery quality



What We're Looking For

Experience in consulting, strategy, digital marketing, or customer success roles, ideally in a SaaS or agency environment.

Proven experience delivering complex, measurable marketing programs using data insights and analytics.

Strong grasp of personalization, A/B testing, segmentation, and performance marketing concepts.

Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with experience engaging executive-level stakeholders.

Comfort working cross-functionally in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.

Strategic thinker who can translate technical performance into business outcomes.

Experience with tools like Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, or similar analytics tools is a plus.

Must be business fluent in Portuguese, English and Spanish

Please send your CV/Resume in English

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
