  • Salario competitivo.

  • Un trabajo dinámico, humano y muy gratificante.

title: Flytt och Bortforsling - Sommarjobb
description:
Sommarjobb i Göteborg – Vecka 30–31

Vill du ha ett aktivt sommarjobb där dagarna går fort och du får jobba tillsammans med ett härligt team?

Vi på Ungdomskraft söker nu flera sommarjobbare till vårt team i Göteborg under vecka 30–31.

Det här är ett perfekt jobb för dig som vill arbeta lite under sommaren, vara fysiskt aktiv och få värdefull arbetslivserfarenhet – utan att behöva ha tidigare erfarenhet.

Det här får du hos oss

  • Ett varierande jobb där ingen dag är den andra lik

  • Schyssta kollegor i din egen ålder

  • Värdefull arbetslivserfarenhet

  • Ett aktivt jobb där du får röra på dig hela dagarna

  • En trygg introduktion tillsammans med en erfaren kollega

Om jobbet

Som en del av vårt team hjälper du privatpersoner runt om i Göteborg med olika typer av hushållsnära tjänster, exempelvis:

  • Flytt- och bärhjälp

  • Transport av möbler

  • Bortforsling

  • Enklare handyman- och monteringsuppdrag

Du arbetar alltid tillsammans med en erfaren medarbetare som gör den här typen av uppdrag varje dag. Det betyder att du får en ordentlig introduktion och stöd under hela perioden, vilket gör det enkelt att snabbt komma in i arbetet. Ingen tidigare erfarenhet krävs, vi lär dig det du behöver kunna.

Arbetstid: Vardagar, dagtid
Plats: Göteborg
Period: Vecka 30–31

Vem söker vi?

Vi bryr oss mer om din inställning än vad som står på ditt CV.

Vi tror att du:

  • Är social och tycker om att träffa människor

  • Är ansvarstagande och punktlig

  • Har en positiv inställning och gillar att samarbeta

  • Inte är rädd för att ta i när det behövs

  • Trivs med ett fysiskt arbete där du är i rörelse

Krav

  • Flytande svenska i tal och skrift

  • God fysik

  • Belastningsregister utan anmärkning

Om Ungdomskraft

Ungdomskraft hjälper unga att ta sina första steg in på arbetsmarknaden samtidigt som vi levererar förstklassig service till våra kunder. Vi tror på ansvar, gemenskap och att det ska vara roligt att gå till jobbet. Hos oss blir du en del av ett engagerat team där alla hjälps åt och utvecklas tillsammans.

Ansök redan idag!

Vill du ha ett fartfyllt sommarjobb där du får arbeta utomhus, träffa människor, utvecklas och tjäna pengar under två intensiva veckor?

Skicka in din ansökan redan idag! Vi rekryterar löpande och antalet platser är begränsat.

CV och erfarenhet är inte obligatorsikt.

title: (수원) H/W & F/W 개발자
description:
About

[ 글로벌 1등 체외진단 기업을 위한 지속적인 기술혁신 ]

에스디바이오센서는 인류의 건강과 행복에 이바지하기 위해 혁신적인 기술력과 노하우가 접목된 체외진단 제품을 공급하는 글로벌 바이오 진단 기업입니다.

2020년 우리는 코로나 바이러스로 전례 없는 힘든 시기를 보냈습니다. 에스디바이오센서는 전 세계 코로나 바이러스 방역에 이바지하고자 다양한 진단 제품 개발에 집중하였고, 빠른 시간 내에 신종 코로나바이러스 유전자 공개 이후 템플릿 합성, 재조합 항원 개발에 성공하였습니다. 이후 STANDARD M nCoV Real-time PCR 제품 질병관리본부 긴급사용 승인, STANDARD Q COVID-19 IgM/IgG 제품 개발, 인플루엔자와 동시에 진단할 수 있는 COVID/Flu 제품 등 다양한 코로나바이러스 진단 제품 개발에 성공하였으며, 현재까지도 끊임없는 제품 개선 및 개발 활동을 통해 가파른 성장을 하고 있습니다.

에스디바이오센서는 구성원과 함께 성장하는 기업입니다.

1. 업계 상위의 보상 수준
2. 업무에만 집중할 수 있도록 지원하는 복리후생
3. 도전하는 만큼 주어지는 기회와 권한
4. 수익이 나면 열매를 함께 공유하는 경영진의 철학
5. 젊은 인재들이 모인 수평적 조직

우리 팀을 소개할게요!
하드웨어&펌웨어 개발자는 기기개발본부에 속해 있으며, 연구소 연구원들과 함께 체외진단 의료기기 개발 연구를 진행하고 있습니다. 수평적인 문화 속에서 구성원 간의 자극을 통해 함께 성장하기를 원하시는 분에게 매우 좋은 기회입니다.

Team Talk (동료의 한 마디)
하드웨어&펌웨어 개발자는 에스디바이오센서의 메인 업무로서 연구소 비즈니스의 성장을 만드는 역할을 합니다. 빠르게 성장하는 회사와 함께 서로에게 자극이 되면서 성장하고자 하는 우수한 동료 분을 기다립니다.

에스디바이오센서에서 함께 글로벌 1등 체외진단 기업을 만들어 나갈 당신을 기다립니다.

Responsibilities

하드웨어(H/W)&펌웨어(F/W) 장비개발 담당자로 MCU 및 주변 회로 설계를 담당하고 ST, TI 등 MCU F/W를 개발합니다. 또한 실제 운영 환경에서 발생하는 문제와 이슈에 대응하고 개선을 담당하고 의료기기 기술 문서를 작성합니다.

• MCU 및 주변 회로 설계
• ST, TI 등 MCU F/W 개발
• 의료기기 기술 문서 작성

Requirements

• 컴퓨터 및 정보통신 등 공학계열 전공자
• 경력 5년 이상에 준하는 경험
• 운전가능자

Preferred

• 광학부를 이용한 측정기 개발 경험자

Benefits

복리후생
• 복지포인트 연120만원 지급
• 식사 제공
• 하계 휴가 지원
• 회사 콘도 지원

Education 교육
• 어학 교육 지원
• 직무 교육 지원
• 임직원 도서 구매 지원

title: Personlig assistent sökes till 43-årig man i Töreboda
description:
Vill du ha ett arbete där du verkligen gör skillnad? Vi söker nu engagerade och trygga personliga assistenter till en 43-årig man som bor i egen lägenhet i Töreboda.

Som personlig assistent får du en viktig roll i att skapa trygghet, struktur och en fungerande vardag för kunden. Du blir en del av ett stabilt team som arbetar nära kunden med fokus på kontinuitet, kvalitet och ett respektfullt bemötande.

Om kunden

Kunden har autism, en lindrig intellektuell funktionsnedsättning med omfattande beteendeutmaningar samt en psykossjukdom. Han har stora kommunikationssvårigheter och behöver därför ett lugnt, tydligt och förutsägbart stöd i sin vardag.

För att skapa trygghet är det viktigt att du som assistent lär känna kunden väl, kan tolka hans kroppsspråk och uppmärksammar tidiga signaler på stress eller oro. Ett låg affektivt och förebyggande arbetssätt är en viktig del av arbetet.

Dina arbetsuppgifter

Som personlig assistent hjälper du kunden med både vardagliga aktiviteter och att skapa en trygg och strukturerad dag. Arbetsuppgifterna innefattar bland annat:

  • Stöd vid personlig hygien, såsom dusch, toalettbesök och tandvård.

  • Hjälp med på- och avklädning. Kunden är noggrann med att kläderna ska sitta på rätt sätt och kan behöva byta om flera gånger under dagen.

  • Stöd vid måltider samt hjälp med matlagning, dukning och disk.

  • Hushållssysslor som städning, tvätt och inköp.

  • Promenader och bilutflykter, aktiviteter som kunden uppskattar och mår bra av.

  • Planering och genomförande av fasta rutiner för att skapa trygghet och förutsägbarhet.

  • Nattarbete med väntetid/dygnsvila samt aktiva insatser vid behov, exempelvis vid toalettbesök eller om kunden vaknar och behöver stöd.

  • Medverkan vid längre utflykter några gånger per år tillsammans med ytterligare en assistent.

Vi söker dig som

Vi tror att du passar för rollen om du är:

  • Lugn, trygg och tålmodig.

  • Lyhörd och uppmärksam på små förändringar.

  • Ansvarsfull och har ett högt säkerhetstänk.

  • Stabil och konsekvent i ditt bemötande.

  • Flexibel men samtidigt trygg med att följa rutiner och överenskomna arbetssätt.

  • Intresserad av att bygga en långsiktig och förtroendefull relation med kunden.

Tidigare erfarenhet av arbete med personer med autism, intellektuell funktionsnedsättning eller psykiatriska diagnoser är meriterande, men stor vikt kommer att läggas vid personlig lämplighet.

Arbetstid

Tjänsten innebär främst nattarbete, cirka två nätter per vecka med väntetid/dygnsvila.

Det finns även möjlighet att arbeta dag- och helgpass vid behov, exempelvis vid sjukfrånvaro, semester eller andra tillfällen då extra stöd behövs.

Vi erbjuder dig

Hos oss får du ett meningsfullt och utvecklande arbete där din insats har stor betydelse för en annan människas livskvalitet.

Du blir en viktig del av ett engagerat assistentteam där vi värdesätter trygghet, kontinuitet och ett professionellt arbetssätt. Tillsammans skapar vi de bästa förutsättningarna för en fungerande och trygg vardag för kunden.

Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Commercial Account Executive - Mixed Industry
description:
The Commercial Account Executive role focuses exclusively on formulating and building a sales strategy within an assigned territory, resulting in revenue growth and new customer acquisition. You will report into a Sales Director working in a small, tight knit team.

Responsibilities:

  • Identify, qualify and close a sales pipeline
  • Prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT Leaders, & technical end-users
  • Build relationships that result in growth opportunities
  • Partner with our Solution Engineers and utilize the village throughout the sales cycle
  • Participate in our sales enablement trainings
  • Business deal-making capability, and the ability to ferret out opportunities, create relationships, find the hidden issues during due diligence, and bring the transaction to closure.
  • Demonstrated track record in planning and implementation of new business activity involving new technology


Required Experience:

  • 5+ years of experience growing revenues to a substantial level and scale bookings growth and net-new customer
  • 2-4 years of experience in sales functions - preferably 2+ years in SaaS related sales
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience
  • Willingness and ability to travel up to 50% of the time
  • Consistent track record of quota achievement in the SaaS industry, closing deals exceeding $50k ARR
  • Experienced in selling SaaS-based solutions, managing complex sales practices and solution-based selling to CXO, senior management and director-level individuals
  • Proficient with web-based demo software and tools
  • Stellar work ethic, passion for closing and exceptional drive
  • Strategic thinker with the ability to execute both short-term and long-term sales plans
  • Strong interpersonal skills and willingness to excel in a team-oriented atmosphere
  • Strong planning, writing, presentation and communication skills

Pay Range (OTE Pay): $180,000 - $200,000

Other Types of Pay: Based on level and role the employee may be eligible for long term incentives in the form of equity and short term incentives of either bonus or commission.

Health Insurance: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life and Disability Insurance, Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

Retirement Benefits: 401(k) plan

Paid Time Off: Talkdesk offers an uncapped paid time off program for exempt employees and an accrual-based program for non-exempt employees; both are subject to manager approval and consistent with business needs.

Paid Holidays: Talkdesk offers 14 paid holidays each year.

Paid Sick Leave: Exempt employees have uncapped paid time off and non-exempt sick leave follows accrual standards; both are subject to manager approval and consistent with business needs.

Method of Application: Apply online.

Application Window: The application window is expected to close at least 3 days from the posting date. The application was posted on 07/24/2026

Benefits and perks listed above may vary based on the nature of your employment with Talkdesk.

All questions or concerns about this posting should be directed to the Talent team at Talent@talkdesk.com.

Talkdesk is pioneering a new era of Customer Experience Automation (CXA), redefining how the world’s most admired brands interact with their customers through AI. Our global team of courageous innovators is customer-obsessed, building AI-first solutions that put empathy, trust, and transparency at the center of every interaction. We foster an inclusive culture where diverse perspectives drive our success and every voice belongs. Combining the stability of a global leader with the agility of a disruptor, Talkdeskers are empowered with the autonomy to drive meaningful impact, while giving back to the communities and environment around us.

Talkdesk has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) and in the G2 Overall Grid® Reports for AI Agents and Contact Center. With seven consecutive years on the Forbes Cloud 100 and multiple AI Breakthrough awards, there has never been a more exciting time to join us as we shape the future of customer experience automation!

Work Environment and Physical Requirements:

Primarily office-environment work, extended periods of sitting or standing, computer-based work. Limited lifting, and equipment usage limited to computer-related equipment (keyboards, mouse, etc.)

The Talkdesk story hinges on empathy and acceptance. It is the shared goal among all Talkdeskers to empower a new kind of customer hero through our innovative software solution, and we firmly believe that the best path to success for our mission is inclusivity, diversity, and genuine acceptance. To that end, we will hire, promote, work along, cheer for, bond with, and warmly welcome into the Talkdesk family all persons without regard to ethnic and racial identity, indigenous heritage, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, marital status, veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

title: Interest & Product Logic Specialist
description:
We are looking for a specialist who combines financial know-how with strong analytical skills to join our Core Ledger team. Our platform contain core ledger systems both fully developed internaly and developed and delivered by external suppliers. As an Interest & Product Logic Specialist, you will define and document the business rules behind interest, fees, and product configurations, and verify and test that what our internal- and external suppliers deliver matches our requirements and works correctly. A significant part of this role is providing hands-on internal support to colleagues across the business who have questions about interest, fees, and product behaviour — this is something all specialists in the team spend a lot of time on. Getting this right is essential — it directly affects how accurate and competitive our financial products are. You will join a small, close-knit team and have a great opportunity to make a direct impact early on and help shape it as it grows.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and document detailed requirements for interest calculation logic, accrual rules, and fee structures, to be delivered by external suppliers

  • Translate financial product requirements from business and product teams into precise specifications for our suppliers

  • Verify and test the product configuration delivered by suppliers, ensuring it behaves correctly for all financial instruments

  • Ensure the mathematical and financial correctness of calculation logic delivered, including edge cases and regulatory requirements

  • Collaborate with finance, compliance, and product teams to validate that supplier deliveries meet business intent

  • Support audit and regulatory reporting by ensuring calculation logic is transparent, traceable, and well-documented

  • Investigate and resolve discrepancies in interest calculations, fee postings, or product behaviour, working with suppliers when needed

  • Define and execute test cases for financial calculation logic, including boundary and regression tests

  • Provide hands-on internal support to other teams (finance, customer service, operations, and others) on questions about interest, fees, and product behaviour — this is a significant and ongoing part of the role

Qualifications and Experience

Desired Skills

  • 4+ years of experience in a financial, analytical, or product role within financial services, lending, or payments

  • Strong understanding of interest calculation methodologies (e.g. effective interest rate, simple interest, compound interest, XIRR)

  • Experience writing detailed financial business rules and specifications for systems built by others

  • Solid grasp of product structures in consumer credit, BNPL, or instalment lending

  • Ability to work with complex financial specifications and verify that delivered systems implement them correctly

Nice to Have

  • Knowledge of Nordic or European consumer credit regulation (e.g. Consumer Credit Directive)

  • Experience with configuration-driven financial product frameworks delivered by suppliers

  • Background in actuarial science, quantitative finance, or financial mathematics

  • Familiarity with accounting principles as they relate to interest and fee recognition

Behaviours

  • Exceptional analytical rigour — comfortable with mathematical precision and financial logic

  • Service-minded and approachable — enjoys helping colleagues understand interest, fee, and product logic

  • Strong bridge-builder between finance/business language and supplier requirements

  • Thorough documenter — understands that correctness must be explainable and auditable

  • Curious and proactive in challenging assumptions and validating requirements

  • Collaborative, with an ability to work effectively with business stakeholders and external suppliers

Education

BSc or MSc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.

Languages

Fluent English is required for this role. Fluent Swedish is not a hard requirement, but is desirable as Swedish is the language spoken locally in the team and will make day-to-day collaboration easier.

What We Offer

  • A key role in building and shaping a modern, cloud-native core ledger platform

  • A collaborative, cross-functional team environment with short decision paths

  • Competitive salary and benefits package

  • Flexible working arrangements with hybrid options

  • Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning

  • A chance to make a real impact in the fintech space across the Nordic market

  • Modern office in central Borås with a strong team culture

title: Outpatient Dialysis LPN/LVN
description:
Description
Provide your personal attention and kindness, professional insight, and a generosity of spirit. Offer expertise and encouragement. Enhance each patient's future-as well as your own.
Connect with your goals and change lives
with Fresenius Medical Care North America.

Here, we make connections that empower patients, their families, and our team members. As the global leader in dialysis healthcare, we form bonds that enable the best possible outcomes and professional satisfaction. While our extensive organization continues to grow, so do opportunities for our professionals to learn, grow, and advance.

Why Join the Fresenius Team?

Passion. Dedication. Knowledge. Motivation. Experience. These are the impressive qualities you'll find in the Fresenius Leadership Team. Our strength in the North American market and extensive global network provide our employees with the best of both worlds-the friendliness of a local organization and the stability of a worldwide organization-for diverse experiences and challenging career opportunities. When you join the Fresenius Medical Care team, you'll be welcomed into a company that is built on the philosophy that our employees are our most important asset. Our career advantages include the following:

  • Fresenius Medical Care is the nation's largest provider of renal care, meeting the needs of more than 135,000 patients at 1,800 clinics throughout the country.
  • Our well-established, trusted organization fosters a spirit of camaraderie, emphasizing friendly collaboration, professional support, and career development.
  • Superior training, UltraCare® quality control, and certification procedures ensure your potential to succeed and advance as a professional.
  • Competitive compensation and exceptional benefits.
  • Outstanding tuition reimbursement program.
  • Recognized among Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies in 2011.
  • National Safety Award from CNA insurance companies for 11 consecutive years.
  • Opportunities to give back by participating in philanthropy and community outreach programs.

PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Supports FMCNA's mission, vision, core values and customer service philosophy. Adheres to the FMCNA Compliance Program, including following all regulatory and division/company policy requirements.

Functions as part of the hemodialysis health care team in providing safe and effective dialysis therapy for assigned patients under the direct supervision of a registered nurse. Participates in the implementation and evaluation of patient care. Responsible for reporting and documenting all new or unusual incidents, information, complaints, or problems to the supervisor. Promotes and assists in the maintenance of a sage and clean work environment.

DUTIES / ACTIVITIES:

CUSTOMER SERVICE:

  • Responsible for driving the FMCNA culture through values and customer service standards.
  • Accountable for outstanding customer service to all external and internal customers.
  • Develops and maintains effective relationships through effective and timely communication.
  • Takes initiative and action to respond, resolve and follow up regarding customer service issues with all customers in a timely manner.

PRINCIPAL RESPONSIBILITIES AND DUTIES:

Patient Related:

  • Provide direct patient care for assigned patient(s).
  • Perform all technical aspects of dialysis procedures for assigned patients as prescribed. Administer and monitor treatments in accordance with current treatment plan or as directed by the supervisor.
  • Monitor and document dialysis treatment parameters on dialysis flow sheets. Document other information related to the care of the patient in the individual patient record.
  • Responsible for reporting any significant information, change in patient condition, or equipment problems to the supervisor.
  • Assist in developing and following the teaching plan to educate the patient and family regarding end stage renal disease, dialysis therapy, diet and medications.
  • Assist with all emergency operational procedures.
  • Administer intravenous medication in accordance with physician orders and state nurse practice laws.
  • Perform and document pre, interim, and post treatment review of patient condition.
  • Initiate basic CPR measures in the event of cardiac and/or pulmonary arrest, and respond to emergency situations related to dialysis treatment.

Technical:

  • Safely operate all dialysis related equipment according to the proper procedures.
  • Provide minor troubleshooting when necessary.
  • Ensure a clean, safe and sanitary environment in the dialysis facility treatment area.
  • Ensure all blood spills are immediately addressed according to FMCNA Bloodborne Pathogen Control Policies.
  • Operate all emergency equipment effectively in accordance to the standard operation procedures.

Staff Related:

  • Participate in staff meetings as scheduled.
  • Assist in short term and long term patient care plan meetings.
  • Acquire information and knowledge in current practice related to dialysis principals and technique by participating in scheduled in-service classes.
  • Act as a resource person for other staff members. Assist with the coordination of the care plan with Physician, Dietitian and Social Worker.
  • Other duties as assigned.
PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:
The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, facility, staff and physicians. The position provides direct patient care that regularly involves heavy lifting and moving of patients, and assisting with ambulation. Equipment aids and/or coworkers may provide assistance. This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over. The employee may occasionally be required to move, with assistance, machines and equipment weighing up to 200lbs., and may lift chemical water solutions of up to 30lbs., up as high as 5 feet. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, Business Unit and Corporate meetings may be required.
The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noises levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials.
EDUCATION:
  • Graduate of an accredited School of Practical Nursing (LPN, LVN)
  • Current appropriate State licensure.
EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:
  • One year medical-surgical nursing experience preferred.
  • Hemodialysis experience preferred but not required.
  • ICU experience preferred but not required.
  • Successfully complete a training course in the theory and practice of hemodialysis.
  • Successfully complete CPR Certification.
  • Must complete the Nurses Technical Training Program upon hire or soon thereafter.
  • Ability to provide coverage at area facilities during times of short-staffing or as required.
  • Employees must meet the necessary requirements of Ishihara's Color Blindness test as a condition of employment.
  • Good communication skills, both verbal and written.

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Cloud Platform Engineer
description:
About Varnish Software

Varnish Software is a Scandinavian-based tech company developing caching technology for some of the world's biggest players in the content delivery market. Our technology helps businesses worldwide improve website performance, deliver video streams, and scale their infrastructure.

About the role 💼

We are looking for a self-driven Cloud Platform Engineer to help build, automate, and work on the tooling and infrastructure that creates our cloud images and containers, as well as the Kubernetes platform they run on.

This is a hands-on engineering role. You'll have the freedom and trust to solve problems independently, improve the platform through automation, and contribute to the way we build and operate our infrastructure. From Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD pipelines to Kubernetes operations and cloud security, you'll own improvements from idea through implementation.

What you will work on 🎯

  • Building and maintaining cloud images and container pipelines across AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

  • Automating infrastructure using Terraform, Ansible, Packer, and Infrastructure as Code practices.

  • Operating and improving our Kubernetes platform, including deployments, scaling, configuration, and troubleshooting.

  • Building CI/CD pipelines for building, testing, scanning, signing, and publishing images and containers.

  • Improving software supply chain security through SBOM generation, vulnerability scanning, artifact signing, and build provenance.

  • Monitoring platform health, improving observability, and contributing to actionable alerting and runbooks.

  • Participating in a shared on-call rotation and helping investigate, resolve, and learn from production incidents.

  • Working closely with other engineering teams to improve developer experience, platform reliability, and automation.

What we expect 🧑‍💻

  • A few years' experience working as a Cloud Engineer, Platform Engineer, DevOps Engineer, Site Reliability Engineer, or in a similar infrastructure-focused role.

  • Strong Linux administration and troubleshooting skills.

  • Hands-on experience with Kubernetes in production environments.

  • Experience building and maintaining Docker/OCI container images.

  • Strong knowledge of at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure).

  • Experience with Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and Ansible or similar tools.

  • Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines.

  • Comfortable writing automation using Python, Go, Shell, or similar languages.

  • A solid understanding of infrastructure security, including IAM, secrets management, and least privilege.

  • The ability to take a well-defined problem, learn from existing patterns, and deliver a high-quality solution independently.

  • Good written and verbal communication skills in English.

We also care about curiosity, a collaborative mindset, and people who enjoy learning. We value engineers who write maintainable automation, share knowledge with their teammates, and continuously look for ways to improve the platform.

Nice-To-Have ⭐️

  • Experience working across multiple cloud providers.

  • Experience with Packer.

  • Experience implementing modern software supply chain security practices using tools such as Syft, Trivy, Grype, Cosign, Sigstore, or similar.

  • Experience with GitOps workflows.

  • Experience with Prometheus, Grafana, or other observability tooling.

  • Experience operating SaaS platforms in production.

How we work 💙

  • A small, experienced engineering team in Karlstad where your work has a visible impact on the platform used by our products and customers.

  • Part of a global engineering organisation where you'll collaborate with developers across multiple teams and locations.

  • Flexible working hours and the ability to work from home. We trust you to manage your own time.

  • Modern infrastructure tooling and cloud-native technologies, with opportunities to influence how we build and operate our platform.

  • A culture that values teamwork, simplicity, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement over unnecessary complexity.

  • A flat, non-hierarchical environment. We value ownership, collaboration, curiosity, and trust

How to Apply

Ready to join our team? Simply click the "Apply" button to start your application process. We look forward to learning more about you and how you can contribute to our success. Don’t hesitate—take the first step towards your new career with us today!

Summer hiring notice: Due to summer vacations, we screen during July but the formal interview process will start in August, however we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.

title: ABA Clinical Supervisor
location: Desert Hot Springs, California, United States
description:
Clinical Supervisor – Mid Tier (12 Units of Master’s Coursework Required)

In-Home, Community-Based, & Clinic Settings | Full-Time
Hourly Rate: $28-$35 DOE + Monthly Bonuses


About The Behavior Lab

The Behavior Lab is a play-based ABA company that helps children with behavioral challenges, developmental delays, or Autism thrive through compassionate, individualized care. We work in homes, schools, clinics, and communities - wherever children need us most.


Our team is supportive, collaborative, and passionate about making a difference. If you're a skilled BCBA looking to grow in a flexible, encouraging environment, we’d love to connect.


About the Role

We’re looking for a compassionate and experienced Clinical Supervisor to lead and support a team of dedicated behavioral health professionals. If you have a strong background working with children and individuals with developmental disabilities - and a passion for making a difference - this is your opportunity to play a key role in shaping high-quality, person-centered care.


As a Clinical Supervisor, you’ll oversee clinical services, guide therapeutic strategy, and ensure our standards for care and documentation are met. This role blends leadership, mentorship, and hands-on clinical expertise to ensure our clients receive the care they deserve.


Key Responsibilities


  • Supervise and support a team of clinicians providing behavioral health services to children and individuals with developmental disabilities.
  • Conduct clinical assessments and help design individualized treatment plans.
  • Oversee data collection and analysis to track patient progress and inform treatment decisions.
  • Ensure timely, accurate, and compliant documentation, including assessments, evaluations, and treatment records.
  • Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to create effective, evidence-based treatment strategies.
  • Mentor clinical staff and provide training on behavioral therapy best practices.
  • Stay up to date with regulatory and organizational guidelines to maintain compliance across services.


What We’re Looking For

Required:

  • Direct experience working with children in behavioral health settings is required.
  • A strong understanding of behavioral therapy techniques, especially for developmental disabilities.
  • Knowledge of medical records management and documentation standards.
  • Exceptional communication and collaboration skills.
  • Empathy, professionalism, and a client-centered mindset.
  • Completion of at least 12 units of a Master’s-level program in a relevant field (e.g., psychology, counseling, social work).


Preferred:

  • Proficiency in data-driven treatment planning and diagnostic evaluation.
  • Experience supervising RBTs or BTs
  • Bilingual in English and Spanish


Why Join Us?

At The Behavior Lab, we believe work should be meaningful and enjoyable. We’re not your typical ABA agency - we’re a close-knit team that supports one another, celebrates wins (big and small), and believes in doing great work without burnout.


Here’s what makes us different:


  • Flexible schedule – You choose your hours and manage your caseload your way
  • Monthly bonuses – We reward the impact you make
  • Growth opportunities – Clear path to Clinical Manager of Programs or BCBA
  • Supportive team – Weekly case consults and mentorship
  • Fun culture – Team socials, group chats, inside jokes, and real friendships
  • Annual retreats – Leadership and upper management enjoy retreats focused on connection, vision-setting, and recharging
  • Full benefits package – Health, dental, vision, 401(k) with match, and more
  • Perks – Paid time off, paid sick time, mileage + phone reimbursement


We’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe diversity and inclusion make us stronger.

title: Imaging Engineer
description:
Logitech is the Sweet Spot for people who want their actions to have a positive global impact while having the flexibility to do it in their own way.

The Team and Role:

Position at Logitech PWS (Personal Workspace solution) engineering group

The Personal Workspace Solution group from Logitech is committed to help people create and communicate their passion and work to the world, anytime anywhere. Putting users front and center, we strive to innovate and elevate their experiences. This is more than computer mice, keyboards, webcams and presentation remotes - it’s about enabling the future beyond today's tools and in a sustainable way. Everything goes through great design and exceptional user experience conveyed in great hardware and elevated by software.

As Imaging Engineer, you will collaborate with a team of engineers on imaging/video products (Webcams and other video capable devices). You will be tasked with delivering amazing video image quality by combining image acquisition, image analysis, image quality tuning and image processing. You will be responsible for analyzing, and implementing video solutions addressing the needs of current and future generations of products in overall objective and subjective image /video quality as well as integrating them in the current and future video capable products.

Your Contribution:

Be Yourself. Be Open. Stay Hungry and Humble. Collaborate. Challenge. Decide and just Do. Share our passion for Equality and the Environment. These are the behaviors and values you’ll need for success at Logitech. In this role you will:

  • Design and execute image quality tests for Logitech webcams.
  • Analyze test results and identify areas for improvement in image quality
  • Collaborate with engineers to optimize image processing algorithms and camera module design
  • Support development of automated tests for IQ validation and webcam end to end testing
  • Implement improvement in the color processing pipeline (Defective pixel correction, color interpolation, gamma, denoise, sharpness, brightness, …)
  • Implement and optimize algorithms utilizing image processing, computational photography, computer vision, Deep Learning techniques as well as AI
  • Support TO (techno office), in porting of features from host to device
  • Contribute to the development of new webcam features that push the boundaries of image quality
  • Lead or assist in the video certification processes for Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and Windows Hello
  • Collaborate with team members to deliver the best cost/experience compromises for our products

Key Qualifications:

For consideration, you must bring the following minimum skills and experiences to our team:

  • Bachelor's degree in Imaging Science, Computer Science, or a related field
  • Strong understanding of image quality metrics and testing methodologies
  • Experience with image processing software and tools (e.g., Imatest, MATLAB)
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Passion for creating high-quality images and a strong user focus

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with camera module design and characterization
  • Familiarity with scripting languages (Python, MATLAB, …)
  • Knowledge of color science and image quality standards

#LI-AL2/104

Across Logitech we empower collaboration and foster play. We help teams collaborate/learn from anywhere, without compromising on productivity or continuity so it should be no surprise that most of our jobs are open to work from home from most locations. Our hybrid work model allows some employees to work remotely while others work on-premises. Within this structure, you may have teams or departments split between working remotely and working in-house.

Logitech is an amazing place to work because it is full of authentic people who are inclusive by nature as well as by design. Being a global company, we value our diversity and celebrate all our differences. Don’t meet every single requirement? Not a problem. If you feel you are the right candidate for the opportunity, we strongly recommend that you apply. We want to meet you!


We offer comprehensive and competitive benefits packages and working environments that are designed to be flexible and help you to care for yourself and your loved ones, now and in the future. We believe that good health means more than getting medical care when you need it. Logitech supports a culture that encourages individuals to achieve good physical, financial, emotional, intellectual and social wellbeing so we all can create, achieve and enjoy more and support our families. We can’t wait to tell you more about them being that there are too many to list here and they vary based on location.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, sex, age, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or on the basis of disability.

If you require an accommodation to complete any part of the application process, are limited in the ability, are unable to access or use this online application process and need an alternative method for applying, you may contact us toll free at +1-510-713-4866 for assistance and we will get back to you as soon as possible.

title: Operations - Dispatcher
description:
🚛 Trucking Operations - Dispatcher

Be the Engine Behind Every Successful Delivery

📍 Location: Whitestown, Indiana
💰 Compensation: $24.00/hour
📅 Schedule: Thursday–Sunday

  • Friday–Sunday: 5:00 AM – 5:00 PM

  • Thursday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM

💥 Why This Role Matters

At TCI, dispatchers are more than schedulers—they are the heartbeat of daily operations.

As a Dispatcher, you’ll coordinate drivers, manage routes, ensure safety compliance, and keep customers satisfied—all while making real-time decisions that impact performance and profitability.

If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy being the person who keeps everything running smoothly, this is your opportunity to step into a high-impact role.

🚀 What You’ll Do

🚦 Operations & Dispatch Management

  • Plan, schedule, and dispatch drivers to ensure all loads are covered

  • Monitor routes, schedules, and real-time changes to keep operations on track

  • Coordinate with teams to optimize routes, equipment, and staffing

🛡️ Safety & Compliance

  • Ensure safety is always the #1 priority across operations

  • Monitor and enforce DOT, HOS (Hours of Service), and federal/state regulations

  • Audit driver logs and report discrepancies

👥 Driver Support & Leadership

  • Train and support new and existing drivers on company policies and procedures

  • Conduct driver meetings and provide ongoing guidance and performance feedback

  • Ensure drivers are properly scheduled and show up ready for their shifts

🤝 Cross-Functional Coordination

  • Partner with Maintenance, Recruiting, Rental, and Safety teams

  • Coordinate equipment needs, repairs, and staffing requirements

  • Serve as a key point of contact for drivers, customers, and internal teams

📊 Customer Service & Performance

  • Maintain strong customer relationships through daily communication

  • Ensure TCI meets or exceeds customer performance targets

  • Balance safety, cost control, and service excellence in every decision

🧠 What You Bring

  • Experience in dispatch, logistics, transportation, or operations (preferred)

  • Strong multitasking and decision-making skills in a fast-paced environment

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

  • High attention to detail and organizational ability

  • Understanding of DOT regulations and Hours of Service (HOS) (preferred)

  • Ability to lead, train, and support drivers

  • Strong sense of accountability, professionalism, and teamwork

🌟 What Sets You Apart

  • You stay calm under pressure and make smart, fast decisions

  • You take ownership of problems and solve them before they escalate

  • You understand the balance between safety, cost, and customer service

  • You’re dependable, flexible, and always ready to step in when needed

💼 Work Environment & Expectations

  • On-site, hands-on operations role with high visibility

  • Requires flexibility for occasional nights, weekends, or holidays

  • Involves regular interaction with drivers, leadership, and customers

  • Fast-paced environment where priorities can shift quickly

🎯 Why Join TCI?

  • Competitive hourly pay at $24.00/hour

  • Be part of a team where your decisions directly impact operations

  • Gain experience across operations, safety, and logistics

  • Join a company that values safety, teamwork, and growth

🚛 About TCI Transportation

Over 45 Years and Counting

Since 1978, TCI Transportation has been dedicated to serving our clients with integrity, passion, and ingenuity. Over the past four decades, we’ve grown into a coast-to-coast transportation partner, continuously expanding our services to help customers operate more efficiently and scale their businesses.

Our story is built on a strong foundation—but we’re far from finished. At TCI, you’ll join a company that is still growing, still innovating, and still investing in its people.

📩 Ready to take control of operations and keep TCI moving?
Apply today and become a key player in our dispatch team.

title: Director Specialist Sales - Egypt
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Director Specialist Sales - Egypt

Who is Mastercard?

Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.
Role Overview
Location: Egypt
• The Director – SME Specialist Sales is based in Egypt, is part of the West Arabia cluster, and reports to the SME Head of Commercialization.
• This role requires a strategic, consultative mindset and the ability to operate credibly as a trusted advisor to clients, combining high-level thinking with strong execution discipline.
• Lead the development and execution of SME growth strategies by combining sales leadership with strategic advisory support, helping clients identify opportunities, solve business challenges and unlock revenue and profitability through tailored solutions.
• Build and manage a strong pipeline while engaging clients through a hands-on, consultative approach, including on-site client interactions, needs assessment, opportunity shaping and solution positioning across Financial Institutions and non-financial institution partners.
• Identify, originate and convert new business opportunities, develop SME ecosystem plays, and drive the commercialization of signed deals to deliver measurable revenue outcomes.
• Contribute to achieving SME P&L objectives across key customers in Egypt while also supporting opportunities in other markets within the ELI (Egypt, Levant and Iraq) cluster, aligned to regional expert sales priorities and business goals.
• Identify product, proposition and business innovation opportunities across SME payment segments.
• Provide subject matter expertise and advisory leadership by working closely with clients and internal stakeholders to diagnose needs, shape strategic recommendations and lead end-to-end execution across sales, product and market teams.
• Lead, coach and develop a high-performing team culture that promotes accountability, collaboration, strong execution and continuous improvement.
Qualifications Required
• Deep knowledge of SME products and services, including SME card and solutions offerings, with significant experience in sales and business development within the SME space.
• Progressive business experience with a strong track record in the payments industry and a solid understanding of commercial and consumer payments.
• Strong strategic thinking and consulting capabilities, including the ability to assess client needs, translate market insights into actionable recommendations and influence decision-making at senior levels.
• Experience in consulting, solution selling or advisory-led client management, with a strong track record of working directly with customers in on-site environments to shape opportunities, guide implementation and deliver business outcomes.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Consumer Lending Business Lead (m/w/d)
description:
Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir einen Consumer Lending Business Lead (m/w/d), der den erfolgreichen Betrieb und die Weiterentwicklung unseres deutschen Konsumentenkreditgeschäfts unterstützt und als vertrauensvoller operativer Sparringspartner für den Country Manager agiert.

Umsetzung und Personalverantwortung bleiben bei den operativen Teams – du sorgst für operative Aufsicht, Koordination und fachliche Führung über Vertrieb, Servicing und Verwaltung hinweg. Es handelt sich um eine Senior-Einzelrolle mit bereichsübergreifender fachlicher Führungsverantwortung, aber ohne disziplinarische Personalverantwortung. Du erzielst Ergebnisse durch strukturierte Koordination, klare Anforderungen, datengestützte Erkenntnisse und starkes Stakeholder-Management.

Deine Aufgaben

  • Kernprozesse in Vertrieb und Onboarding (Lead-Erfassung, KYC-/Bonitätsprüfung, Entscheidung, Auszahlung, Aktivierung) abbilden, dokumentieren, überwachen und optimieren; Automatisierung und Kontrollen einführen, um Durchlaufzeiten und Fehlerquoten zu senken.

  • Kundenkontaktpunkte entlang der Customer Journey definieren und verbessern; Vorlagen und Gesprächsleitfäden erstellen; NPS und externe Bewertungen überwachen und Verbesserungsmaßnahmen leiten.

  • Verantwortung für die Erstellung, Pflege und Weiterentwicklung von Standardprozessen und Eskalationsregelungen sowie für die Sicherstellung einer einheitlichen Dokumentation und konsistenter Reporting-Standards.

  • Gemeinsam mit den operativen Teams Onboarding- und Auffrischungsschulungen entwickeln und koordinieren; bei Veränderungsprozessen fachlich unterstützen.

  • Operative KPIs überwachen und analysieren (Konversionsrate, Erstzahlungsquote, Zahlungsausfälle, NPS), Ursachenanalysen leiten und Korrekturmaßnahmen umsetzen.

  • Standards für Qualitätssicherung und Kontrollen mit den operativen Teams festlegen, deren Wirksamkeit überwachen und Verbesserungen vorschlagen.

  • Lösung operativer Störungen und vertriebsbezogener Probleme koordinieren, mit angemessener Eskalation und zeitnaher Behebung.

  • Eng mit dem Country Manager und den operativen Teams zusammenarbeiten, um operative Umsetzung an Geschäftsprioritäten und Kundenerfahrung auszurichten; fachliche Vorgaben bereitstellen, ohne disziplinarische Führungsverantwortung.

  • Kommunikation und Abstimmung mit externen Partnern sicherstellen, um Geschäftsziele effizient umzusetzen und langfristige Beziehungen zu pflegen.

  • Sicherstellen, dass operative Prozesse und Kundenkommunikation im Einklang mit internen Richtlinien, Regulatorik und Risikostandards stehen.

  • Den Country Manager bei Geschäftsentwicklung, Produkt-/Kanal-/Kampagneneinführungen und Business Cases unterstützen.

Dein Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium (Betriebswirtschaft, Finanzwesen, Volkswirtschaft o. ä.); MBA von Vorteil.

  • Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch (C1/C2) und gutes Englisch.

  • Erfahrung in Koordination/Optimierung von Vertriebs-, Service- oder Backoffice-Prozessen, idealerweise in einem Finanzinstitut; umfassendes Verständnis von Vertriebs-Pipelines und Kundenserviceprozessen.

  • Systematische, analytische Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägter Entscheidungsfähigkeit und Umsetzungsstärke.

  • Überzeugende Kommunikations- und Präsentationsfähigkeiten, mündlich wie schriftlich.

  • Fähigkeit, Beziehungen aufzubauen, effektiv zusammenzuarbeiten und Konflikte zu lösen.

  • Sicherer Umgang mit CRM-/Kreditvergabesystemen, BI-Tools und Excel.

  • Erfahrung im deutschen Banken-/Konsumentenkreditgeschäft von Vorteil.

  • Kundenorientierte, proaktive und lösungsorientierte Grundhaltung, mit hohem Verantwortungsbewusstsein und Liebe zum Detail.

  • Teamorientiert, aber auch fähig zum eigenständigen Arbeiten; sicher im Umgang mit mehreren Prioritäten in einem dynamischen Umfeld.

Was wir bieten

  • Ein dynamisches und unterstützendes Arbeitsumfeld, in dem du von erfahrenen Kolleginnen und Kollegen lernst.

  • Praxisnahe Erfahrung durch anspruchsvolle Projekte in einem modernen, schnelllebigen Unternehmen.

  • Vielfältige Lern- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten für deine berufliche Weiterentwicklung.

  • Einblicke in Banking- und Rechtsthemen in einem internationalen Umfeld.

  • Karrierechancen in einer schnell wachsenden Bank.

title: QA Lead – Core Ledgers
description:
We are looking for an experienced QA Lead to own quality assurance for our Core Ledger platform. Our team does not develop the core ledger systems ourselves — they are built and delivered by external suppliers. Your role is to set the test strategy, verify and regression test what our suppliers deliver, and make sure nothing goes live unless it meets our requirements. Since the systems involved are financial, precision and thoroughness really matter — you will be the last line of defence before a delivery is approved.

This is a newly established, hands-on position within a small team. In this initial phase, the role will not carry direct reports; instead, the focus will be on personally executing tests, developing the test strategy, and leading verification and regression testing efforts in close collaboration with the wider team.


Key Responsibilities

  • Define and own the overall test strategy for the Core Ledger domain, covering functional, regression, integration, and acceptance testing of supplier deliveries

  • Personally build and maintain test plans, test cases, and (where applicable) automated regression suites

  • Work with the Head of Core Ledger IT, Core Ledger Specialists, and Product Manager to embed quality from requirements through to delivery approval

  • Verify and regression test ledger transaction flows, balance calculations, and reconciliation logic delivered by suppliers

  • Drive a quality mindset across requirements writing, ensuring testability is considered from the start

  • Investigate and triage defects, coordinating resolution with suppliers and internal stakeholders

  • Define and track quality metrics; report on test coverage, defect trends, and delivery readiness

  • Participate in go/no-go decisions for supplier deliveries based on quality criteria

  • Support internal teams with questions about test results, known issues, and quality status

  • Contribute to risk assessments for major platform changes or new product introductions

Qualifications and Experience

Desired Skills

  • 5+ years in QA roles, with experience leading test strategy and acceptance testing for complex systems

  • Strong experience with test automation tools and frameworks (e.g. Selenium, Cypress, RestAssured, Playwright)

  • Deep knowledge of testing methodologies — functional, regression, integration, and acceptance testing

  • Experience verifying and accepting deliveries from external suppliers or vendors

  • Solid understanding of how to structure test plans and test cases against detailed requirements

Nice to Have

  • Experience testing financial systems — ledgers, payment flows, reconciliation, or core banking

  • Knowledge of performance and load testing tools (e.g. Gatling, k6, JMeter)

  • Experience working with vendor or supplier delivery models

  • Experience in a regulated financial or payments environment

Behaviours

  • Quality champion — passionate about getting things right, not just fast

  • Detail-oriented and methodical, especially in high-stakes financial contexts

  • Strong communicator who can articulate quality risks to both technical and business audiences

  • Proactive and self-driven — able to identify gaps and take initiative to address them independently

  • Collaborative, working closely with a small team and external suppliers

Education
Bsc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field. An ISTQB certification (or similar) is a plus.

Languages
Fluent English is required for this role. Spanish and Swedish language is a plus.

We Offer

  • A key role in building and shaping a modern, cloud-native core ledger platform

  • A collaborative, cross-functional team environment with short decision paths

  • Competitive salary and benefits package

  • Flexible working arrangements with hybrid options

  • Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning

  • A chance to make a real impact in the fintech space across the Nordic market

  • Modern office in central Málaga with a strong team culture

title: Senior Technical Program Manager
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Technical Program Manager

Overview

As a Senior Technical Program Manager at Mastercard, you’ll bring your expertise with conceptualizing, coordinating and driving technology projects, and coaching Agile practices in high-performing, self-organizing development teams. We’re building a global B2B, microservices-based platform to help businesses of all sizes streamline how they manage payments when buying or selling products & services. As a global business, the projects you lead for Mastercard will deliver software operating at massive scale requiring a focus on performance, security, and reliability.

This role will support our Network Solutions team within Payment Networks, assisting the development team with building out software solutions for Mastercard.

Role:
• Dive as deep as you want into the tech stack, the integration patterns, the organizational capabilities, and the company wide assets that can be leveraged to provide technical solutions to customer problems.
• Contribute to the strategies, design choices, and even the cloud infrastructure necessary to build comprehensive and achievable execution plans to deliver high-profile new features and capabilities for our customers.
• Drive the execution of an initiative that may span multiple teams and integrations, reporting meaningful status along the way.
• Coach your teams on self-organization and working effectively cross-functionally.
• Remove impediments and escalate obstacles for your teams.
• Coordinate with the product owner on priorities to maximize business value.
• Facilitate team events including grooming, planning, demo and retrospective meetings.

All About You:
• You operate with independence and autonomy.
• You have experience defining and building cloud-native services at scale with a deep understanding of the service’s capabilities and underlying technologies.
• You have experience breaking down problems, organizing work, planning sprints, and delivering technical programs using Agile delivery models (Scrum, Kanban, etc.)
• You have a track record of data driven decision-making and applying continuous improvement methodologies across teams.
• You can debate the delivery strategy with business teams, architects, engineers, and designers.
• You can communicate to executives, peers, and staff with impact, eloquence, and authenticity.
• You have experience building relationships, partnering with and influencing teams while commanding the respect of the individuals you work with across the organization.
• You have in-depth experience in SAFe or Scrum@Scale practices and have worked in large-scale team environments.
• You thrive in an Agile environment focused on continuous improvement.
• You are passionate about the art of delivering the highest quality software to customers and doing the right thing.
• Broad understanding of Software Engineering Concepts/Methodologies
• Huge plus if you have expertise in Payments, IaaS, PaaS, CI/CD, Microservice APIs, DevOps and Agentic AI

Education:
•Bachelor's degree in information systems, Information Technology, Computer Science or Engineering or equivalent work experience

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

O'Fallon, Missouri: $96,000 - $163,000 USD

title: Profesor/a de Educación Física
description:
Cada vez son más los estudiantes que buscan apoyo para mejorar su rendimiento en Educación Física, preparar pruebas físicas o desarrollar hábitos de vida saludables con la ayuda de un profesor particular.

title: Instructor, Prelicensure Clinical
description:
If you’re passionate about building a better future for individuals, communities, and our country—and you’re committed to working hard to play your part in building that future—consider WGU as the next step in your career.

Driven by a mission to expand access to higher education through online, competency-based degree programs, WGU is also committed to being a great place to work for a diverse workforce of student-focused professionals. The university has pioneered a new way to learn in the 21st century, one that has received praise from academic, industry, government, and media leaders. Whatever your role, working for WGU gives you a part to play in helping students graduate, creating a better tomorrow for themselves and their families.

The salary range for this position takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs.

At WGU, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their position, and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is:

Grade: Faculty 507

Pay Range: $38.79 - $58.17

Job Description

**THIS IS AN ON-SITE POSITION WITHIN THE ORLANDO, DAYTONA, CENTRAL FL REGION**

Prelicensure Clinical Instructors (Hourly) are subject matter experts who teach and mentor students when engaged in experiential clinical course work; in a remote/onsite environment. This faculty position varies in the type, length, and intensity of instructional methods and is based upon student and facility needs. The Clinical Instructor works on a team to support an assigned group of students and offers specialized instruction on challenging experiential/clinical course work. Faculty interacts with students in both group and one-on-one settings. The Clinical Instructor ensures students are successful within their clinical placement setting as they oversee clinical learning.

Compensation Expectations:

This role is paid hourly and bi-weekly. The employee will be required to enter all hours worked in an accurate and timely manner.

Essential Functions and Responsibilities:

Act as a steward for carrying out WGU’s mission and strategic vision. This faculty must:

  • Demonstrate a high level of commitment, effectiveness, and consistency.

  • Serves as a professional role model for students in communication, ethical comportment, and problem-solving.

  • Provides expertise in an assigned content area and maintains current knowledge in their field.

  • Demonstrates an understanding of, and participates in, the implementation of the philosophy and goals of the program.

  • Fosters students learning through innovative teaching practices; to include how to perform duties in the clinical setting.

  • Responds with urgency to meet student needs and communicates professionally and respectfully with students and all other members of the WGU community.

  • Makes appropriate student referrals for remediation or other needs, utilizing WGU policies and procedures.

  • Communicates with the student's assigned coach/preceptor as needed to discuss student progress and concerns.

  • Provide final review and approval for student's clinical time.

  • Offers proactive support and outreach to assigned students.

  • Uses technology-based teaching and communication platforms to aid students in the development of competencies.

  • Submits electronic clinical student evaluation form(s) after completion of clinical intensive.

  • Collaborates with other professionals within the university to promote a positive, student-obsessed atmosphere.

  • Responsible for responding with urgency to changing requirements, priorities, and short deadlines.

  • Maintains a working knowledge and understanding of relevant state regulations.

  • Conducts both in-person and/remote methods of student evaluation

Knowledge, Skill and Abilities:

  • Must be able to work well under stress and meet deadlines.

  • Capacity for self-motivation and working independently.

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

  • Aptitude for learning innovative technology.

  • Must comply with site specific orientation and onboarding requirements.

  • Problem Solving Skills: Investigates appropriate resources and involves partners when appropriate; future-oriented; assesses what will help/hinder achieving goals; focuses on what is important.

  • Interpersonal Skills: Deals with others in a considerate, respectful, and unbiased manner; approaches conflict proactively; solicits and shares feedback openly; listens with empathy and maintains composure.

  • Accuracy: Draws on information garnered from careful cultivation of a broad knowledge of WGU’s goals and processes; investigates beyond assumptions.

  • Adaptability: Course corrects behaviors and communication style to meet the needs of a wide range of situations; tackles obstacles appropriately; is comfortable with ambiguity.

  • Reliability: Accountable to others; does what it takes to get the job done; actions are consistent with words; follows through on commitments; exhibits exceptional integrity.

  • Attitude: Displays commitment to the organization and to personal growth; self-motivated and able to motivate and inspire others; asserts self appropriately to champion ideas; tells the truth in a direct and constructive manner.

  • Initiative: Self-directs with a strong bias for action; leads the way in improvement of performance or processes; displays keen interest in students and organization.

Competencies:

Organizational or Student Impact:

  • Accountable for decisions that impact individual students.

  • Facilitates learning experiences that support student's attainment of knowledge and skills.

  • Acts independently in executing teaching practice with specific guidance from senior faculty and program leaders.

Problem Solving & Decision Making:

  • Works on diverse matters of various levels of complexity.

  • Receives general direction from their immediate supervisor or manager.

  • Able to effectively utilize resources to address student concerns and inquiries.

  • Supports students' needs to help them achieve course or program outcomes at the individual student level.

  • Follows university and department established policies and best practices.

Communication & Influence:

  • Communicates with students as appropriate to support student questions and needs.

  • Communicates with fellow faculty members as appropriate within and outside of the department.

  • Expected to provide feedback regarding discipline and practice leadership.

Leadership & Talent Management

  • Considered a contributing and collegial team member.

  • Adheres to learning and operational quality guidance and instruction

  • Supports initiatives within specialty.

  • Displays a positive attitude toward change and supports change management practices

Job Qualifications:

Minimum Requirements:

  • Master's degree in nursing; Education is verified.

  • Two years minimum Nursing experience

  • Work history demonstrating continuous advancement and success in roles requiring creative problem solving and collaboration.

  • Unencumbered RN license

  • Active, unencumbered license in designated state

  • State and federal legislative requirements and college accreditation requirements may apply to maintain the necessary credentialing for this role.

  • Work history demonstrating continuous advancement and success in roles requiring creative problem solving and collaboration.

  • As needed, support the Health Placement Team to secure clinical partnerships.

Preferred:

  • Doctorate, or terminal degree in a specific content area.

  • Strong experience with distance education and distance learning students is preferred.

  • Terminal degree and all applicable licenses or certifications.

  • Prior experience in clinical teaching/nursing education.

  • , support the Health Placement Team to secure clinical partnerships.

Physical Requirements:

  • Prolonged periods sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

  • Must be able to lift up to 15 pounds at times.

  • Specific requirements of the job such as travel and the environment in which the job is performed.

Position & Application Details

Part-Time Intermittent Positions (classified as intermittent and less than 30 standard weekly hours): This is a part-time, intermittent position (classified to work less than 30 SWH) that may be eligible for mental healthcare; retirement savings plan; wellbeing program; and prorated paid holidays and accrued sick time.

How to Apply: If interested, an application will need to be submitted online. Internal WGU employees will need to apply through the internal job board in Workday.

Additional Information

Disclaimer: The job posting highlights the most critical responsibilities and requirements of the job. It’s not all-inclusive.

Accommodations: Applicants with disabilities who require assistance or accommodation during the application or interview process should contact our Talent Acquisition team at recruiting@wgu.edu.

Equal Employment Opportunity: All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any protected characteristic as required by law.

title: Lead Teacher for PreK Classroom - Full Time - Immediate Start
description:
Benefits:
  • 401(k)
  • Free uniforms
  • Health insurance
  • Training & development
About Us
Celebree School of Northbrook is a new childhood education center serving children from 6 weeks to 5 years old.
We are currently seeking an experienced and passionate lead teacher for our PreK room.
What You’ll Do
- Lead and manage a classroom in compliance with Illinois DCFS licensing requirements

If you’re passionate about childcare and ready to take a leadership role in a supportive school environment, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Dedicated Logistics - Business Development Manager
description:
🚀 Create Solutions. Win Customers. Grow Your Career.

Business Development Manager

💰 $75,000–$110,000 Annual Salary + Commissions

Sell solutions. Build partnerships. Make a lasting impact.

Are you a consultative sales professional who enjoys uncovering customer challenges, building customized transportation solutions, and winning long-term business?

At TCI Transportation, we're looking for a driven Business Development Manager to help expand our Dedicated Transportation, Leasing, Maintenance, and Logistics services. This isn't a transactional sales role—it's an opportunity to become a trusted advisor to customers while building a rewarding, high-income career.

If you enjoy opening doors, solving complex business problems, and turning conversations into long-term partnerships, we'd love to meet you.

🎯 What You'll Be Responsible For

📈 Generate New Business by identifying, prospecting, and developing relationships with new customers.

🤝 Build Trusted Partnerships by understanding each customer's operational challenges and delivering customized transportation solutions.

🚛 Sell TCI's Complete Portfolio including:

  • Dedicated Transportation

  • Fleet Leasing

  • Fleet Maintenance

  • Logistics Solutions

  • Transportation Services
