  • Impacto real: ayuda a tus estudiantes a comunicarse en un idioma clave y expandir sus horizontes culturales.

title: Solution Advisor
description:
About the role

We are seeking a dynamic and client-focused Solution Advisor/Presales to join our growing EMEA-based team. In this role, you will be instrumental in supporting sales efforts for the Truvio Finance Suite (consisting of ExFlow and SKsoft products) within the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (D365 F&O) ecosystem.

As a Solution Advisor, you’ll be at the heart of our growth and connecting client challenges with powerful solutions. You won’t just support sales, you’ll drive it.

Acting as a trusted advisor, you’ll:

  • Bring products within the Truvio Finance Suite (consisting of ExFlow and SKsoft products) to life through compelling demonstrations

  • Shape deals before they’re won

  • Influence stakeholders at all levels—including CFOs

  • Turn complex finance needs into clear, high-impact value stories

If you thrive on presenting, influencing, and solving real business problems, this role is for you.

What You’ll Do

  • Partner closely with sales to win new business

  • Deliver engaging, tailored product demonstrations in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations

  • Lead discovery workshops to uncover client needs

  • Craft compelling solution narratives and value propositions

  • Support RFPs, proposals, and technical responses

  • Build impactful demo environments tailored to industries

  • Provide high-level solution design guidance

  • Collaborate with product teams to shape future innovation

  • Ensure a smooth transition from sales to implementation

  • Work with the Customer Success organisation to support crafting adoption plans and explore opportunities in Truvio's installed base

What You Bring

  • Solid experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, especially financial modules

  • Background in presales, solution consulting, or client-facing roles

  • Strong understanding of finance, accounting, and treasury processes

  • Confidence presenting to senior stakeholders and executives

  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to simplify complexity

  • Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving abilities

  • Understanding of value selling; expert level articulation and fluency build up of Return on Invest Based Value Propositions

  • Fluency in French, with the ability to confidently engage with French-speaking clients and stakeholders and presentation ready English language

Who You Are

You’re more than just experienced; you’re driven, curious, and commercially minded.

You enjoy being in front of clients, confidently leading conversations and turning complex ideas into clear, compelling stories. You naturally build trust, ask the right questions, and know how to position solutions in a way that resonates with both business and technical audiences.

You thrive in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same. You take ownership, stay organized, and bring structure to ambiguity. Whether you’re preparing a demo, supporting a proposal, or engaging with senior stakeholders, you bring energy, clarity, and purpose to everything you do.

Other information:

Location: Europe, hybrid or remote

Employment type: Full-time, permanent

Travel: Up to 20%

About Truvio

At Truvio you will be part of a fast-growing modern SaaS environment who brings together established Microsoft Dynamics ISVs in one unified company, delivering a single, intelligent product portfolio built exclusively for Dynamics 365. This way we help customers and partners close the ERP Value Gap by unifying finance, operations, and commerce within a Microsoft-native suite.

We are a team of more than 450 employees worldwide, with colleagues based across North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia. With our core values Take ownership, Build Trust, Drive innovation and Succeed together, we have a mission to help organizations get more from their ERP investments.

title: Advanced Market Insurance Agents-High Level Strategy for Serious Professionals
description:
Location: Remote Position/ Work from Home
Job Type: Full-Time/Part-Time
Compensation: 100% Uncapped Commission-Based

About Us:

GIA Legacy Planning is a dynamic, client-focused insurance agency specializing in personalized insurance solutions that protect families and businesses. Our culture is rooted in integrity, mentorship, and continuous professional growth—we believe in building people, not just policies.

Join our team as an Advanced Market Insurance Agent and step into a fast-paced, data-driven industry where your performance and growth truly matter. You’ll have access to modern platforms designed to help you stay organized, streamline your workflow, and maximize your time management, all while enjoying the flexibility to control your own schedule and work from home.

Professional development here isn’t just offered—it’s actively encouraged. With ongoing training, mentorship, and growth opportunities, you’ll sharpen your skills, advance your career, and make a meaningful impact in people’s lives. If you’re motivated, adaptable, and ready to thrive in a high-performance environment, we’d love to connect with you.

Responsibilities:

  • Identify client needs and recommend tailored insurance solutions

  • Develop and maintain strong client relationships

  • Generate leads through networking, referrals, and company-provided resources

  • Educate clients on available policies and coverage options

  • Follow up with prospects to close sales and maintain customer satisfaction

  • Stay informed about industry trends and product offerings

  • Work independently while collaborating with a supportive team

What We Offer:

  • Competitive commission structure with potential for bonuses

  • Flexible work schedule (remote position)

  • Comprehensive training and mentorship programs

  • Access to top-rated insurance carriers and products

  • Opportunities for career growth and leadership development

  • Supportive team culture with ongoing coaching and professional development

Requirements:

  • Insurance license (or willingness to obtain one – we provide guidance)

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills

  • Self-motivated with a results-driven mindset

  • Ability to work independently and manage time effectively

  • Basic computer skills for CRM and client management

title: Partner Relationship Manager
description:
About Windcave

Founded in 1999, Windcave is a global payments technology company, pioneering Payments as a Service (PaaS) and cutting-edge financial services. Millions of transactions flow through our platform every day for some of the world's most recognizable brands across all industries.


About role:

Our Sydney office is looking for a highly committed Partner Relationship Manager, who thrives on business development to join our growing organisation. This is an exciting opportunity to carve new business relationships and drive business sales, utilising your sales experience in expanding our partnerships within the unattended, ecommerce, EFTPOS and merchant service channels.


Position Attributes:

  • Highly motivated and ambitious sales professional with a strong focus on new business acquisition
  • High-energy self-starter with a proven drive to exceed OTE targets through new business growth and partnerships
  • Proven ability to generate opportunities independently through personal networks, market outreach, and proactive business development, supported by Windcave’s internal resources
  • Comfortable building a self-generated sales pipeline while leveraging marketing, partner, and internal referral channels
  • Commercially minded sales professional capable of acting as a technical liaison for product deployment and integration aligned with customers’ payment requirements
  • Strong interest in learning and developing expertise within the payments technology sector
  • Enthusiastic about representing a high-growth, market-leading organisation
  • Demonstrates integrity, professionalism, and a strong motivation to achieve outstanding results


Role Requirements:

  • Dynamic personality with high energy and an entrepreneurial mindset
  • Ability to quickly learn and articulate new product and solution offerings
  • Comfortable operating in a varied role, including:
    • New business development
    • Partner relationship management
    • Customer engagement
    • Providing first-line assistance when required
  • Proactive in developing new customer relationships, including face-to-face meetings and on-site engagement
  • Willingness to attend industry events, networking opportunities, and trade shows to promote Windcave and identify new opportunities
  • Comfortable with proactive outreach activities, including cold calling and targeted prospecting, as part of a broader business development strategy
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Professional presentation and strong interpersonal skills
  • Collaborative team player with a desire to contribute to a positive, high-performing team culture
  • Strong presentation skills with experience delivering solution-based sales presentations
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools including:
    • Word
    • Excel
    • PowerPoint
  • Ability to clearly explain payments solutions to non-technical audiences
  • Awareness of the payments, retail, and technology landscape, including competitors and market trends
  • Understanding of merchant services providers highly desirable
  • Previous experience in a fast-paced telesales, or business development roles preferred

Benefits:

  • Hybrid work model (Mandatory 3 days' work from office Monday, Tuesday, and Friday).
  • Competitive remuneration package
  • Career Development and long-term career opportunity
  • Diverse, energetic and friendly team environment

Send us your CV if you are keen to explore your options with Windcave!


Windcave thanks all applicants in advance as only those shortlisted will be contacted.

title: MECHANICAL DESIGN ENGINEER TO ALFDEX
description:
Are you an experienced Mechanical Design Engineer looking for an opportunity to help shape the next generation of sustainable technology? At Alfdex, we are expanding our R&D organization and building our mechanical design capability around our electric drive systems. As part of this strategic investment, we are now recruiting two Mechanical Design Engineers who want to play a key role in developing products that contribute to a greener future.

WE OFFER

At Alfdex, you will work with cutting-edge technology in a dynamic and collaborative environment where innovation, sustainability and engineering excellence are at the heart of everything we do. As we continue our transition towards electrified products, these newly created positions offer a unique opportunity to influence both the role itself and the future development of our products.

You will become part of a highly skilled R&D organization where long-term product development, cross-functional collaboration and technical curiosity drive everyday work. With projects spanning several years, you will have the opportunity to follow products throughout the development lifecycle while working closely with colleagues in Landskrona and Stockholm, as well as suppliers and development partners internationally. We offer competitive benefits, a strong team culture and excellent opportunities for professional growth.

YOU WILL

As one of our new Mechanical Design Engineers, you will focus on the mechanical development of Alfdex' electric drive units - an essential part of our next-generation products combining mechanics, electronics and electric motors. You will play a central role in developing generic platform solutions while also supporting customer-specific projects and continuous product improvements. Your responsibilities will include:

  • Developing and maintaining mechanical designs, CAD models, drawings and BOMs for electric drive units.

  • Driving the mechanical development of Alfdex' generic drive unit platform together with internal teams and external development partners.

  • Collaborating closely with colleagues within mechanics, electronics and software to develop next-generation mechatronic products

  • Supporting customer projects, engineering changes and continuous product improvements throughout the product lifecycle.

  • Working closely with suppliers and manufacturing to ensure robust, manufacturable and cost-efficient designs.

  • Participating in technical investigations, design reviews, validation activities and continuous improvement initiatives.

  • Contributing to the long-term development of future drive unit concepts and technologies.


You will based at our HQ in Landskrona and Occasional travel may be required as part of this role.

YOU ARE

We are looking for experienced Mechanical Design Engineers who enjoy solving complex technical challenges and want to contribute to the development of innovative products in an international engineering environment. You are curious, communicative and motivated by working in a dynamic environment where you can influence both solutions and ways of working. You combine a structured and proactive approach with strong problem-solving skills, and you are comfortable working both independently and in close collaboration with cross-functional teams. We believe you have:

  • A university degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related technical discipline.

  • Several years of experience in mechanical design and product development.

  • Strong experience working with modern 3D CAD systems; experience from Creo is an advantage.

  • Experience working closely with manufacturing, suppliers and product development teams.

  • Professional proficiency in English is required, and Swedish is considered a strong plus.

Experience within mechatronics, electric motors, electronics packaging, automotive or other high-quality manufacturing industries is considered highly valuable, as is knowledge of verification, validation and Design for Manufacturing principles.

WANT TO KNOW MORE?

We are collaborating with Nexer Recruit for this recruitment. If you have any questions, please contact: Johanna Värmfors – johanna.varmfors@nexergroup.com, +46 730 821 230 Artan Bitiqi – artan.bitiqi@nexergroup.com, +46 723 61 28 44.

We look forward to hearing from you.

ABOUT ALFDEX

Alfdex is pushing technical boundaries by developing innovative solutions that target today’s urgent needs for sustainability. By thinking outside the box, we create opportunities for customers worldwide to reduce their environmental impact. Our innovative g-Technology is spearheading the journey into a greener future.

Alfdex was founded in 2002 as a joint venture by the two Swedish companies Alfa Laval and Haldex. In 2011 Concentric took over the Haldex holding of the Alfdex shares and the two global companies now share Alfdex AB 50/50. Both owners contribute to Alfdex’ success through extensive knowledge and experience within their fields.

Serving major customers all over the world, Alfdex has its headquarters in Landskrona in the southwest of Sweden. Here you will find our production, administration and logistics teams. An additional production site is situated in Kunshan, China and soon Indianapolis, USA. Our Research & Development and design departments are located in Stockholm and Landskrona. Alfdex benefits from the extensive network of its mother companies to support customers in all parts of the world in a timely and efficient manner.

title: Community Support Worker
description:
Mental Health & Disability Support Workers

Wollongong, NSW | Morning & Evening Shifts | Ongoing


We are seeking experienced Mental Health & Disability Support Workers to support participants with complex mental health needs across the Wollongong region.

This is a high-priority recruitment due to changes in the existing workforce.

Applicants must have availability across at least 3–4 days per week and be able to commit to a minimum of 10–15 hours per week.


About the Role


You will be supporting participants with complex mental health conditions, including:

  • Schizophrenia
  • Schizoaffective Disorder
  • PTSD
  • Co-occurring alcohol and drug use
  • Psychosocial disability and complex behavioural needs

Support will involve working in dynamic environments where participants may experience distress, emotional dysregulation, or periods of mental health deterioration.


Key Responsibilities

  • Provide person-centred mental health support in line with individual care plans
  • Supervise and prompt medication administration in accordance with protocols
  • Implement Positive Behaviour Support Plans (BSPs), including proactive and reactive strategies
  • Use de-escalation techniques, low-arousal communication, and emotional regulation skills during periods of distress
  • Support participants experiencing symptoms such as paranoia, delusions, or disorganised thinking while maintaining safety
  • Work confidently in environments where substance use may be present, applying harm-minimisation approaches and professional boundaries
  • Recognise early warning signs and follow escalation pathways, including contacting emergency services when required
  • Maintain accurate, objective documentation including case notes, incident reports, and handovers
  • Communicate effectively with the wider support team and management
  • Respect participant choice and control, including the right to refuse support
  • Assistance with personal care and manual handling when required
  • Support with hygiene, cleaning, and bedding
  • Assist with daily living tasks to promote independence


Essential Requirements

  • Demonstrated experience supporting individuals with complex mental health needs, ABI, and/or behavioural challenges
  • Strong emotional regulation skills and calm, professional communication style
  • Experience implementing Behaviour Support Plans and following safety protocols
  • Ability to work independently and within a team in high-pressure environments
  • NDIS Worker Screening Check (or willingness to obtain)
  • First Aid & CPR (or willingness to obtain)
  • Valid Driver’s License and reliable vehicle (mandatory)
  • Availability across a minimum of 3–4 days per week

title: Manager, Industry Standards
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Manager, Industry Standards

Overview

Mastercard is developing the standards that will define trust in the next generation of digital commerce. As blockchain and digital assets evolve, industry-wide standards are critical to ensuring security, interoperability, and scalable adoption across ecosystems.
The Industry Standards team is responsible for shaping, driving, and operationalizing global standards that underpin Mastercard’s role in secure digital transactions. This includes active leadership in emerging blockchain and digital asset standards bodies, ensuring Mastercard influences the baseline frameworks that the ecosystem adopts.
We are seeking a Manager, Industry Standards to help drive Mastercard’s strategy and engagement in blockchain, cryptocurrency, and digital asset standards translating emerging standards into practical, adoptable frameworks that support ecosystem trust and commercial scalability.

Role

In this role, you will define and execute Mastercard’s approach to blockchain and digital asset standards. You will represent Mastercard across key industry forums, shape the direction of emerging standards, and ensure alignment with Mastercard’s broader security, product, and regulatory objectives.

You will focus on driving real industry impact, translating standards into usable constructs such as assurance models, certifications, and integration frameworks that enable adoption at scale.

Responsibilities
• Support Mastercard’s engagement with global blockchain and digital asset standards bodies (e.g., BSSC, ISO, ANSI), influencing the development of security and operational standards
• Define Mastercard positions on emerging topics such as blockchain security, digital asset controls, and interoperability frameworks
• Help lead the operationalization of blockchain security standards into measurable controls, conformance criteria, assessment methodologies, certification programs, and audit frameworks that support ecosystem-wide adoption.
• Partner across internal teams (Security Solutions, Blockchain & Digital Assets, Legal, Product) to align standards strategy to business priorities
• Help define approaches for security assurance, independent assessment, conformance validation, and trust signaling for blockchain ecosystems.
• Build and maintain relationships with industry peers, regulators, and ecosystem partners shaping blockchain standards
• Support thought leadership and market engagement to drive awareness and adoption of standards (e.g., industry forums, publications, webinars)

Experiences
• Expertise in cybersecurity, payments, or digital asset ecosystems, with strong understanding of blockchain technologies and risk models
• Ability to translate complex technical standards into practical, business-aligned outcomes
• Demonstrated ability to operate in emerging, ambiguous domains and shape strategic direction
• Comfortable navigating both technical and policy discussions across global forums
• Familiarity with blockchain security disciplines such as key management, smart contract security, node operations, custody, digital asset infrastructure, or related domains.
• Experience participating in standards development organizations, industry consortia, regulatory initiatives, or technical working groups.
• Working knowledge of security and control frameworks such as NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI, cryptographic assurance programs, or equivalent.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Tonttivastaava
description:
Tonttivastaava

The Nooa Company on vakavarainen, voimakkaasti kasvava, uuden sukupolven asuntokehittäjä ja kiinteistöalan konserni, joka on juuri palkittu Suomen kovimpana kasvuyhtiönä.

The Nooa Company on kasvanut nopeasti Suomen kiinnostavimmaksi asuntokehittäjäksi. Olemme kunnianhimoinen tiimi, joka rakentaa kauniita ja kohtuuhintaisia koteja ihmisen ympärille.

Haemme nyt tonttivastaavaa vakituiseen työsuhteeseen!

Rooli

Tonttivastaavana löydät ja hankit tontit, joille Nooan seuraavat hankkeet rakennetaan ja kehität Nooan tonttiportfoliota.

Työ on aktiivista ihmisten kanssa toimimista, missä kontaktoit kiinteistönomistajia ja neuvottelet tonttikaupat.

Työ sopii sinulle, kun olet tavoitteellinen, viihdyt ihmisten parissa, tartut asioihin oma-aloitteisesti, haluat nähdä konkreettisia tuloksia omasta tekemisestäsi ja nautit kaupanteosta.

Nooalla tehdään työtä pienessä ja tiiviissä tiimissä. Päätökset tehdään nopeasti, vastuu on aitoa ja oman työn tulokset näkyviä. Jos haluat rakentaa uraa kasvavassa kiinteistöalan yhtiössä ja olla mukana määrittämässä, mitä Nooa seuraavaksi rakentaa, hae meille töihin.

Ominaisuudet

Haemme motivoitunutta, reipasta ja tavoitteellista tekijää. Jos sinulla on kokemusta ja näyttöjä onnistumisista aktiivisesta myynnistä, pärjäät varmasti tässäkin tehtävässä. Jos sinulla ei ole kokemusta vastaavanlaisesta työstä, mutta olet aktiivinen ja halukas kehityymään, tulet oppimaan kaiken tarvittavan meidän kyydissä.

Tarjoamme

Nuorekkaan ja dynaamisen työskentely-ympäristön kasvuyhtiössä sekä kilpailukykyisen palkan ja hyvät kannustin- ja bonusmahdollisuudet.

Hae nyt

Lähetä CV, motivaatiokirje ja palkkatoiveesi mahdollisimman pian. Täytämme paikan heti sopivan henkilön löydyttyä. Huom! Käsittelemme vain Teamtailorin kautta jätetyt hakemukset.

Lisätietoja antaa:

Jeremia Similä

044 085 3297

jeremia.simila@nooacompany.fi

title: Global Roaming Coordinator Executive
description:

Why you should join Telenor Shared Services Pakistan

At Telenor Shared Services Pakistan, you get the opportunity to grow your expertise in an inclusive and supportive environment. You will collaborate with international teams, develop professionally, and contribute to meaningful global services.

Telenor Shared Services Pakistan delivers transactional business services in Finance & Accounting and IT. Together with Telenor Shared Services Norway, we support Telenor employees globally and make their work easier and more efficient.

The practicalities

Application deadline: 31 July 2026

title: Patient Care Technician - PCT
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Functions as part of the dialysis health care team in providing safe and effective dialysis therapy for patients under the direct supervision of a licensed nurse in accordance with organization policies, procedures, and training and in compliance with regulations set forth by the corporation, state, and federal agencies. Responsible for the setup and operation of hemodialysis machines. Assist in the maintenance of a safe and clean working environment. Supports the organization commitment to the Quality Enhancement Program (QEP) and CQI Activities, including those related to patient satisfaction and actively participates in process improvement activities that enhance the likelihood that patients will achieve the organization Quality Enhancement Goals (QEP).

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Patient Related

Education:

  • Assist other health care members in providing patient specific detailed education regarding adequacy measures where appropriate - Online Clearance Monitoring (OLC), Adequacy Monitoring Program (AMP), Urea Kinetic Modeling (UKM), and regarding disease process/access.

Treatment:

  • Welcome assigned patients and inquire as to their wellbeing since their last treatment.

  • Evaluate vascular access for patency, perform vascular access cannulation, and perform administration of heparin as delegated or as allowed by state law.

  • Obtain necessary pre and post treatment vital signs and weight and perform vascular access evaluation pre- treatment

  • Initiate dialysis treatment according to prescribed orders including blood flow (QB) and dialysate flow (QD).

  • Evaluate intradialytic problems and provide intervention as prescribed by physician order or as directed.

  • Monitor patients’ response to dialysis therapy.

  • Obtain vital signs prior to reinfusion; perform all relevant functions necessary for the discontinuation of treatment - document.

  • Discontinue dialysis treatment according to established procedures and evaluate patient prior to termination of venous access - standing & sitting blood pressure

  • Obtain Hemostasis and apply appropriate dressings.

  • Evaluate the patient prior to discharge.

  • Perform and record Pre and Post dialysis evaluation, weight, and vital signs with initial identification

  • Responsible for calculating and entry of individual patients’ dialysis machine programming for Ultrafiltration (UF) goal; treatment time; and UF modeling as prescribed.

  • Responsible for ensuring appropriate safety alarms are enabled, including Narrow Venous Limits, (NVL) 160.

  • Record accurate and timely information regarding vital signs and treatment parameters onto the Hemodialysis Treatment Sheet with initial identification

  • Responsible for accurate documentation of information related to patient treatment including completion of Hemodialysis Treatment Sheet and, if applicable, progress notes.

  • Report any significant information and/or change in patient condition directly to the registered nurse or supervisor.

  • Observe patient and conduct machine safety checks according to facility policy. Report any change or unusual findings to the registered nurse or supervisor.

  • Perform and document any intervention for unusual patient status and document patients' response to intervention.

  • Evaluate patients’ vascular access during treatment including arterial and venous monitoring pressures, provide appropriate intervention as needed, document and report any unusual findings to the registered nurse or supervisor.

  • Obtain all prescribed laboratory testing and prepare specimens for collection.

  • Ensure that all blood spills are immediately cleaned with appropriate disinfectant according to facility policy.

  • Prepare, organize, and efficiently utilize supplies and equipment to prevent wastage.

  • Monitors patients performing self-care under the supervision of RN.

Staff Related

  • Assists in training of applicable direct patient care staff on appropriate programs under the supervision of a nurse.

Education/Communication:

  • Follows all organization business policies, procedures and systems incorporated into training, including compliance with ethical business practices

  • Maintain environmental integrity and aesthetics - ensure all areas are safe and clean.

  • Clean and disinfect dialysis machine surface, chair, equipment, and surrounding area between treatments according to facility policy.

  • Conduct all tasks necessary for preparation for dialysis treatment and document where appropriate and perform all required pretreatment dialysis machine alarm testing including Pressure Holding (PHT).

  • Initiate Solution Delivery System (SDS) system.

RECORD KEEPING:

  • Complete and document ongoing participation in Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) activities.

  • Enters all treatment data into the designated clinical application in an accurate and timely manner.

  • Review treatment sheets for completeness, ensure nursing signatures are documented, and ensure

omitted entries are completed or corrected by appropriate staff.

  • Prepare initial patient chart for admission and compile paperwork for appropriate placement in chart.

  • Prepare/print lab requisitions for prescribed or stat Lab specimens according to laboratory destination.

  • Collect, label, appropriately prepare and store lab samples according required laboratory specifications.

  • Ensure collection of lab specimens by appropriate lab courier.

INFECTION CONTROL:

  • Assists in collecting information for infection control audits.

  • Supports staff and patient Adherence to infection control practices.

  • Follows infection control Policies and Procedures

  • Participates in and reinforces infection control education of patients and families under the direction of the CM or designee as allowed by state law.

TRANSITIONAL CARE UNIT:

  • Follows all newly admitted patients through their first 4 weeks of dialysis and coordinating their transitional dialysis care

  • Reinforces all education and care related matters as it relates to the new patient as allowed by state law

  • Liaisons with appropriate FKC staff members to provide the best patient experience including making sure all disciplines are scheduled to see patient according to plan.

  • Assists in assuring patient understanding or home dialysis products, benefits of home and how home dialysis can work for them.

  • Sets and preforms a treatment on all machines used in the transitional care unit.

  • Assist in assuring patient understanding of treatment options including demonstration of the peritoneal dialysis set-up.

  • Assists with coordination of transition to patient’s modality choice.

  • Assists with supply ordering and inventory for TCU

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee

encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable

individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Employees are required to take the Ishihara's Color Blindness test as a condition of employment. Note that: Failing the

Ishihara Test for Color Blindness does not preclude employment. The Company will consider whether reasonable

accommodations can be made.

  • Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, facility staff, and physicians. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, Business Unit and Corporate meetings may be required.

  • The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials

  • The position provides direct patient care that regularly involves heavy lifting and moving of patients and assisting with ambulation. Equipment aids and/or coworkers may provide assistance. This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over. The employee may occasionally be required to push and/or pull equipment, exerting up to 15 pounds of force. The employee may be required to lift and to lower solutions on a frequent basis of up to 30 lbs., and on an occasional lift basis up to 40 lbs., as high as 5 feet. There is a two-person assist program and "material assist” devices for the heavier items.

EDUCATION AND LICENSES:

  • High School diploma or G.E.D. required.

  • Must meet Center for Medicaid/Medicare Services (CMS)-approved state and/or national certification requirements within the required state or CMS timeline.

  • All appropriate state licensure, education, and training (if any) required.

  • Demonstrated commitment to organization culture, values, and customer service standards

EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

  • Previous patient care experience in a hospital setting or a related facility preferred.

  • Continued employment is dependent on successful completion of the organization dialysis training program and successful completion of CPR certification.

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Lead Store Advisor, On Hannam Store
description:
In short

This is your opportunity to become a core part of On’s Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) Store Retail team and assist in the successful launch and ongoing operations of On’s first store in Korea.

As a Lead Store Advisor, your passion for delivering exceptional customer service in a fast-paced environment while maintaining a positive work environment for our team will be a core part of driving On’s Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) approach. On will rely on you to deliver exceptional customer service to each and every customer while embodying On’s core values and passion for movement.

Your mission

- Continuously lead and communicate through On's values and brand philosophy in team and customer interactions

- Foundational cash handling expectations and standardized operating procedures – example; responsible for all cash transactions and opening and closing of tills, point of sale systems etc.

- Opening and closing store procedures and ensuring loss and prevention standards are followed

- Maintain in-depth knowledge of technical product information and stay up to date with On’s global activities and projects (e.g. sustainability efforts, athlete stories)

- Coach the team to ensure excellent standard of all technical product knowledge

- Contribute and streamline feedback to continuously improve store processes, customer engagement and team happiness in line with our company culture of radical candor

- Maintain the visual standards in the store to deliver a premium experience and optimize sell through strategy

- Lead and maintain exceptional stock room strategy and inventory management standards

- Create an inclusive workplace culture by treating all employees and customers with respect.

The Pillars: You will oversee a designated store area, of the following (Back of House, Visual Merchandising, Product, Community, or People), driving strategy implementation, monitoring execution and fostering team engagement to ensure success in the focus area.

Your story

- 3+ years of experience within a consumer-facing or retail work environment, preferably in a supervisory role

- Background in the Running, Fashion or Sports Industry is preferred, but not a must

- Strong communication, hospitality, and interpersonal skills

- Ability to take initiative, learn quickly, and work both independently and as part of a team

- Eye for maintaining outstanding store condition and visual merchandising standards

- Experience with Microsoft Dynamics and / or other ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning system) is preferred, but not required.

- Entrepreneurial spirit who expects the unexpected

Physical Demands: This position involves constant moving, talking, hearing, reaching, grabbing, climbing stairs, and standing for consecutive hours. May occasionally involve stooping, kneeling, and crouching.

Meet the team

As you might expect from a young consumer brand, the DTC Retail team at On is a fast-paced place to be. Together with your store team and leadership as well as the global team, you will help to revolutionize conventional retail business and deliver the WOW to our customers on a daily basis. This team will work out of our brand new Seoul locations.

What we offer

On is a place that is centered around growth and progress. We offer an environment designed to give people the tools to develop holistically – to stay active, to learn, explore and innovate. Our distinctive approach combines a supportive, team-oriented atmosphere, with access to personal self-care for both physical and mental well-being, so each person is led by purpose.

title: Transition Assistance Advisor
description:
Overview

Nakupuna Solutions seeks an Transition Assistance Advisor. The Office of Program Transition and Supply Chain (PTSC) leads the Department of State’s health systems transition planning on behalf of the Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy (GHSD). In this capacity, PTSC develops and oversees the execution of bilateral transition MOUs and country implementation plans, establishing requirements, standards, targets, and timelines, and monitoring progress against those plans. PTSC also serves as GHSD’s center of excellence for private-sector engagement, private-finance mobilization, and economic analysis. In addition, the office oversees the global supply chain contract, ensuring the safe, timely, and effective delivery of HIV, malaria, and TB commodities to more than 70 countries on a regular and ongoing basis.

Responsibilities

The Program Transition team aims to strengthen the effectiveness, efficiency, and sustainability of U.S. Government–supported health programs by advancing health systems integration and strategic health financing. The Senior Advisor will lead development and implementation of frameworks, metrics, and financing approaches that support partner governments in assuming increasing ownership of health programs in a responsible and enduring manner. The position also aims to elevate health as a foreign policy priority by providing high-level technical and policy advice to Department and Embassy leadership, informing bilateral health MOUs, and shaping global health policies and partnerships with multilateral institutions, partner governments, and the private sector.

  • Devise frameworks that promote more effective and efficient use of clinical and financial resources across health program areas during the process of health systems integration
  • Apply experiential background (including from work as a physician and health financing specialist at multilateral development banks) to provide novel insights about donor-supported health programming, helping to ensure that USG partner countries take on increasing levels of ownership for health programs in an efficient and enduring manner
  • Engage with private sector and pharmaceutical industry to improve health outcomes
  • Provide expert advice and recommendations to GHSD leadership on health financing mechanisms (e.g., co-investment policy, G2G mechanisms, results-based financing, etc.) that will be necessary to increase partner governments' levels of self-reliance
  • Serve as a technical expert contributing to the development, coordination, and dissemination of new or revised policies related to integration and more efficient use of clinical resources and human resources for health
  • Counsel and advise senior management on program transition strategies to employ based on a comprehensive knowledge of country and regional contexts, including their financial landscapes, public health statistics, and public health programs

Deliverables:

  • Prepare technical analyses and options papers on health financing mechanisms (co-investment, G2G, results-based financing, contingency/emergency instruments) for internal decision-makers
  • Design and update frameworks, models, or tools that improve efficiency and integration of clinical and financial resources across health program areas
  • Produce country- and region-specific financing profiles and recommendations to support increased partner government self-reliance
  • Develop and maintain a set of indicators and metrics to track transition of health program responsibility from donors to partner governments
  • Produce monitoring reports summarizing trends, risks, and progress toward country ownership and sustainability
  • Draft or co-author USG and interagency policy papers, strategy documents, and guidance related to health systems integration, health financing, and donor-to-country transition
  • Prepare policy briefs, talking points, and decision memos to support Department and Embassy leadership in elevating health as a foreign policy priority
  • Contribute to internal knowledge platforms by posting relevant analyses, tools, and lessons learned

Qualifications

Skills/Qualifications:

  • Advanced knowledge of health financing, health systems strengthening, and health systems integration
  • Strong understanding of global health security, health assistance modalities, and donor-to-country transition of health programs
  • Broad knowledge of health commodities, including diagnostics, therapeutics, and other emerging technologies
  • Demonstrated ability to design and apply frameworks that improve efficiency and effectiveness of clinical and financial resource use across health program areas
  • Deep experience working in international development and global health, including extended engagement with multilateral development institutions
  • Ability to translate multilateral development bank practices and instruments into practical guidance for USG health programming
  • Experience engaging with partner governments, donors, and other stakeholders on health systems, financing, and policy issues
  • Ability to provide strategic direction on health systems integration and resource efficiency.

Education and Experience:

  • MD and MPH degrees, or equivalent advanced training in medicine and public health
  • Minimum of 7 years of experience in international development and/or global health
  • At least 5 years of experience working with a multilateral development institution (e.g., World Bank)
  • Demonstrated background in clinical medicine, public health, health systems, health economics/financing, and monitoring and evaluation

Clearance Requirements: Secret Clearance required. Must be U.S. citizen.

Physical Requirements: The ideal candidate must at a minimum be able to meet the following physical requirements of the job with or without a reasonable accommodation:

  • Ability to perform repetitive motions with the hands, wrists, and fingers.
  • Ability to engage in and follow audible communications in emergency situations.
  • Ability to sit for prolonged periods at a desk and working on a computer.

The Nakupuna Companies use a market-based compensation strategy to ensure that our employees are compensated within applicable market ranges commensurate with multiple factors, including but not limited to the individual’s particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability, organizational requirements, and position location. The projected compensation for this position is $140,500.00 to $165,500.00 (annualized USD). The salary range displayed represents the typical range for this position and is just one component of Nakupuna Companies total compensation package for employees.

title: Patient Care Technician - PCT CCHT - Dialysis
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Functions as part of the dialysis health care team in providing safe and effective dialysis therapy for patients under the direct supervision of a licensed nurse in accordance with organization policies, procedures, and training and in compliance with regulations set forth by the corporation, state, and federal agencies. Responsible for the setup and operation of hemodialysis machines. Assist in the maintenance of a safe and clean working environment. Supports the organization commitment to the Quality Enhancement Program (QEP) and CQI Activities, including those related to patient satisfaction and actively participates in process improvement activities that enhance the likelihood that patients will achieve the organization Quality Enhancement Goals (QEP).

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Patient Related

Education:

  • Assist other health care members in providing patient specific detailed education regarding adequacy measures where appropriate - Online Clearance Monitoring (OLC), Adequacy Monitoring Program (AMP), Urea Kinetic Modeling (UKM), and regarding disease process/access.

Treatment:

  • Welcome assigned patients and inquire as to their wellbeing since their last treatment.
  • Evaluate vascular access for patency, perform vascular access cannulation, and perform administration of heparin as delegated or as allowed by state law.
  • Obtain necessary pre and post treatment vital signs and weight and perform vascular access evaluation pre- treatment
  • Initiate dialysis treatment according to prescribed orders including blood flow (QB) and dialysate flow (QD).
  • Evaluate intradialytic problems and provide intervention as prescribed by physician order or as directed.
  • Monitor patients’ response to dialysis therapy.
  • Obtain vital signs prior to reinfusion; perform all relevant functions necessary for the discontinuation of treatment - document.
  • Discontinue dialysis treatment according to established procedures and evaluate patient prior to termination of venous access - standing & sitting blood pressure
  • Obtain Hemostasis and apply appropriate dressings.
  • Evaluate the patient prior to discharge.
  • Perform and record Pre and Post dialysis evaluation, weight, and vital signs with initial identification
  • Responsible for calculating and entry of individual patients’ dialysis machine programming for Ultrafiltration (UF) goal; treatment time; and UF modeling as prescribed.
  • Responsible for ensuring appropriate safety alarms are enabled, including Narrow Venous Limits, (NVL) 160.
  • Record accurate and timely information regarding vital signs and treatment parameters onto the Hemodialysis Treatment Sheet with initial identification
  • Responsible for accurate documentation of information related to patient treatment including completion of Hemodialysis Treatment Sheet and, if applicable, progress notes.
  • Report any significant information and/or change in patient condition directly to the registered nurse or supervisor.
  • Observe patient and conduct machine safety checks according to facility policy. Report any change or unusual findings to the registered nurse or supervisor.
  • Perform and document any intervention for unusual patient status and document patients' response to intervention.
  • Evaluate patients’ vascular access during treatment including arterial and venous monitoring pressures, provide appropriate intervention as needed, document and report any unusual findings to the registered nurse or supervisor.
  • Obtain all prescribed laboratory testing and prepare specimens for collection.
  • Ensure that all blood spills are immediately cleaned with appropriate disinfectant according to facility policy.
  • Prepare, organize, and efficiently utilize supplies and equipment to prevent wastage.
  • Monitors patients performing self-care under the supervision of RN.

Staff Related

  • Assists in training of applicable direct patient care staff on appropriate programs under the supervision of a nurse.

Education/Communication:

  • Follows all organization business policies, procedures and systems incorporated into training, including compliance with ethical business practices

  • Maintain environmental integrity and aesthetics - ensure all areas are safe and clean.
  • Clean and disinfect dialysis machine surface, chair, equipment, and surrounding area between treatments according to facility policy.
  • Conduct all tasks necessary for preparation for dialysis treatment and document where appropriate and perform all required pretreatment dialysis machine alarm testing including Pressure Holding (PHT).
  • Initiate Solution Delivery System (SDS) system.

RECORD KEEPING:

  • Complete and document ongoing participation in Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) activities.
  • Enters all treatment data into the designated clinical application in an accurate and timely manner.
  • Review treatment sheets for completeness, ensure nursing signatures are documented, and ensure

omitted entries are completed or corrected by appropriate staff.

  • Prepare initial patient chart for admission and compile paperwork for appropriate placement in chart.
  • Prepare/print lab requisitions for prescribed or stat Lab specimens according to laboratory destination.
  • Collect, label, appropriately prepare and store lab samples according required laboratory specifications.
  • Ensure collection of lab specimens by appropriate lab courier.

INFECTION CONTROL:

  • Assists in collecting information for infection control audits.
  • Supports staff and patient Adherence to infection control practices.
  • Follows infection control Policies and Procedures
  • Participates in and reinforces infection control education of patients and families under the direction of the CM or designee as allowed by state law.

TRANSITIONAL CARE UNIT:

  • Follows all newly admitted patients through their first 4 weeks of dialysis and coordinating their transitional dialysis care
  • Reinforces all education and care related matters as it relates to the new patient as allowed by state law
  • Liaisons with appropriate FKC staff members to provide the best patient experience including making sure all disciplines are scheduled to see patient according to plan.
  • Assists in assuring patient understanding or home dialysis products, benefits of home and how home dialysis can work for them.
  • Sets and preforms a treatment on all machines used in the transitional care unit.
  • Assist in assuring patient understanding of treatment options including demonstration of the peritoneal dialysis set-up.
  • Assists with coordination of transition to patient’s modality choice.
  • Assists with supply ordering and inventory for TCU

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee

encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable

individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Employees are required to take the Ishihara's Color Blindness test as a condition of employment. Note that: Failing the

Ishihara Test for Color Blindness does not preclude employment. The Company will consider whether reasonable

accommodations can be made.

  • Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, facility staff, and physicians. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, Business Unit and Corporate meetings may be required.
  • The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials
  • The position provides direct patient care that regularly involves heavy lifting and moving of patients and assisting with ambulation. Equipment aids and/or coworkers may provide assistance. This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over. The employee may occasionally be required to push and/or pull equipment, exerting up to 15 pounds of force. The employee may be required to lift and to lower solutions on a frequent basis of up to 30 lbs., and on an occasional lift basis up to 40 lbs., as high as 5 feet. There is a two-person assist program and "material assist” devices for the heavier items.

EDUCATION AND LICENSES:

  • High School diploma or G.E.D. required.
  • Must meet Center for Medicaid/Medicare Services (CMS)-approved state and/or national certification requirements within the required state or CMS timeline.
  • All appropriate state licensure, education, and training (if any) required.
  • Demonstrated commitment to organization culture, values, and customer service standards

EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

  • Previous patient care experience in a hospital setting or a related facility preferred.
  • Continued employment is dependent on successful completion of the organization dialysis training program and successful completion of CPR certification.

The rate of pay for this position will depend on the successful candidate’s work location and qualifications, including relevant education, work experience, skills, and competencies.

Hourly Rate: $25.00 - $30.00

Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave.
Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Sales Advisor
description:
About TOTEME

TOTEME is a fashion house rooted in Swedish sensibilities, modern aspirations and the notion of style. With an appreciation for women’s many roles and how pieces are worn in practice, we create emblematic designs with an emphasis on materiality and shape. Representing a direct and decisive way of dressing, the collections are contextualized in curated edits, visuals and spaces.

TOTEME was founded in 2014 by Elin Kling and Karl Lindman. From the atelier in Stockholm, we create collections spanning ready-to-wear, shoes, bags, accessories and jewelry.

The role

TOTEME is looking for a Sales Advisor to join our store team at Le Bon Marché in Paris full-time (35 hours/week). In this role you will act as a brand ambassador, providing excellent service and building long-term relationships with our customers and driving business in all aspects, from sales to brand building. You will report to the Store Manager.

Responsibilities

  • Build and nurture strong client relationships to drive loyalty, engagement, and an elevated shopping experience.

  • Work both independently and collaboratively to support daily operations and achieve sales targets.

  • Engage in merchandising and styling to maintain a visually compelling store environment.

  • Plan and execute daily store operations.

  • Work towards company standards and goals.

  • Participate in and contribute to TOTEME’s social and environmental sustainability strategy.

Your profile

  • Ideally at least 2 years of previous experience in retail, with high-end service with a strong lifestyle brand.

  • Proven success in delivering outstanding customer service, clienteling, and exceeding sales goals.

  • Strong understanding of fashion, styling, and brand storytelling.

  • Fluent in English and French (both written and spoken); additional languages are a plus.

About you

  • A dedicated TOTEME ambassador who embodies our culture and values.

  • Genuinely curious about people and motivated to understand our client base.

  • Passionate about delivering a luxury experience and exceeding client expectations.

  • Driven and results-oriented with a proactive and positive mindset.

  • Highly organized and structured with an eye for detail.

  • Available to work a flexible schedule, including evenings and weekends.

This is a full-time position based at TOTEME’s store in Le Bon Marché, Paris, starting as soon as possible.

TOTEME has teams in Stockholm, London, New York, and Shanghai, as well as retail spaces across Europe, North America, and Asia.

TOTEME has an internal career development and title framework and the job title used in the recruitment ad may differ from the title used internally at TOTEME.

Candidates progress through application, screening, interviews or case assessments, followed by final pre-employment checks and a formal offer before joining our team. We anonymize names and personal details in applications during the screening stage to support fair and skills-based hiring.

By applying to this position, I agree with TOTEME’s privacy policy and to be contacted about future job opportunities for up to two years from the submission date. Find out more about our privacy policy here.

title: School Meal Cover
description:
A little bit about us

Looking for a job that actually makes a difference - and gives you something new every day?

At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help kids build healthy habits for life.

We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are!

So, what’s the job?

We’re on the hunt for School Meals Cover Staff - reliable, adaptable people who can jump into action at short notice and keep things running like clockwork.

This isn’t your typical 9 - 5. You’ll be our on-the-ground hero, stepping into different schools as needed (when our staff in the schools are absent), bringing the smiles, and making sure no child goes hungry. If you’re looking for routine and predictability, this might not be for you. But if you like variety, enjoy being out and about, this could be your perfect fit - keeping you on your toes and feeling young!

Just like our regular school meals workers, you will be the smiley face representing us in the school. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the schools. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven and then distributing them to classrooms.

If there’s no need for staff cover on a given day, you’ll head out to schools to carry out simple (but effective) audits. Our goal is always to provide the best possible service to children - and that includes staying on top of hygiene standards and following all HACCP and EHO guidelines.

If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you:

  • Go into a new work environment each day with enthusiasm and confidence.

  • Load pre-packaged school meals into ovens.

  • Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines.

  • Unload ovens and distribute lunches to classrooms.

  • Ensure adequate stock levels.

  • Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule.

  • Maintain temperature and cleaning records.

  • Restock the fruit bowls in each classroom.

  • Distribute snacks to classrooms.

  • Conduct audits in schools to ensure compliance with EHO requirements.

  • HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided.

What we’re looking for

Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers - people who are sound, good-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal.

You’ll need to be a quick learner, great with people, and happy to go with the flow.

A full driving licence and your own transport are essential - some schools might be just around the corner, others could be a bit of a spin. But that’s part of the fun - you’ll get to know every back road and coffee spot in your area!

We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every school in Ireland has a lift (just yet)!

Working with children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly.

If you’ve worked with kids or food before, great. If not, don’t let it put you off - we value people from all backgrounds and we’ll show you the ropes.

All you need is the ability to read English, follow instructions, and heat each lunch with care.

I still have questions actually…

No problem! We’re here to answer them.

  • When will I work?

You will need to be available Monday to Friday from 7 am to 3.30pm. Your precise hours per day will depend on the school’s requirements.

  • Will I have to work all year long?

No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well-deserved rest!

  • What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag?

Well, for starters - a decent rate of pay and a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back.

The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms.

And best of all? You get to be part of a lovely school environment, surrounded by friendly faces (and the occasional tiny comedian in a uniform).

  • How much is it paid?

It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn €580 per week, and we’ll also cover your expenses for using your own car

title: School meal worker (Hot Drop) - St Patricks GAA
description:
A little bit about us

Looking for a job that actually makes a difference - and fits into your day-to-day routine?

At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help children build healthy habits for life.

We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are!

So, what’s the job?

We’re on the hunt for School Lunch Operatives – reliable, cheerful people who take pride in doing things right and bring a bit of warmth to every school day.

Think of yourself as the final link in the lunch chain. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the Hot Drop Locations. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven, loading them into vans and driving them to the schools.

If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you:

  • Load pre-packaged school meals into ovens.

  • Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines.

  • Unload ovens and load into vans.

  • Drive the lunches in a company vehicle to the local schools.

  • Ensure adequate stock levels.

  • Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule.

  • Maintain temperature and cleaning records.

  • HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided.

What we’re looking for

Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers - people who are sound, good-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal.

We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every facility in Ireland has a lift

Working for children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly.

All you need is the ability to read English, have full clean Irish driving license, and heat each lunch with care.

We’re looking for cheerful people who like being out and about. Teleportation would make life easier, but until that arrives, we need drivers who are happy to take a roughly one-hour trip to deliver meals to local schools.

I still have questions actually…

No problem! We’re here to answer them.

When will I work?

This job is offered on a part-time basis, 5 hours a day, Monday through Friday! Typical hours of work are 08:00 to 13:00 with some flexibility required as start and finish times may be a little earlier or later.

Will I have to work all year long?

No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well-deserved rest! Our staff sign on to Social Welfare as Educational Sector Workers during the summer months.

What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag?

Well, for starters - a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back.

The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms.

How much is it paid?

It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn €14.65 per hour – 50c above minimum wage.
