title: (수원)임베디드 SW 개발자
description:
About

[ 글로벌 1등 체외진단 기업을 위한 지속적인 기술혁신 ]

에스디바이오센서는 인류의 건강과 행복에 이바지하기 위해 혁신적인 기술력과 노하우가 접목된 체외진단 제품을 공급하는 글로벌 바이오 진단 기업입니다.

2020년 우리는 코로나 바이러스로 전례 없는 힘든 시기를 보냈습니다. 에스디바이오센서는 전 세계 코로나 바이러스 방역에 이바지하고자 다양한 진단 제품 개발에 집중하였고, 빠른 시간 내에 신종 코로나바이러스 유전자 공개 이후 템플릿 합성, 재조합 항원 개발에 성공하였습니다. 이후 STANDARD M nCoV Real-time PCR 제품 질병관리본부 긴급사용 승인, STANDARD Q COVID-19 IgM/IgG 제품 개발, 인플루엔자와 동시에 진단할 수 있는 COVID/Flu 제품 등 다양한 코로나바이러스 진단 제품 개발에 성공하였으며, 현재까지도 끊임없는 제품 개선 및 개발 활동을 통해 가파른 성장을 하고 있습니다.

에스디바이오센서는 구성원과 함께 성장하는 기업입니다.

1. 업계 상위의 보상 수준
2. 업무에만 집중할 수 있도록 지원하는 복리후생
3. 도전하는 만큼 주어지는 기회와 권한
4. 수익이 나면 열매를 함께 공유하는 경영진의 철학
5. 젊은 인재들이 모인 수평적 조직

우리 팀을 소개할게요!
임베디드 소프트웨어 개발자는 기기개발본부에 속해 있으며, 연구소 석박사 인원들과 함께 체외진단 의료기기 개발 연구를 진행하고 있습니다. 수평적인 문화 속에서 구성원 간의 자극을 통해 함께 성장하기를 원하시는 분에게 매우 좋은 기회입니다.

Team Talk (동료의 한 마디)
임베디드 소프트웨어 개발자는 에스디바이오센서의 메인 업무로서 연구소 비즈니스의 성장을 만드는 역할을 합니다. 빠르게 성장하는 회사와 함께 서로에게 자극이 되면서 성장하고자 하는 우수한 동료 분을 기다립니다.

에스디바이오센서에서 함께 글로벌 1등 체외진단 기업을 만들어 나갈 당신을 기다립니다.

Responsibilities

(임베디드) 소프트웨어S/W 개발자는 git을 통한 형상관리, Qt를 사용한 GUI 개발을 통한 임베디드 보드 Application 개발 및 유지보수를 수행합니다.

• 자사 제품 체외진단 의료기기 M10, F200 등 S/W 신규 개발 및 유지보수
• 체외진단 의료기기 인허가 문서 작성

Requirements

• C/C++ 언어를 사용한 개발 경험
• Git, SVN 등 형상관리 경험
• 개발자 인허가 문서 작성 경험
• 학사 이상 소프트웨어, 컴퓨터공학 등 관련 전공자

Preferred

• 제약 바이오 업계에 대한 폭넓은 이해가 있는 분
• QT 기반 크로스 플랫폼 임베디드 장비 개발 경험
• 통신에 대한 개념(Serial 및 TCP/IP 기반 장비 통신 등) 이해

Benefits

복리후생
• 복지포인트 연120만원 지급
• 식사 제공
• 하계 휴가 지원
• 회사 콘도 지원

Education 교육
• 어학 교육 지원
• 직무 교육 지원
• 임직원 도서 구매 지원

title: Student Social Work Clinical Placement
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:
The student intern supports FMCNA's mission, vision, core values and customer service philosophy. Adheres to the FMCNA Compliance Program, including following all regulatory and FMS policy requirements.
The student intern functions as part of the hemodialysis health care team in providing safe and effective dialysis therapy for patients under the direct supervision of a licensed social worker in accordance with FMCNA policies, procedures, and training and in compliance with regulations set forth by the corporation, state, and federal agencies. Assist in the maintenance of a safe and clean working environment. Supports the FMCNA commitment to the Quality Enhancement Program (QEP) and CQI Activities, including those related to patient satisfaction and actively participates in process improvement activities that enhance the likelihood that patients will achieve the FMC Quality Enhancement Goals (QEP).
DUTIES / ACTIVITIES:
An integral part of Social Work education that promotes students' identification with the purposes, values and ethics of the profession; fosters the integration of empirical and practice-based knowledge; and promotes the development of professional competence (CSWE Educational Policy and Accreditation Standards). A minimum number of required field education hours are established by the CSWE.
The social worker student/intern may under direct guidance of a Licensed Social Worker:
CUSTOMER SERVICES:
  • Responsible for driving the FMS culture through values and customer services standards.
  • Accountable for outstanding customer service to all external and internal customers.
  • Develops and maintains effective relationships through effective and timely communication.
  • Takes initiative and action to respond, resolve, and follow up regarding customer services issues with all customers in a timely manner.
PRINCIPLE RESPONSIBILITIES AND DUTIES RELATED TO PATIENT CARE:
Patient Assessment/Care Planning Counseling:
  • As a member of the interdisciplinary team, assesses patients' psychosocial status, strengths and areas of need that may affect rehabilitation and optimal treatment outcomes as part of the comprehensive patient assessment.
  • Participates in care planning in collaboration with the patient and healthcare team to identify effective interventions that will help the patient meet rehabilitation, treatment goals, and improve quality of life.
  • Utilizes FMS Ultra Care patient education programs, established social work theory and methods and quality of life measurement instruments as part of assessment and care planning to resolve barriers and meet patient treatment goals.
  • Provides established outcomes driven psycho-educational counseling methods aimed at improving areas of need, treatment adherence, vocational/educational rehabilitation and/or quality of life.
  • Provides supportive and goal directed counseling to patients who are seeking transplant.
  • Assesses patient awareness of advance directives; assists with accessing advance directive forms/information and facilitates discussion of advance directive wishes, if necessary, with the healthcare team and the patient's family/support persons.
  • Will provide general information about Do Not Resuscitate Orders and Advanced Directives.
  • Provides information and assists the team and patient with referral to community resources (home health services, vocational rehabilitation, etc.) to facilitate optimal treatment outcomes.
  • Provides usual care and/or Social Work Intensive to address non-adherence and quality of life concerns for all patients.
  • Knowledgeable of and adheres to FMCNA Social Work Policy, including documentation.
Patient Education:
  • Assess patient knowledge of kidney disease for barriers that may affect adherence to treatment. Works with patient, family and health care team to provide education tailored to the patient's learning style and needs.
  • With other members of the interdisciplinary team, provides appropriate information about all treatment modalities. Facilitates the transplant referral process and collaborates with interdisciplinary team, if delegated by Clinical Manager.
  • Provides ongoing education to patient/family regarding psychosocial issues related to End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) and all support services that are available.
  • Reviewed patient rights and responsibilities, grievance information (company and network) and other facilities policies with patient and/or the patients' representative to ensure patients' understanding of the rights and expectations of them.
  • Collaborates with the team on appropriate QAI activities.
Insurance and Financial Assistance:
  • Maintains current knowledge regarding local vocational/educational rehabilitation programs and assist patients with referral and access to vocational rehabilitation to enable them to remain employed, become employed or receive education.
  • Understands FMCNA financial policies and procedures as they related to patient services. Collaborate with Financial Coordinators to educate patients about programs that may be available to assist with uncovered services, how to resolve billing concerns, and understand financial responsibilities.
Staff Related:
  • Works with the administrative support staff to maintain updated patient resource lists (e.g. maintain updated list of transportation resources).
  • Develops and provides training to staff pertaining to psychosocial topics as needed. Trains staff in Ultra Care Programs as needed.
  • Other duties as assigned.
PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:
The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
Day to day work includes desk and personal computer work and interaction with patients, facility staff and physicians. The position requires travel between assigned facilities and various locations within the community. Travel to regional, Business Unit and Corporate meetings may be required.
The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels. May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials.
EDUCATION:
High School diploma or G.E.D.
EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:
Preferred outstanding customer service to all external and internal customers. Possess the desire to deliver superior care to our patients and embrace Fresenius' mission and values.
RELATIONSHIPS:
The Intern cannot work under the supervision of a family member.
The Intern cannot care for a patient who is a family member. If an Intern's family member is a patient at the facility in which they work, the Intern will be scheduled to work on days the patient is not in the facility or will be assigned to another patient.
SUPERVISION:
The Clinical Manager assigns and closely monitors all SW Intern assignments with direct supervision from a SWFI based on the agreed education plan.
OTHER:
Performs additional duties as assigned.

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Strategic Accounts Manager, Data Centers - US Robotics
location: Remote, California, United States of America
description:
At ABB, we help industries outrun - leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation - for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world.

This role sits within ABB's Robotics business, a leading global robotics company. We're entering an exciting new chapter as we’ve announced the plan for SoftBank Group to acquire ABB Robotics. SoftBank is a globally recognized technology group and investor/operator focused on AI, robotics, and next-generation computing. By joining us now, you’ll be part of a pioneering team shaping the future of robotics—working alongside world-class experts in a fast-moving, innovation-driven environment.

This Position reports to:

Local Business Line Leader, Robotics Ind


What you'll do:

In this role, you will own a sub-segment of Strategic Accounts and drive profitable sales growth in the colocation data center space selling customizable robotics solutions to hyperscalers and other critical businesses.

In addition to developing and implementing the sales strategy for your accounts and coordinate all commercial activities to achieve customer satisfaction through delivering value and efficiently resolving issues you will also possess technical knowledge, solid sales and communication skills and exceptional industry savvy.

This role can be based in California, South Carolina, Arizona (major data center hubs) or in a metro area with data center presence, and in reasonable commutable distance to major airport for travel.

Key Responsibilities

  • Achieve consistent top-line sales growth with the assigned Strategic accounts.

  • Accountable for developing, maintaining and growing the sales pipeline.

  • Comfortable in front of the key decision makers at various levels and locations.

  • Lead a virtual team of Sales and Sales Support professionals across multiple states and regions.

  • Create, plan and implement detailed Account plans and achieve timely completion of objectives.

  • Develop player maps and implement a program to connect the key sponsors at both sides for building longer partnerships.

  • Drive early-stage engagement to influence design for automation friendly specifications.

  • Lead and manage multi-year and multi-location sales programs and projects.

  • Develop knowledge of the product portfolio and Create customer value propositions and portfolio offering (Robots, Accessories, Function Packages, Robotic Cells, Full Turn-key Systems from the Global Solution Centers and Customer Service teams.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree with 10+ years of commercial experience in Account Management and Key Account Management roles; strong preference will be given to candidates with knowledge and contacts with the data center automation space.

  • Solution selling, client engagement and industry awareness.

Desired Qualifications

  • Seasoned commercial campaigner in dealing with complex decision-making units (DMU’s) within large global customer organizations.

  • Connections with hyperscalers, proven B2B sales expertise; preferably in data centers or adjacent industry such as electric, energy, generators, etc.

  • Experience with customers, including sales, problem resolution and negotiation skills.

  • Willingness to travel up to 50% across the United States.

  • Candidate must be able to work in the United States without ABB support.

What's in it for you

We empower you to take the lead, share bold ideas, and shape real outcomes. You’ll grow through hands-on experience, mentorship, and learning that fits your goals. Here, your work doesn’t just matter, it moves things forward

ABB is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer for protected Veterans and Individuals with Disabilities at ABB.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their- sex (gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation), marital status, citizenship, age, race and ethnicity, inclusive of traits historically associated with race or ethnicity, including but not limited to hair texture and protective hairstyles, color, religious creed, national origin, pregnancy, physical or mental disability, genetic information, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal and state law.

For more information regarding your (EEO) rights as an applicant, please visit the following websites: https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf

EST Monday through Friday by contacting an ABB HR Representative at 1-888-694-7762.

Protected Veterans and Individuals with Disabilities may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access ABB's career site as a result of your disability. title: Procurement & Sourcing - Software, VP
description:
Who we are looking for

The VP of Software Sourcing is an important role within State Street’s Global Procurement Team and responsible for the preparation, analysis, negotiation and finalization of the end-to-end Procurement process for software and cloud engagements. This sourcing manager will support/be a part of the global, regional and local category sourcing strategies working with our global IT vendors.

Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining plays an important role in the overall success of the organization. Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. To make that happen we need people like you to help navigate employees and the organization as a whole. In your role you will strive for cutting-edge solutions, that are straightforward and scalable. You will help us build resilience and execute day to day deliverables at our best.

Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for.

What you will be responsible for

Based in State Street’s John Adams Building in Quincy, Boston, this Sourcing Manager / Category SME – Software and Cloud you will

  • Perform direct negotiations and manage large and complex agreements
  • Find and deliver saving opportunities via stakeholder engagement and data analysis
  • Draft and negotiation Master License and Cloud agreements with support from Legal
  • Negotiate IT security schedules with support from IT Security and Legal SMEs
  • Develop stakeholder relationships on a face to face basis across our Boston locations using a white glove approach as needed with senior stakeholders(Quincy)
  • Develop vendor relationship with key Infrastructure, Cyber Security and other critical vendors where stakeholders are based in the Quincy office.
  • Identify risks and opportunities related to achieving financial objectives; work with process partners to mitigate risks and realize opportunities
  • Heavily contribute to P&L and Cost Avoidance savings for the team
  • Analyze data to identify opportunities to reduce existing cost base
  • Coordinate, prepare and manage RFx/competitive bids to optimize cost and service
  • Perform all duties in accordance with prescribed regulatory compliance guidelines
  • Quickly learn the State Street end to end process, and work within the guidelines to gain saving and delivery results

What we value

These skills will help you succeed in this role

  • A number of years (10+) strategic sourcing experience in IT related spend is a must with referenceable knowledge in cloud software
  • A strong understanding of all facets of global IT vendors
  • Proven track record of delivering P&L saving projects
  • Should have strong commercial skills and be able to deliver cost effective outcomes
  • Top level negotiation skills with ability to influence cross-functionally
  • Understanding of best in class procurement, risk, Information Security and payable processes
  • Proven self-starter who can work independently and within a team while effectively juggling multiple priorities
  • Strong strategic thinker and problem solver with attention to detail
  • Personal resilience to work within an extremely fast-paced, ambiguous environment
  • Established written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to manage challenging stakeholders diplomatically
  • Good presentation skills and a keen eye for detail a must

Salary Range:

$110,000 - $181,250 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers

Read our CEO Statement

title: Vice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Vice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)

Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)

Who is Mastercard?
Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships, and innovation, we help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture of inclusion and respect. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.

Overview

As Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance), you will lead Mastercard’s enterprise-wide value quantification program – scaling and unifying our current Services Value Quantification (SVQ) into a holistic Mastercard Value Quantification (MVQ) capability across Core, Commercial & New Payment Flows (CNPF), and Services. Reporting to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, you will own the program’s strategy, governance, and execution. Your mission is to embed a value-first culture and ensure that quantifying customer value is at the heart of how Mastercard designs, sells, and delivers its solutions. You will establish a scalable, persona-based MVQ framework and platform to provide tailored value narratives and ROI insights. This role serves as a critical enterprise connector – collaborating across Product, Sales, Pricing, Customer Success, Finance, Technology, and regional teams – to drive broad adoption and tangible impact of MVQ globally. (This position reports to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, within Mastercard’s Services organization.)

Key Responsibilities
• Enterprise MVQ Vision & Leadership: Define and champion the enterprise MVQ vision and roadmap. Lead the evolution of SVQ into one unified MVQ program that quantifies and communicates the value of Mastercard’s solutions across all lines of business.
• Program Management & Governance: Establish and chair an MVQ governance framework that aligns cross-functional priorities, standardizes methodologies, and eliminates duplicative value quantification efforts. Implement a disciplined operating model with clear priorities and accountability.
• Holistic Value Story & Scalable Platform: Develop Mastercard’s cohesive value story with persona-specific value propositions and ROI narratives for key audiences (e.g., account teams, product teams, pricing, executives). Oversee MVQ platform enhancements to deliver these insights at scale, enabling users to generate tailored, customer-ready value analyses.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including Product, Sales (Account Management Customer Solutions Center, Services Business Leads), Pricing, Customer Success, and others – to embed MVQ into product, pricing, sales, and customer engagement processes, driving a unified, value-focused approach across the customer lifecycle.
• Adoption & Enablement: Drive enterprise adoption of MVQ and practices. Develop training, communications, and support programs to integrate MVQ into the daily workflows of client-facing teams. Use feedback and metrics to continually improve the platform and value delivery.
• Platform & Data Stewardship: Guide the ongoing development of the MVQ platform, working with Technology and Analytics partners to integrate new data sources, product value models, and analytical capabilities. Ensure the platform is robust, intuitive, and compliant with Mastercard’s standards.
• People Leadership: Build and lead a high-performing MVQ team. Provide mentorship, coaching, and clear direction to develop talent and expertise. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and results-driven culture that embraces a value-first mindset and grows the next generation of value experts.

Key Skills & Qualifications
• Extensive experience: 15+ years in strategy, program management, consulting, or related roles leading large-scale, cross-functional initiatives in complex organizations (payments, financial services, or B2B technology sector preferred).
• Strategic & analytical acumen: Proven strength in strategic thinking, financial analysis, and data-driven problem solving. Adept at developing ROI models and translating analytics into actionable business insights.
• Value & commercial expertise: Familiarity with value-based selling, pricing strategy, and customer value management. Ability to articulate and quantify the business value of technology or service solutions to customers and internal stakeholders.
• Leadership & influence: Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence senior executives and diverse stakeholders. Skilled at driving alignment in a matrixed environment through vision and collaboration.
• Change management: Demonstrated success in driving adoption of new tools, processes, or cultural initiatives at scale. Passion for evangelizing innovation, with a track record of leading organizational change and embedding new practices.
• People development: Strong people management capabilities. Experience building and mentoring high-performing teams, developing talent, and fostering an inclusive, high-energy culture.
Leadership Attributes
• Visionary & enterprise-minded: Strategic thinker who sees the big picture and can articulate a compelling, forward-looking vision for value quantification as a competitive differentiator.
• Collaborative influencer: Excels at leading through influence and building consensus. Cultivates trust and teamwork across functions, geographies, and levels to achieve shared goals.
• Results-focused & accountable: Driven to deliver tangible results. Sets high standards, measures progress and takes ownership to ensure execution and business impact.
• Innovative & adaptable: Embraces change with a continuous improvement mindset. Stays ahead of industry trends (e.g., data analytics, AI) to evolve capabilities and encourage creative solutions.
• Authentic & inclusive leader: Exemplifies Mastercard’s values and Decency Quotient (DQ). Creates a culture of integrity, inclusion, and empowerment. Inspires and develops others through mentorship and leading by example.

Success Measures
• Widespread Adoption: MVQ is widely utilized across Mastercard’s businesses, with strong engagement from product, sales, account management, and customer success teams globally.
• Measurable Impact: MVQ drives tangible commercial outcomes – influencing key deal wins, supporting value-based pricing and renewals, and helping demonstrate ROI to customers – resulting in improved client satisfaction, retention, and revenue growth.
• Executive Alignment: MVQ is championed by senior leadership as a critical enterprise capability. Its insights are regularly incorporated into strategic planning, business reviews, and go-to-market decisions, reinforcing Mastercard’s value-driven culture and competitive differentiation.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: EE Intern
description:

title: Master Social Worker - MSW
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Provides psychosocial services to patients treated by the facility including in-center and home dialysis patients (if applicable) utilizing Social Work Theory of Human Behavior and accepted methods of social work practice. Works with the health care team to promote positive adjustment, rehabilitation and improved quality of life for our patients. In collaboration with the interdisciplinary team, informs, educates and supports staff in understanding the emotional, psychological and behavioral impact of Chronic Kidney Disease on the patient and family to ensure comprehensive quality care of our patients. Supports the Fresenius Kidney Care (FKC) commitment to the Quality Indicators and Outcomes and Quality Assessment and Improvement (QAI) Activities, including those related to patient satisfaction and quality of life and actively participates in process improvement activities that enhance the likelihood that patients will achieve the FKC Quality Goals. This is an entry level MSW role.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Patient Assessment / Care Planning / Counseling
    • As a member of the interdisciplinary team, assesses patients' psychosocial status, strengths and areas of need that may affect rehabilitation and optimal treatment outcomes as part of the comprehensive patient assessment.
    • Participates in care planning in collaboration with the patient and healthcare team to identify effective interventions that will help the patient meet rehabilitation, treatment goals, and improve quality of life.
    • Utilizes FKC patient education programs, established social work theory and methods, social work focused interventions, and quality of life measurement instruments as part of assessment and care planning to address barriers and meet patient treatment goals.
    • Provides monitoring and interventions for the patient to adjust to dialysis and achieve optimal psychosocial status and quality of life.
    • Provides supportive counseling services to patients as permitted within the scope of their clinical training and state license.
    • Provides educational and goal directed counseling to patients who are seeking transplant.
    • Provides information and assists the team and patient with referral to community resources (home health services, vocational rehabilitation, etc.) to facilitate optimal treatment outcomes.
    • Maintains current knowledge regarding local vocational/educational rehabilitation programs and assist patients with referral and access to vocational rehabilitation to enable them to remain employed, become employed or receive education.
    • Assesses patient awareness of advance directives; assists with accessing advance directive forms/information and facilitates discussion of advance directive wishes, if necessary, with the healthcare team and the patient's family/support persons.
    • In collaboration with the physician and nurse, participates in the discussion of patient DNR status in the facility to ensure patient and/or family understand and make an informed decision about their care.
    • Knowledgeable of and adheres to FMCNA Social Work Policy and Measuring Patient Physical and Mental Function Policy, including documentation.
    • Documents based on MSW interaction and interventions provided to patient and/or family.
  • Quality
    • Provides psychosocial support and/or Social Work Focused Interventions to address non-adherence, quality outcome, and quality of life concerns for all patients based on acuity level.
    • Participates in monthly Quality review meetings with the interdisciplinary team. Reports on quality indicators related to adherence, such as Missed and Shortened Treatments, Quality of Life Trends, and Service Recovery.
  • Patient Education
    • Assesses patient knowledge of kidney disease for barriers that may affect adherence to treatment. Works with patient, family and health care team to provide education tailored to the patient’s learning style, communication barriers, and needs.
    • With other members of the interdisciplinary team, provides appropriate information about all treatment modalities.
    • Facilitates the transplant referral process and collaborates with interdisciplinary team on transplant waitlist management.
    • Provides ongoing education to patient/family regarding psychosocial issues related to End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) and all support services that are available.
    • Reviews patient rights and responsibilities, grievance information (company and network) and other facilities policies with patient and/or the patients’ representative to ensure patients’ understanding of the rights and expectations of them.
    • Collaborates with the team on appropriate QAI activities.
  • Patient Admission and Continuity of Care
    • Reviews Patient Rights and Responsibilities, Grievance Procedure & Important Numbers Handout, FKC Non-discrimination policy, DNR Statement (if applicable) and address any immediate needs/concerns.
    • Understands the referral and admission process and supports the clinic in regard to the patient needs for scheduling to maximize adherence and adjustment.
    • The Social Worker will interview the patient to identify root causes or concerns for the discharge request, (i.e. transfer to hospice, relocation, dissatisfaction with services or staff) and share causes/concerns with operational leadership.
  • Insurance and Financial Assistance
    • Collaborates and functions as a liaison for patient with Insurance Coordinators to address issues related to insurance.
    • In collaboration with Insurance Coordinators, provides information and education to patients about payment to dialysis (federal, state, commercial insurance, state renal programs, AKF HIPP, and entitlement programs).
    • Collaborates with the Insurance Coordinator of any changes to patient state that impacts insurance i.e. transplantation, discharged, loss of coverage, or extended travel.
    • Refers patients to patient billing solutions (PBS) department for questions/concerns in regard to treatment related bills
  • Staff Related
    • Assists with interview process and decision to hire new personnel if requested by SW Manager/Senior Manager.
    • Works with the administrative support staff to maintain updated patient resource lists (e.g. maintain updated list of transportation resources).
    • Provides training to staff pertaining to psychosocial topics as needed.
    • Contributes and participates with weekly team huddles. Discusses any urgent patient issues with staff.
  • Adheres to work defined caseload guidelines based on state regulatory requirements.
  • Performs other related duties as assigned.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  • Travel required (if multiple facilities or home visits, if applicable)

SUPERVISION:

  • None

EDUCATION AND REQUIRED CREDENTIALS:

  • Masters in Social Work
  • Must have state required license
  • Meets the applicable scope of practice board and licensure requirements in effect in the State in which they are employed

EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS:

  • 0 – 2 years’ related experience

The rate of pay for this position will depend on the successful candidate’s work location and qualifications, including relevant education, work experience, skills, and competencies.

Annual Rate: $60,000.00 - $101,000.00

Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Master Social Work - MSW
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

Provides psychosocial services to patients treated by the facility including in-center and home dialysis patients (if applicable) utilizing Social Work Theory of Human Behavior and accepted methods of social work practice. Works with the health care team to promote positive adjustment, rehabilitation and improved quality of life for our patients. In collaboration with the interdisciplinary team, informs, educates and supports staff in understanding the emotional, psychological and behavioral impact of Chronic Kidney Disease on the patient and family to ensure comprehensive quality care of our patients. Supports the FMCNA commitment to the Quality Indicators and Outcomes and Quality Assessment and Improvement (QAI) Activities, including those related to patient satisfaction and quality of life and actively participates in process improvement activities that enhance the likelihood that patients will achieve the FMCNA Quality Goals.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Patient Assessment / Care Planning / Counseling
    • As a member of the interdisciplinary team, assesses patients' psychosocial status, strengths and areas of need that may affect rehabilitation and optimal treatment outcomes as part of the comprehensive patient assessment.
    • Participates in care planning in collaboration with the patient and healthcare team to identify effective interventions that will help the patient meet rehabilitation, treatment goals, and improve quality of life.
    • Utilizes FKC patient education programs, established social work theory and methods, social work focused interventions, and quality of life measurement instruments as part of assessment and care planning to address barriers and meet patient treatment goals.
    • Provides monitoring and interventions for the patient to adjust to dialysis and achieve optimal psychosocial status and quality of life.
    • Provides supportive counseling services to patients as permitted within the scope of their clinical training and state license.
    • Provides educational and goal directed counseling to patients who are seeking transplant.
    • Provides information and assists the team and patient with referral to community resources (home health services, vocational rehabilitation, etc.) to facilitate optimal treatment outcomes.
    • Maintains current knowledge regarding local vocational/educational rehabilitation programs and assist patients with referral and access to vocational rehabilitation to enable them to remain employed, become employed or receive education.
    • Assesses patient awareness of advance directives; assists with accessing advance directive forms/information and facilitates discussion of advance directive wishes, if necessary, with the healthcare team and the patient's family/support persons.
    • In collaboration with the physician and nurse, participates in the discussion of patient DNR status in the facility to ensure patient and/or family understand and make an informed decision about their care.
    • Knowledgeable of and adheres to FMCNA Social Work Policy and Measuring Patient Physical and Mental Function Policy, including documentation.
    • Documents based on MSW interaction and interventions provided to patient and/or family.
  • Quality
    • Provides psychosocial support and/or Social Work Focused Interventions to address non-adherence, quality outcome, and quality of life concerns for all patients based on acuity level.
    • Participates in monthly Quality review meetings with the interdisciplinary team. Reports on quality indicators related to adherence, such as Missed and Shortened Treatments, Quality of Life Trends, and Service Recovery.
  • Patient Education
    • Assesses patient knowledge of kidney disease for barriers that may affect adherence to treatment. Works with patient, family and health care team to provide education tailored to the patient’s learning style, communication barriers, and needs.
    • With other members of the interdisciplinary team, provides appropriate information about all treatment modalities.
    • Facilitates the transplant referral process and collaborates with interdisciplinary team on transplant waitlist management.
    • Provides ongoing education to patient/family regarding psychosocial issues related to End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) and all support services that are available.
    • Reviews patient rights and responsibilities, grievance information (company and network) and other facilities policies with patient and/or the patients’ representative to ensure patients’ understanding of the rights and expectations of them.
    • Collaborates with the team on appropriate QAI activities.
  • Patient Admission and Continuity of Care
    • Reviews Patient Rights and Responsibilities, Grievance Procedure & Important Numbers Handout, FKC Non-discrimination policy, DNR Statement (if applicable) and address any immediate needs/concerns.
    • Understands the referral and admission process and supports the clinic in regard to the patient needs for scheduling to maximize adherence and adjustment.
    • The Social Worker will interview the patient to identify root causes or concerns for the discharge request, (i.e. transfer to hospice, relocation, dissatisfaction with services or staff) and share causes/concerns with operational leadership,
  • Insurance and Financial Assistance
    • Collaborates and functions as a liaison for patient with Insurance Coordinators to address issues related to insurance.
    • In collaboration with Insurance Coordinators, provides information and education to patients about payment to dialysis (federal, state, commercial insurance, state renal programs, AKF HIPP, and entitlement programs).
    • Collaborates with the Insurance Coordinator of any changes to patient state that impacts insurance i.e. transplantation, discharged, loss of coverage, or extended travel.
    • Refers patients to patient billing solutions (PBS) department for questions/concerns in regard to treatment related bills
  • Works with the administrative support staff to maintain updated patient resource lists (e.g. maintain updated list of transportation resources).
  • Provides training to staff pertaining to psychosocial topics as needed.
  • Contributes and participates with weekly team huddles. Discusses any urgent patient issues with staff.
  • Adheres to work defined caseload guidelines based on state regulatory requirements.
  • Performs other related duties as assigned.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

  • The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  • Travel required (if multiple facilities)

SUPERVISION:

  • None

EDUCATION AND REQUIRED CREDENTIALS:

  • Masters in Social Work
  • Must have state required license

EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS:

  • 2 – 5 years’ related experience

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Commercial Used Truck Sales Manager
description:
🚛 Lead the Market. Own the Inventory. Drive the Results.

Commercial Used Truck Sales Manager

💰 Base Salary: $80,000–$115,000

Performance-Based Leadership Opportunity

Build a business. Lead a team. Maximize profitability.

Are you the type of sales leader who sees opportunity where others see inventory?

At TCI Transportation, we're looking for an experienced Commercial Used Truck Sales Manager to lead one of our fastest-growing business segments. This isn't just another dealership management role—it's an opportunity to build a high-performing operation, develop a winning sales culture, and drive meaningful business growth.

If you know the commercial truck market, thrive on competition, and enjoy turning strategy into results, we want to talk with you.

🎯 What You'll Be Responsible For

🚚 Lead the day-to-day operations of TCI's Used Truck Sales division.

📈 Develop inventory, pricing, merchandising, and sales strategies that maximize revenue and gross profit.

💰 Acquire quality inventory through:

  • Trade-ins

  • Fleet relationships

  • Lease returns

  • Auctions

  • Wholesale channels

  • Dealer partnerships

🔍 Evaluate & Price medium- and heavy-duty commercial trucks using market trends and valuation expertise.

📊 Monitor Performance through key business metrics including:

✔ Gross Profit

✔ Inventory Turn

✔ Aging Inventory

✔ Average Days to Sale

✔ Closing Ratios

✔ CRM Compliance

✔ Forecast Accuracy

✔ Sales Activity

🤝 Build Relationships with fleet operators, dealerships, wholesalers, transportation partners, and commercial customers.

👥 Recruit, Coach & Develop a motivated sales team focused on accountability, execution, and customer service.

📱 Drive CRM Excellence by ensuring pipeline management, forecasting, reporting, and customer follow-up are consistently executed.

🚀 Partner Across Departments with Service, Operations, Marketing, Finance, and Executive Leadership to continuously improve business performance.

⭐ What Makes You a Great Fit

You bring...

✅ 5+ years of leadership experience in:

  • Used Commercial Truck Sales

  • Fleet Sales

  • Truck Dealership Operations

  • Transportation Equipment Sales

  • Commercial Vehicle Sales Leadership

✅ Strong understanding of Class 5–8 truck markets

✅ Proven success improving sales, margins, inventory turn, and profitability

✅ Experience managing and developing successful sales professionals

✅ Excellent negotiation and relationship-building skills

✅ Strong financial and analytical abilities

✅ Experience with CRM platforms, inventory systems, and reporting dashboards

✅ A bias toward action and continuous improvement

🌟 Bonus Points If You Have

⭐ Medium- or Heavy-Duty Truck Dealership experience

⭐ Auction purchasing and wholesale remarketing experience

⭐ Fleet lifecycle or lease return expertise

⭐ KPI-driven sales leadership experience

⭐ AI-enabled sales technology or automation experience

⭐ Commercial Driver's License (CDL)

📊 How You'll Make an Impact

Your success will be measured by your ability to improve:

📈 Revenue Growth

💵 Gross Profit

🚚 Inventory Turn

⏱ Days to Sale

📉 Aging Inventory

📋 Forecast Accuracy

🤝 Customer Satisfaction

📱 CRM Adoption

👥 Team Performance

💼 Why Join TCI?

At TCI, you'll have the opportunity to make a real impact—not just manage a department.

You'll work alongside an executive leadership team that values innovation, accountability, and smart decision-making. Your ideas will matter. Your leadership will shape the future of our Used Truck Sales division.

What We Offer

💰 Competitive Base Salary: $80,000–$115,000

🏥 Comprehensive Medical, Dental & Vision Benefits

💼 401(k) with Company Match

🏖 Paid Time Off & Paid Holidays

📈 Career Growth & Leadership Development Opportunities

🚚 Join a stable, growing company with more than 45 years of industry success

If you're looking for a position where you can truly build something, this is it.

🚛 About TCI Transportation

Over 45 Years and Counting

Since 1978, TCI Transportation has been dedicated to serving our clients with integrity, passion, and ingenuity.

Today, we're a coast-to-coast transportation partner providing logistics, leasing, rental, maintenance, and workforce solutions to some of the nation's most recognized brands.

We're still growing.
We're still innovating.
And we're still investing in exceptional people.

🚀 Ready to Take the Wheel?

Bring your leadership, market expertise, and passion for winning to a company that values initiative, accountability, and results.

Apply today and help drive the future of Commercial Used Truck Sales at TCI.

title: Profesor/a de Electrónica | Horario Flexible |
description:
Cada vez son más los estudiantes que buscan apoyo para aprender electrónica, preparar exámenes o reforzar sus conocimientos con la ayuda de un profesor particular.

En Tusclasesparticulares buscamos profesores/as de electrónica para impartir clases particulares a estudiantes de diferentes niveles.

Tareas

  • Impartir clases de electrónica adaptadas al nivel y objetivos de cada alumno.

  • Explicar conceptos de electrónica analógica, digital, circuitos y otros contenidos relacionados.

  • Preparar ejercicios, prácticas y materiales para reforzar el aprendizaje.

  • Realizar el seguimiento del progreso de los estudiantes.

Requisitos

  • Formación en Electrónica, Ingeniería Electrónica, Ingeniería Industrial, Telecomunicaciones o áreas relacionadas.

  • No es necesaria experiencia previa como profesor/a.

  • Capacidad para explicar conceptos técnicos de forma clara y práctica.

  • Compromiso con el aprendizaje de los estudiantes.

Beneficios

  • Horario flexible.

  • Posibilidad de impartir clases online, presenciales o en modalidad híbrida.

  • Retribución de 15 a 30 €/hora.

  • Tú decides tu disponibilidad y el número de alumnos que quieres atender.

title: Profesor de apoyo escolar - Campaña de Verano
description:
¡Únete a la comunidad educativa líder! En Tusclasesparticulares seguimos creciendo y buscamos Profesores/as de Apoyo y Mentores Educativos con vocación por la enseñanza y motivación por ayudar a alumnos a alcanzar su máximo potencial académico.

Si eres estudiante universitario, graduado en Magisterio, Psicología o Pedagogía, o simplemente te apasiona la docencia, ¡este es tu sitio! 🚀

📍 Elige tu modalidad:

  • Teletrabajo / Online (Desde donde quieras).

  • Presencial (En tu zona).

  • Híbrido (Tú decides).

🕐 Lo que nos hace diferentes:

  • Flexibilidad total: Tú marcas tus horarios y disponibilidad.

  • Sin experiencia previa: Valoramos tu conocimiento y tu actitud por encima de tu trayectoria.

  • Impacto real: Ayuda a estudiantes de Primaria, ESO y Bachillerato a mejorar sus resultados.

Tus funciones principales:

  • Impartir clases de refuerzo escolar en las materias que mejor domines.

  • Enseñar técnicas de estudio y organización del tiempo.

  • Motivar al alumno y realizar un seguimiento de su desarrollo académico.

  • Dinamizar el aprendizaje de forma amena y efectiva.

Qué ofrecemos:

  • Remuneración competitiva: Genera ingresos mientras disfrutas de tu tiempo libre.

  • Posiciónate en la plataforma líder de clases particulares en España.

  • Incorporación inmediata.

📩 ¡No dejes pasar el verano! Postúlate ahora, configura tu disponibilidad estival y empieza a dar clase en la mayor comunidad educativa del país.

title: IP 이커머스 플랫폼 운영 AMD
description:
About

마플코퍼레이션은 최고 수준의 IT 개발력과 제조 인프라를 보유하고 있는 기술 기반의 회사로, ‘세상의 변화를 만드는 크리에이터들의 성장을 돕는다’는 비전하에 POD (Print on demand) 커머스 플랫폼 마플, 크리에이터 커머스 플랫폼 마플샵, 넥슨 IP커머스 플랫폼 NEXON ESSENTIAL, 버추얼 · 게임 라이브 스트리밍 플랫폼 씨미를 개발/운영하고 있습니다.

• MARPPLE (marpple.com)
마플은 국내 업계 1위 굿즈/단체 상품 제작 플랫폼으로 개인을 위한 맞춤형 굿즈부터 기업형 단체 굿즈까지, 고객의 이야기를 세상에 단 하나뿐인 상품으로 만들어가는 커스텀 굿즈 제작 전문 플랫폼 입니다.

• MarppleShop (marpple.shop)
크리에이터 커머스 플랫폼 마플샵은 크리에이터가 본연의 나다움과 창의성을 발견하며, 팬과 함께 성장할 수 있는 IP커머스 생태계를 구축하는 것에 집중하는 브랜드 입니다.

• 씨미 (ci.me)
씨미는 버추얼 스트리밍과 게임 방송에 특화된 라이브 스트리밍 플랫폼으로, 스트리머와 팬이 실시간으로 소통하며 함께 성장하는 경험을 만듭니다. 방송을 넘어 커뮤니티와 굿즈/커머스까지 활동이 자연스럽게 확장되는 생태계를 지향합니다.

https://www.marpplecorp.com

Responsibilities

[담당 서비스]
• IP 이커머스 플랫폼
• 온라인 폐쇄몰

[담당 업무]
• IP 이커머스 플랫폼 운영 및 관리 (MD / AMD)
• 고객 문의 응대 및 CS 운영
• 자체 어드민을 통한 상품 등록 및 수정
• 상품 콘텐츠 운영 및 관리 (상품 촬영 핸들링, 품목고시, 상세페이지 등)
• 계약 관리 및 정산 관리
• 재고 및 물류 운영 관리
• 출고 일정 및 배송 관련 운영 업무
• 파트너사 커뮤니케이션 및 핸들링

Requirements

• 학력무관
• 이커머스 플랫폼 운영 경험 3년 이상
• 온라인 쇼핑몰 또는 폐쇄몰 운영 경험 보유자
• CS 운영 및 고객 응대 경험 보유자
• 정산 업무 경험 보유자
• 원활한 고객 및 파트너사 커뮤니케이션 역량을 보유한 분

Preferred

• 파트너사 및 고객 커뮤니케이션에 능숙한 분
• 온라인 커머스 및 IP 커머스 산업 전반에 대한 이해와 관심이 높은 분
• 꼼꼼한 업무 처리 능력을 바탕으로 정산 관리에 강점이 있는 분
• 굿즈 및 IP 상품 운영 경험이 있는 분
• 변화하는 업무 환경에 유연하게 대응할 수 있는 분
• 스스로 업무를 개선하고 주도적으로 문제를 해결하는 분
• 자기주도적으로 목표를 설정하고 실행할 수 있는 분

Benefits

정해진 틀에 무조건 맞추기 보다 목표를 달성하기 위해 효율적인 방식을 유연하게 선택합니다.
팀원이 각자 할 일을 맡아서 수행하고 결과에 책임지고 있습니다.

• 유연 근무제 시행
• 자율 출근
• Slack, Notion 을 이용한 문서 관리 및 이슈 트래킹

[휴가]
• 자율 휴가제
• 출산 휴가
• 월차, 연차

[기타]
• 고급 커피 제공 ( 셀프 드립 )
• 간식 무한 제공
• 석식 제공
• 점심 식사 비용 지원
• 경조사비
• 4대 보험
• 연차월차
• 상조제도
• 팀별 회식지원
• 야근시 택시비지원
• 자유복장

title: Product Marketing Manager – Open Finance & Developer Experience
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Product Marketing Manager – Open Finance & Developer Experience

Role Overview
We are seeking a high-impact, commercially driven Product Portfolio Marketing Manager to shape and execute go-to-market strategy for the Mastercard Open Finance & Developer Experience portfolio across priority markets.

This role partners closely with Open Finance & Developer Experience business leadership, product, sales, and regional marketing teams to translate product strategy and capabilities into clear, differentiated value propositions, scalable use cases, and revenue-driving growth programs. Play a key role in advancing Mastercard’s leadership position in Open Finance and embedded financial services.

The ideal candidate combines strong product marketing fundamentals with deep understanding of payments and finance trends, and can effectively engage diverse buying groups—from technical developers to business decision-makers,


Key Responsibilities

Portfolio Positioning & Messaging

Define and evolve portfolio-level open finance messaging and value propositions grounded in customer needs, buyer personas, and market trends

Translate product capabilities into commercially meaningful use cases and solution narratives

Ensure consistency and differentiation across products and markets

Partner with product to shape roadmap inputs based on market and customer insights

Go-to-Market & Launch Execution

Support development of use case playbooks and core GTM assets to drive adoption across priority markets

Support Tier 1 product launches and own end-to-end go-to-market for Tier 2/3 launches (positioning, naming, and execution)

Partner with cross-functional teams to ensure coordinated execution across channels and regions

Sales Enablement & Commercial Impact

Develop advanced sales enablement assets that clearly articulate value, use cases, and path to revenue

Equip sales teams with buyer-specific messaging tailored to technical and business audiences

Support regional sales teams and business development in driving pipeline, adoption, and revenue growth

Market Intelligence & Insights

Conduct competitive analysis, customer research, and trend monitoring to inform positioning and strategy

Leverage data to identify opportunities, refine messaging, and strengthen market differentiation

Campaign & Content Integration

Partner with campaign, digital, and field marketing teams to activate integrated, insight-driven campaigns

Ensure all content and campaigns reflect priority use cases and portfolio strategy

Oversee development of web, blog and social content and key marketing assets

Program Management & Performance

Manage agency partners and budgets to deliver high-quality assets and campaigns

Track and report on campaign performance and GTM effectiveness, optimizing based on results

Maintain portfolio documentation and product inventory



Qualifications:

Proven experience in product marketing, portfolio marketing, or GTM roles in B2B technology or financial services

Proactive, entrepreneurial mindset with a strong learning orientation; continuously identifies opportunities to drive innovation and accelerate growth in fast-paced environments

Proven ability to build compelling positioning and value propositions across multiple markets

Experience developing use-case-led marketing and sales enablement

Track record of executing product launches and integrated marketing campaigns

Strong cross-functional leadership skills across global, multi-cultural and remote product, sales, and regional teams

Analytical mindset with ability to turn insights into actionable GTM strategies

Strong understanding of Open Finance ecosystems and competitive landscape a plus

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

Purchase, New York: $135,000 - $222,000 USD

title: Director, Partner Success
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Director, Partner Success

Director, Partner Success

Overview
Mastercard Services leverages the company’s unique data, analytics, technology, and expertise to help customers grow, optimize performance, and manage risk. It delivers solutions across areas like fraud and cybersecurity, customer acquisition and engagement, open finance, and business insights—making commerce smarter, safer, and more personalized through data driven products and advisory capabilities. One of our strategic objectives is to diversify our revenue via new distribution pathways, including through Partners. As such, we are increasing our focus around Partner Success to support the growth of both our partner and MA revenue.

As Global Director, Partner Success, you will define and build Mastercard Services' global partner success strategy, creating the framework through which Partner Success Managers consistently deliver support customers to deliver value, accelerate product adoption, and drive long-term growth. Reporting to the Global Customer Success VP, this role sits at the intersection of Product, Customer Success, Sales, Marketing, and Partner Development, shaping how Mastercard scales customer outcomes through a global ecosystem of strategic partners.

The role will work in deep partnership with the Partner Success Managers across the business to enable partners to successfully deliver Mastercard Services solutions at scale.
This role is for a strategic builder who thrives in making sense out of complexity and can influence across the organization to develop partner success motions into a crucial component of our value delivery approach.

Role
Partner Success Strategy & Operating Model
• Own the global Partner Success vision and strategy, ensuring alignment with Services growth objectives and product priorities.
• Design and evolve the partner engagement model, including best practices in segmentation, coverage, lifecycle ownership, and digital and AI-enabled success motions.
• Establish scalable operating models that enable partners to deliver consistent customer outcomes across diverse partnership models, markets, segments, and products.

Partner Lifecycle & Capability Development
• Define the end-to-end Partner Success framework, ensuring partners are equipped to drive onboarding, adoption, value realization, renewal, and expansion.
• Create standards and methodologies that drive consistency, quality, and scalability across the partner ecosystem.

Adoption of strategy, operating model and frameworks
• Accountable for the adoption of this vision, operating model and frameworks to drive consistency in how Partner Success is managed at Mastercard
• Drive operating efficiency to allow our Partner Success managers to more effectively deliver partner outcomes

Performance, Insights & Governance
• Create recommendations on Partner Success KPI’s, ensuring alignment on data availability/hygiene through to incentive plans to simply and accurately track progress
• Leverage customer, partner, and market insights to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, translating findings into scalable actions and operating model improvements.

Cross-Functional Leadership & Growth
• Partner closely with Product, Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Partner Development leaders to embed Partner Success priorities into business planning, product strategy, and go-to-market execution.
• Translate product and business objectives into prioritized Partner Success initiatives, programs, and measurable outcomes.
• Shape partner-led growth motions that accelerate adoption, expand customer value, and unlock new revenue opportunities across the Services portfolio.
• Inform capacity planning and annual budget planning
• Represent Partner Success on the Partner Business Development Leadership Team, serving as the global voice of partner-delivered customer value and ecosystem performance.

All About You
• Experience in customer success, product management, consulting, or related roles, with a track record of delivering measurable outcomes
• Experience working in partner-led or ecosystem models, with an understanding of indirect customer engagement
• Proven success improving retention, increasing adoption, and identifying growth opportunities with channel partners
• Collaborative, proactive leader who builds strong relationships and contributes to a high-performing culture
• Strong communication and storytelling skills, translating complexity into clear, executive-ready narratives
• Strong data fluency, with experience using analytics and AI-driven insights to inform performance and strategy
• Ability to define success metrics, interpret partner and customer signals, and translate insights into scalable actions
• Customer centric mindset with a focus on scalable, innovative solutions
• Proven ability to influence and collaborate across Product, Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success in a matrixed environment
• Excellent analytical and problem-solving capabilities with a data-driven approach

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
