title: International Payroll Specialist
description:
About the role

Are you an experienced payroll professional who wants to do more than run payroll? In this role, you will have the opportunity to build, improve and scale international payroll processes in a modern, fast-growing SaaS environment. We are looking for an International Payroll Specialist who enjoys taking ownership, creating structure and making payroll work smoothly across multiple countries.

This is a role for someone who wants to combine hands-on payroll expertise with process development, stakeholder collaboration and the chance to make a real impact as we continue to grow internationally.

In this role, you will be part of the Finance department and report to the Director of Financial Operations. You will work closely with People & Culture, Finance, managers and external payroll providers to ensure accurate, timely and high-quality payroll delivery across our markets.

What you will do

· Take full ownership of end-to-end international payroll delivery, ensuring accuracy, timeliness and high-quality standards.

· Map current local payroll workflows and design a unified, scalable global payroll operating model that improves efficiency and reduces manual errors.

· Standardize and optimize payroll processes connected to onboarding and offboarding, ensuring smooth data flow between HR, managers and external vendors.

· Act as system owner for payroll and time-attendance platforms, driving improvements and ensuring reliable system usage.

· Evaluate, select, negotiate with and implement external payroll providers in both new and existing markets.

· Lead payroll-related projects from start to finish, including implementation plans, timelines, budgets and change management across local entities.

· Ensure international payroll processes comply with local tax laws, labour regulations and data privacy standards such as GDPR.

· Coordinate external payroll audits and support strong payroll governance across all markets.

· Act in line with our company values: Take Ownership, Build Trust, Drive Innovation and Succeed Together.

About you

· Have higher education within payroll from a Swedish institution, such as vocational education/YH, or a degree in payroll administration.

· Bring several years of qualified payroll experience, preferably from a coordinating or leadership role.

· Have solid experience managing and scaling payroll across multiple countries simultaneously.

· Have experience selecting, negotiating with and managing external global payroll providers such as Papaya Global, ADP or Deel.

· Enjoy collaborating closely with People & Culture/HR teams, Finance and external partners.

Skills & systems

· Strong Excel skills and confidence working with payroll data, reports and reconciliations.

· Experience working with modern HRIS platforms such as Workday, HiBob or Humaans, and an understanding of how they integrate with payroll ecosystems.

· Fluency in both English and Swedish, with the ability to communicate clearly in writing and verbally.

· Experience with Visma is considered a plus, or a willingness to develop your knowledge further.

Personal attributes

· You are structured, detail-oriented and comfortable working in an environment where accuracy, confidentiality and discretion are essential.

· You take ownership of your work and enjoy improving processes, simplifying workflows and creating structure where needed.

· You are a clear and collaborative communicator who can build trust with internal stakeholders, external providers and colleagues across different countries.

· You are proactive, solution-oriented and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining quality and attention to detail.

· You are curious, pragmatic and motivated by contributing to a payroll function that supports both employees and the business.

Other information

Type of employment: Permanent, full-time

Location: Hybrid working model with our Stockholm office as your main location.

Start: As soon as possible

Interested?

If you are ready to take ownership of international payroll and help create scalable, compliant and efficient payroll processes, we would love to hear from you.

Apply today and join us on our journey.

About Truvio

At Truvio you will be part of a fast-growing modern SaaS environment who brings together established Microsoft Dynamics ISVs in one unified company, delivering a single, intelligent product portfolio built exclusively for Dynamics 365. This way we help customers and partners close the ERP Value Gap by unifying finance, operations, and commerce within a Microsoft-native suite.

We are a team of more than 450 employees worldwide, with colleagues based across North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia. With our core values Take ownership, Build Trust, Drive innovation and Succeed together do we have a mission to help organizations get more from their ERP investments.

title: Outpatient Registered Nurse - RN
description:
PURPOSE AND SCOPE:

The professional registered nurse Outpatient RN CAP 1 is an entry level designation into the Clinical Advancement Program (CAP). This position is accountable and responsible for the provision and coordination of clinically competent care including assessment, planning, intervention and evaluation for an assigned group of patients. This may include delegation of appropriate tasks to direct patient care staff including but not limited to RNs, LVN/LPNs and Patient Care Technicians. As a member of the End Stage Kidney Disease (ESKD) health care team, this position participates in decision-making, teaching, leadership functions, and quality improvement activities that enhance patient care outcomes and facility operations.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

All duties and responsibilities are expected to be performed in accordance with Fresenius Kidney Care policy, procedures, standards of nursing practice, state and federal regulations.

· Performs all essential functions under the direction of the Supervisor and with guidance from the Clinical Educator, Preceptor or in collaboration with other Registered Nurses.

· Performs ongoing, systematic collection and analysis of patient data pre – during – post hemodialysis treatment for assigned patients and documents in the patient medical record, makes adjustments or modifications to treatment plan as indicated and notifies Team Leader, Charge Nurse, Supervisor or Physician as needed.

· Assesses, collaborates, and documents patient/family’s basic learning needs to provide initial and ongoing education to patients and family.

· Directs and provides, in collaboration with direct and ancillary patient care staff, all aspects of the daily provision of safe and effective delivery of hemodialysis therapy to assigned patients.

· Administers medications as prescribed or in accordance with approved algorithm(s), and documents appropriate medical justification and effectiveness.

· Initiates or assists with emergency response measures.

· Serves as a resource for health care team, participates in staff training and orientation of new staff as assigned.

· Ensures correct laboratory collection, processing and shipping procedures are performed and reschedules missed or insufficient laboratory collections.

· Identifies expected outcomes, documents and updates the nursing assessment and plan of care for assigned patients through collaboration with the Interdisciplinary Team.

· Ensures patient awareness related to transplant and treatment modality options.

· Required to complete CAP requirements to advance.

· Performs all other duties as assigned by Supervisor.

PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORKING CONDITIONS:

The physical demands and work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

· The position provides direct patient care that regularly involves heavy lifting, moving of patients and assisting with ambulation. Equipment aids and/or coworkers may provide assistance.

· This position requires frequent, prolonged periods of standing and the employee must be able to bend over.

· The employee may occasionally be required to move, with assistance, machines and equipment of up to 200 lbs., and may lift chemical and water solutions of up to 30 lbs. as high as 5 feet.

· The work environment is characteristic of a health care facility with air temperature control and moderate noise levels.

· May be exposed to infectious and contagious diseases/materials.

· Day to day work includes desk work, computer work, interaction with patients, facility/hospital staff and physicians.

· The position may require travel to training sites or other facilities.

· May be asked to provide essential functions of this position in other locations with the same physical demands and working conditions as described above.

SUPERVISION:

Assigned oversight of Patient Care Technicians/LPNs/LVNs/RNs as a Team Leader or designated Nurse in Charge, after meeting all the following:

· Successful completion of all FKC education and training requirements for new employees.

· Must have a minimum of 9 months experience as a RN.

· Must have a minimum of 3 months experience in chronic/acute hemodialysis as a RN.

EDUCATION and LICENSURE:

· Graduate of an accredited School of Nursing.

· Current appropriate state licensure.

· Current or successful completion of CPR BLS Certification.

· Must meet the practice requirements in the state in which he or she is employed.

EXPERIENCE AND REQUIRED SKILLS:

· Entry level for RNs with less than 2 years of Nephrology Nursing experience as a Registered Nurse.

· Chronic/acute hemodialysis experience (preferred).

Fresenius Medical Care maintains a drug-free workplace in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: Director, Specialist Sales - SME Solutions
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Director, Specialist Sales - SME Solutions

Who is Mastercard?

Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.
Role Overview
Location: Riyadh
• The Director – SME Specialist Sales is based in Riyadh, leads the KJB cluster covering KSA, Jordan and Bahrain, and reports to the SME Head of Commercialization.
• This role requires a strategic, consultative mindset and the ability to operate credibly as a trusted advisor to clients, combining high-level thinking with strong execution discipline.
• Lead the development and execution of SME growth strategies by combining sales leadership with strategic advisory support, helping clients identify opportunities, solve business challenges and unlock revenue and profitability through tailored solutions.
• Build and manage a strong pipeline while engaging clients through a hands-on, consultative approach, including on-site client interactions, needs assessment, opportunity shaping and solution positioning across Financial Institutions and non-financial institution partners.
• Identify, originate and convert new business opportunities, develop SME ecosystem plays, and drive the commercialization of signed deals to deliver measurable revenue outcomes.
• Contribute to achieving SME P&L objectives across key customers in the KJB cluster, with primary focus on KSA, Jordan and Bahrain, aligned to regional expert sales priorities and business goals.
• Identify product, proposition and business innovation opportunities across SME payment segments.
• Provide subject matter expertise and advisory leadership by working closely with clients and internal stakeholders to diagnose needs, shape strategic recommendations and lead end-to-end execution across sales, product and market teams.
• Lead, coach and develop a high-performing team culture that promotes accountability, collaboration, strong execution and continuous improvement.
Qualifications Required
• Deep knowledge of SME products and services, including SME card and solutions offerings, with significant experience in sales and business development within the SME space.
• Progressive business experience with a strong track record in the payments industry and a solid understanding of commercial and consumer payments.
• Strong strategic thinking and consulting capabilities, including the ability to assess client needs, translate market insights into actionable recommendations and influence decision-making at senior levels.
• Experience in consulting, solution selling or advisory-led client management, with a strong track record of working directly with customers in on-site environments to shape opportunities, guide implementation and deliver business outcomes.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Partner Account Manager - SaaS Reseller Partnerships (Europe)
description:
About Varnish Software

Varnish Software is a Scandinavian-based tech company developing caching technology for some of the world's biggest players in the content delivery market. Our technology helps businesses worldwide improve website performance, deliver video streams, and scale their infrastructure.

About the role

As our Partner Account Manager, you'll play a key role in growing our reseller business in Europe. You'll own the commercial relationship with our reseller partners - from identifying and onboarding new partners to enabling their success and helping them grow their business with Varnish.

This is a highly collaborative role where you'll work closely with Sales, Marketing and Product while serving as the trusted advisor to your partner portfolio.

Your mission is simple: empower partners to confidently sell, provision, bill, and support Varnish's SaaS solutions, and grow together.

Key Responsibilities

  • Identify, recruit, and negotiate agreements with strategic reseller partners across Europe.

  • Lead the commercial and operational onboarding of new partners, ensuring they understand our reseller model, pricing structure, and provisioning tools.

  • Deliver sales enablement and Train-the-Trainer sessions that equip partner sales teams to confidently position and demonstrate our solutions.

  • Develop annual and quarterly business plans with partners, tracking performance against forecasts and growth targets.

  • Support partners on strategic sales opportunities through co-selling while maintaining a partner-first approach and ensuring they own the customer relationship.

  • Conduct Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) to evaluate business performance, identify expansion opportunities, reduce churn, and strengthen partner success.

  • Build trusted relationships with key stakeholders, including senior decision-makers and C-level executives.

Required Skills & Experience

  • 5+ years of experience in SaaS partner management, channel sales, or business development.

  • Proven experience working with Value-Added Resellers (VARs), Managed Service Providers (MSPs), Professional Services organizations, hyperscaler marketplaces, or similar partner ecosystems.

  • An established network of potential partners within Europe.

  • Strong relationship management skills with the ability to engage and influence stakeholders at executive level.

  • A commercial, data-driven mindset with experience managing forecasts, sales pipelines, and business performance.

  • Strong business acumen combined with the ability to understand technical concepts and communicate the value of SaaS solutions effectively.

  • Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken.

    We may not be able to provide visa/work permit sponsorship for this role. Current authorization to work in EU/EES will be needed to complete the hiring process.

Who You Are

We're looking for someone who enjoys building lasting relationships and creating value through collaboration. You're commercially driven and thrive in an environment where you can combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution. You take ownership of your work, are proactive in identifying opportunities, and are motivated by helping partners succeed.

You communicate with confidence, adapt easily to different stakeholders and cultures, and build credibility through trust and expertise. You're equally comfortable discussing commercial strategy with executives as you are collaborating with technical teams. Most importantly, you're curious, solution-oriented, and excited about being part of a growing international technology company where your impact will be visible.

About the team 💙

Our team in Europe plays a crucial role in understanding our clients' need, implementing solutions effectively, and forging long-lasting partnerships.

We embrace a company culture inspired by innovation and teamwork. For us, a sense of belonging, equality and trust is key for building high performing teams who thrive as professionals and individuals.

How to Apply

If you're excited about building strong partnerships and helping shape the future of SaaS infrastructure, we'd love to hear from you. Ready to join our team? Simply click the "Apply" button to start your application process. We look forward to learning more about you and how you can contribute to our success.

Please note that, due to the holiday season, it may take us a little longer than usual to get back to you regarding your application.

title: [AX] AI 리서치 엔지니어
description:
About

[콕스웨이브와 Gen-AI Transformation의 미래]
LLM의 등장은 단순히 기술의 진보를 넘어, 인간과 기계가 소통하는 근본적인 언어를 바꾸고 있어요.
콕스웨이브는 이러한 시대적 흐름을 'Gen-AI Transformation(AX)'으로 정의하며, 단순히 AI 기술을 선보이는 것을 넘어 실 사용자의 삶에 선명하고 확실한 임팩트를 남기는 사용자 경험을 창조해요.

[우리가 걸어온 독보적인 여정]
콕스웨이브는 글로벌 빅테크 기업들과 긴밀하게 호흡하며 전례 없는 속도로 성장하고 있어요.
• 2026.01: 70억 원 규모 Pre-A 투자 유치 (총 누적 투자액 125억 원 달성)
• 2026.01: OpenAI와 공동으로 '멀티 에이전트 워크플로우 해커톤' 개최
• 2025.09: 개인 맞춤형 AI 튜터 에이전트 플랫폼 '에듀탭(EduTap)' 정식 출시
• 2025.03: Claude 개발사 Anthropic과 파트너십 체결 및 '코리아 빌더 서밋' 공동 주최
• 2025.01: 대규모 기업을 위한 Enterprise 솔루션 정식 출시
• 2025 : 글로벌 테크(Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA, AWS, PwC 등)와 긴밀한 협력 및 파트너십 구축
• 2023.10: SaaS 솔루션으로 Product Hunt 'Product of the Day' 3위 달성

[우리가 일하는 방식]
• Trust & Honesty | 투명한 신뢰: 우리는 건강한 충돌을 두려워하지 않아요. 솔직한 피드백으로 서로의 성장을 돕고, 끈끈한 신뢰를 바탕으로 동료의 성공을 위해 아낌없이 지원해요.
• Logical Leadership | 논리적 주도성: 직급과 연차에 상관없이, 가장 합리적이고 논리적인 제안이 의사결정의 중심이 돼요. 누구나 오너십을 가지고 스스로의 업무를 정의하며 리딩해요.
• Total Immersion | 압도적 몰입: 우리는 단순한 'Task'를 처리하는 데 그치지 않아요. 우리가 만드는 기술이 세상에 남길 실질적인 가치에 집중하며, 정점에 도달하기 위해 치열하게 몰입해요.

[우리가 해결하려는 문제]
• AI가 기업의 핵심 업무에 깊숙이 들어올수록 가장 중요한 질문은 하나로 좁혀져요. "이 에이전트 정말 비즈니스를 맡길 만큼 믿을 수 있는가?"
• 사용자 만족도 평가 혹은 출시 전 QA 는 일시적인 해결책으로, 엔터프라이즈 환경에서 언제든 치명적인 결과로 이어질 수 있어요. 상시로 동작하는 체계적인 AI 통제 시스템이 반드시 마련되어야 기업에서도 믿고 활용할 수 있는 기반이 갖춰져요.
• 우리는 기업들이 안심하고 사용할 수 있는 에이전트 통제의 새로운 기준을 세우고, 이 기술적 난제를 함께 돌파할 동료를 찾아요.

Responsibilities

• Agent 오류 탐지 및 벤치마크 구축: 에이전트의 환각, 추론 오류, 도구 사용 실패 같은 문제를 잡아내는 모델을 연구해요. 감에 의존하지 않고 수치로 증명할 수 있도록, 자체 벤치마크 데이터셋과 자동화된 평가 파이프라인을 직접 만들어요.
• Agent 파이프라인 최적화: 비용, 추론 속도, 정확도 사이의 최적점을 찾아요. 프롬프트 엔지니어링부터 전체 파이프라인 설계까지 다루며, 실제 서비스에서 Agent가 최고의 성능을 내도록 개선해요.
• Research-to-Production: 연구실 수준의 실험 코드(PoC)를 실제 프로덕션에 올릴 수 있는 상태로 다듬어요. 엔지니어링 팀이 안정적으로 서비스에 연동할 수 있도록 코드를 리팩토링하고 테스트 코드를 작성해 이관해요.
• 논문 및 특허 자산화: 업무 과정에서 풀어낸 어려운 문제들과 독창적인 해결책을 그냥 휘발시키지 않아요. 이를 정리해 탑티어 학회(NeurIPS, ICLR 등)에 논문을 투고하거나 특허를 출원하여 우리 팀만의 확실한 기술 자산으로 만들어요.

Requirements

• AI (ML/LLM) 역량: Transformer 구조와 LLM의 동작 원리를 이해하고 있으며, PyTorch, HuggingFace 등 Python 기반 ML 프레임워크를 실무 수준에서 막힘없이 다룰 수 있어요.
• 에이전트 설계 및 평가: Multi-Agent 시스템이나 ReAct 등을 활용해 에이전트를 직접 설계하고 제어해 본 분. 또한 LLM-as-a-Judge 같은 최신 평가 방법을 이해하고 실제 코드에 적용해 봤어요.
• 빠른 논문 구현 역량: 최신 논문을 읽고 끝나는 것이 아니라, 그 안의 복잡한 아이디어를 실제 돌아가는 코드로 빠르게 구현해서 테스트해 볼 수 있어요.
• 문제 정의와 주도성: 위에서 내려오는 태스크만 처리하는 것이 아니라, 현장의 진짜 문제를 스스로 찾아내고 프로젝트의 시작부터 끝까지 직접 끌고 가본 경험이 있어요.
• 기술 커뮤니케이션: 본인이 해결한 복잡한 기술적 성과를 논문, 특허, 기술 문서 등으로 남이 이해하기 쉽게 정리할 수 있어요.

Preferred

• 컴퓨터 공학, AI 관련 석/박사 학위를 보유하시거나, 그에 준하는 실무 연구 및 개발 경험이 있어요.
• 대규모 벤치마크 데이터셋을 직접 만들어 보거나, 에이전트가 실패하는 케이스 및 안전성 에 대해 깊게 연구해 봤어요.
• vLLM 등을 활용해 추론 환경을 최적화해 보거나 모델 경량화 경험이 있어요.
• 자신의 기술적 성과를 특허로 출원 및 등록해 본 경험이 있어요.

Benefits

• 최신 업무 장비 제공
• 근로계약 외 별도 식대 소정 제공
• 간식 및 음료 제공
• 야근 시 저녁 식대 지원
• 오후 11시 이후 퇴근 시 택시비 지원
• 월간 팀 회식비 지원
• 경조사 지원 및 근속 연차별 포상 제도 운영
• 업무 관련 도서 지원
• 업무 유관 컨퍼런스 참가비 지원

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Software Engineer

Who is Mastercard? Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.

Overview
The Fraud Products team develops fraud prevention and identity verification solutions on Mastercard's Decision Management Platform. Our high-performance Java platform processes billions of transactions each month, delivering the scale, speed, and reliability that power Mastercard's global payments network.

Role
•Design, develop, and support scalable, high-performance applications.
•Build secure, reliable, and maintainable solutions for complex business challenges.
•Participate in Agile development, design reviews, code reviews, and security reviews.
•Troubleshoot production issues and optimize applications for performance and reliability.
•Drive engineering best practices, automation, and continuous improvement.
•Collaborate with global teams to deliver software at scale.

All About You
•Strong experience with Java/J2EE, Spring Boot, and distributed systems.
•Experience building and supporting high-performance, highly available applications.
•Strong understanding of software design, architecture, data structures, secure coding, and testing practices.
•Experience troubleshooting production issues and improving system performance, scalability, and reliability.
•Experience with code reviews, operational excellence, and production support.
•Hands-on experience with Kubernetes, Cloud Foundry, Linux, Shell Scripting, Oracle/PLSQL, and SQL.
•Experience working in Agile development environments.
•Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience.

Required Technical Skills
•Core Java (including multithreading)
•Java/J2EE
•Spring Framework / Spring Boot
•Distributed Systems & Distributed Computing at Scale
•Kubernetes, Cloud Foundry
•Linux & Shell Scripting
•Oracle, PL/SQL, Advanced SQL
•Software Design & Architecture
•Secure Coding Practices
•Unit, Functional & Integration Testing
•Performance Engineering
•Code Reviews & Troubleshooting
•IntelliJ or JBoss Developer Studio

Desirable Skills
•Experience with distributed computing platforms and in-memory data grid technologies at scale, such as:
GemFire
Apache Spark
Distributed Redis
Hazelcast
GridGain / Apache Ignite

•MQ/JMS messaging technologies
•Solaris/UNIX or Red Hat Linux
•Experience integrating vendor and open-source solutions into enterprise platforms

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. title: School Meal Cover (part time) - Meath
description:
A little bit about us

Looking for a part-time job that actually makes a difference - and gives you something new every day?

At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help kids build healthy habits for life.

We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are!

So, what’s the job?

We’re on the hunt for a Part-Time School Meals Cover Worker - someone reliable, adaptable and ready to jump into action when needed to keep things running like clockwork.

This is a part-time role working Tuesdays and Wednesdays during the school term.

This isn’t your typical 9 - 5. You’ll be our on-the-ground hero, providing cover across our schools when needed, bringing the smiles and making sure no child goes hungry. If you’re looking for routine and predictability, this might not be for you. But if you like variety, enjoy being out and about, this could be your perfect fit - keeping you on your toes and feeling young!

Just like our regular school meals workers, you will be the smiley face representing us in the school. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the schools. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven and then distributing them to classrooms.

If there’s no need for staff cover on a given day, you’ll head out to schools to carry out simple (but effective) audits. Our goal is always to provide the best possible service to children - and that includes staying on top of hygiene standards and following all HACCP and EHO guidelines.

If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you:

  • Go into a new work environment each day with enthusiasm and confidence.

  • Load pre-packaged school meals into ovens.

  • Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines.

  • Unload ovens and distribute lunches to classrooms.

  • Ensure adequate stock levels.

  • Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule.

  • Maintain temperature and cleaning records.

  • Restock the fruit bowls in each classroom.

  • Distribute snacks to classrooms.

  • Conduct audits in schools to ensure compliance with EHO requirements.

  • HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided.

What we’re looking for

Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers - people who are sound, good-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal.

You’ll need to be a quick learner, great with people, and happy to go with the flow.

A full driving licence and your own transport are essential - some schools might be just around the corner, others could be a bit of a spin. But that’s part of the fun - you’ll get to know every back road and coffee spot in your area!

We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every school in Ireland has a lift (just yet)!

Working with children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly.

If you’ve worked with kids or food before, great. If not, don’t let it put you off - we value people from all backgrounds and we’ll show you the ropes.

All you need is the ability to read English, follow instructions, and heat each lunch with care.

I still have questions actually…

No problem! We’re here to answer them.

  • When will I work?

You will need to be available Tuesdays and Wednesdays from 7:00 am to 3:30 pm. Your precise hours each day will depend on the schools' requirements.

  • Will I have to work all year long?

No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work during the school terms and have the school holidays off to enjoy some well-deserved rest!

  • What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag?

Well, for starters - a decent rate of pay and a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back.

The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms.

And best of all? You get to be part of a lovely school environment, surrounded by friendly faces (and the occasional tiny comedian in a uniform).

  • How much is it paid?

It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. This part-time role for Tuesday & Wednesday role pays €232 per week, and we’ll also cover your expenses for using your own car.

title: Supply Chain – Planning Process Expert (Sr Manager equivalent)
description:
Position Location Details: Upon agreement between you and your Supervisor, you are entitled to a flexible arrangement where you will be able to split your time between working from the Waltham, MA or Franklin, TN office and working remotely.

Supply Chain – Planning Process Expert

We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic Supply Chain Planning Expert to lead the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of integrated planning capabilities across the organization. This role will play a critical part in transforming end-to-end supply chain planning processes through advanced planning solutions, data-driven decision-making, and cross-functional collaboration.

The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in supply chain planning, SAP S/4HANA and IBP implementations, demand and supply planning, process governance, and business transformation within complex operational environments. The role requires a strong combination of strategic thinking, technical capability, stakeholder engagement, and operational execution.

Organization

The function reports to: Senior Director, US Process Owner Plan-to-Treat (PTT). Organizational unit: Global Procurement

Key Responsibilities

Project Implementation & Planning Transformation

  • Lead the definition and implementation of end-to-end supply chain planning process requirements across demand, supply, inventory, and integrated business planning functions.
  • Translate complex US-specific clinical and market demand patterns into scalable technical requirements within SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP environments.
  • Drive planning solution design, configuration alignment, and deployment activities to support business transformation initiatives.
  • Oversee data model design, master data readiness, integration validation, user acceptance testing (UAT), cut-over planning, and go-live execution.
  • Collaborate with business, IT, and implementation partners to ensure successful delivery of planning capabilities aligned with organizational objectives.
  • Identify process gaps, system inefficiencies, and optimization opportunities to improve planning accuracy, responsiveness, and operational agility.

End-to-End Process Governance

  • Define, shape, and continuously evolve the integrated planning vision, ensuring strong alignment between demand, supply, inventory, and broader business priorities.
  • Establish governance frameworks, planning standards, and performance metrics to drive consistency and operational excellence across planning functions.
  • Champion standardization, simplification, and harmonization of planning processes across US Clinics business unit.
  • Drive the adoption of advanced analytics, scenario planning, forecasting methodologies, and digital planning capabilities to improve decision quality and business outcomes.
  • Act as a trusted strategic advisor to business stakeholders by translating market dynamics, demand variability, and operational constraints into actionable planning insights.
  • Facilitate cross-functional alignment between Supply Chain, Commercial, Operations, Finance, and IT teams to support integrated decision-making.
  • Support organizational change management initiatives and ensure successful user adoption of new planning processes and technologies.

Experience, know-how and skills critical for success

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Operations, Engineering, Business, Information Systems, or related field.
  • 10+ years of experience in supply chain planning, integrated business planning, or supply chain transformation roles.
  • Proven experience leading or supporting SAP S/4HANA and SAP IBP implementation programs.
  • Strong understanding of demand planning, supply planning, inventory optimization, forecasting, and Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP)/Integrated Business Planning (IBP).
  • Experience translating business requirements into technical and functional solution designs.
  • Demonstrated expertise in planning process governance, operational excellence, and large-scale transformation initiatives.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to influence senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience within healthcare, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, clinical supply chain, or highly regulated industries.
  • Knowledge of advanced analytics, predictive forecasting, and scenario modeling techniques.
  • Familiarity with project delivery methodologies and enterprise transformation programs.
  • APICS/ASCM certification or equivalent supply chain certification preferred.
  • Experience working within global, matrixed organizations.

Key Competencies

  • Strategic Planning & Execution
  • Integrated Business Planning (IBP)
  • SAP S/4HANA & SAP IBP Expertise
  • Demand & Supply Planning
  • Forecasting & Scenario Analysis
  • Process Governance & Standardization
  • Cross-Functional Leadership
  • Data-Driven Decision Making
  • Change Management
  • Stakeholder Engagement & Influence

Other

1) Languages: English Advanced, other languages an advantage

2) Travel requirements: Willingness to travel for business purposes (up to 20%)

The rate of pay for this position will depend on the successful candidate’s work location and qualifications, including relevant education, work experience, skills, and competencies.

Annual salary: $117,700.00 - $196,200.00 - Waltham, MA location

Benefit Overview: This position offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) with company match, paid time off, parental leave and potential for performance-based bonuses depending on company and individual performance.

Fresenius Medical Care is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, parental status, national origin, age, disability, military service, or other non-merit-based factors

title: B2B Field Marketing Manager
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

B2B Field Marketing Manager

Our Purpose

We work to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. We cultivate a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects their individual strengths, views, and experiences. We believe that our differences enable us to be a better team – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results.

Title and Summary

Marketing Manager, B2B Field Marketing – North America

Overview:

Mastercard Services Marketing is seeking an experienced B2B Field Marketing Manager to help drive North America demand generation, field engagement, and regional campaign execution. This role will translate global Services priorities into market-relevant programs that support pipeline creation, sales acceleration, and customer engagement across priority solutions and segments.

As a key member of the North America field marketing team, you will partner closely with sales, business development, product marketing, regional marketing, and global marketing teams to develop and execute integrated campaigns. These programs will leverage field events, executive engagements, account-based marketing, digital activation, content, and post-event nurture to reach the right audiences and move opportunities forward.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role for a marketer who thrives in a fast-moving, matrix environment. The ideal candidate is commercially minded, highly organized, comfortable working with sales stakeholders, and energized by turning strategy into well-executed programs that create measurable business value.

Role:

• Manage and execute in-market campaigns including digital advertising campaigns, virtual events including webinars, and development of sales enablement content with the goal of driving Data & Services sales with merchant organizations.
• Contribute to in-market event (owned events and industry events) execution including branding and content development of US merchants event strategy and lead the event planning and execution.
• Analyze campaign performance against evaluation metrics (e.g., leads and sales opportunities generated), and consistently communicate outcomes to stakeholders while identifying opportunities to improve performance.
• Effectively partner with third parties (e.g., marketing agencies, event organizers, etc.) and internal partners to drive desired outcomes.
• Partner closely with sales and business development teams to align campaign priorities, support target account engagement, optimize lead handoff, and track post-campaign follow-up.
• Plan and manage regional events, trade shows, executive briefings, and other field activities that build brand relevance, deepen customer engagement, and generate qualified leads.
• Own hands-on campaign execution and performance reporting for field events, target account marketing activities, and regional demand generation programs.
• Navigate Mastercard approvals, contracting, budget, and reporting processes with strong attention to detail and operational discipline.
• Coordinate with regional business owners, country teams, product marketing, partner marketing, customer marketing, and sales teams to deliver integrated programs that are relevant to North America market needs.

All About You:

• Minimum of 7 years of B2B marketing experience, with strong experience in field marketing, demand generation, account-based marketing, or event-led customer engagement.
• Self-directed, creative, and commercially minded; able to proactively bring ideas forward, solve problems, and find practical ways to improve campaign performance and customer engagement.
• Strong writing and messaging skills for field marketing activities, including speaker submissions, stage presentations, event invitations, campaign localization, direct promotion copy, and sales enablement materials.
• Proven ability to work effectively in a matrixed marketing organization and build strong relationships with sales, product, regional marketing, and global marketing partners.
• Hands-on experience developing email campaigns, event communications, nurture journeys, and other campaign assets.
• Detail-oriented, well organized, and able to manage multiple programs, stakeholders, timelines, and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
• Analytical and performance-oriented, with the ability to pull, interpret, and present marketing data to inform decisions and communicate results.
• CRM experience required; Salesforce.com experience preferred.
• Marketing automation experience required; Salesforce Marketing Cloud experience preferred.
• Comfortable traveling up to 25% of the time to support North America events, customer engagements, and internal planning moments.
• Bachelor’s degree in marketing, business, communications, or a relevant field of study preferred.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Mastercard is a merit-based, inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if you require accommodations or assistance to complete the online application process or during the recruitment process, please contact reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do not include any medical or health information in this email. The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your email promptly.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

In line with Mastercard’s total compensation philosophy and assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the successful candidate will be offered a competitive base salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain part time) employees generally include: insurance (including medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life insurance); flexible spending account and health savings account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S. observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match; deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities in some locations.

Pay Ranges

Arlington, Virginia: $135,000 - $222,000 USD

Atlanta, Georgia: $117,000 - $193,000 USD

Boston, Massachusetts: $135,000 - $222,000 USD

Purchase, New York: $135,000 - $222,000 USD

title: Retail Operations Specialist US
description:
About TOTEME

TOTEME is a fashion house rooted in Swedish sensibilities, modern aspirations and the notion of style. With an appreciation for women’s many roles and how pieces are worn in practice, we create emblematic designs with an emphasis on materiality and shape. Representing a direct and decisive way of dressing, the collections are contextualized in curated edits, visuals and spaces.

TOTEME was founded in 2014 by Elin Kling and Karl Lindman. From the atelier in Stockholm, we create collections spanning ready-to-wear, shoes, bags, accessories and jewelry.

The Role

TOTEME is looking for a Retail Operations Specialist - US to join our Team in New York City full time. In this role you will oversee and support the operations of our retail spaces across the US and coordinate communication and planning to and from headquarters.

The role will function as a bridge between the store teams and the support functions at headquarters, and act as a retail advocate to such teams; Supply Chain, Buying, Planning, E-commerce, Tech, Production, Finance & Store Development. You will report to the Retail Operations Manager and work directly with the corporate retail team.

Responsibilities

  • Oversee and optimize store operations, KPI’s and related costs.

  • Execute and support the planning of operational activities, such as price changes, promotions, and product launches.

  • Create, update and maintain comprehensive handbooks, manuals and policies related to retail operations.

  • Support with tech related issues and proactively work with improvements within the digital infrastructure.

  • Communicate company information, guidelines and updated procedures to store teams, and follow up on adaption, serving as a liaison between HQ and store teams, ad-hoc and through planned touchpoints.

  • Support and/or manage maintenance and store touch-ups and own contacts with local vendors, keeping track of budgets and approvals from Store Design & Development.

  • Monitor store stock levels, deliveries and replenishment, ensuring stock integrity.

  • Encourage the use of tools and systems to drive operational excellence and improve communications, including point of sale (POS) and Inventory Management Systems, Reporting tools and other retail programs or applications.

  • Support with in-store tech and IT, such as keeping inventory of and ordering phones, tablets, printers and scanners when needed.

  • Manage relationships with third-party vendors for services such as security systems, internet, cleaning and maintenance.

  • Participate in and contribute to TOTEME’s social and environmental sustainability strategy.

Your Profile

  • Minimum five years of experience from operations in luxury retail environments.

  • Experience from an international fashion retail business

  • Broad understanding and expertise within IT & Tech

  • Hands-on experience with business related systems such as POS, OMS, Inventory Management- and BI-tools.

  • Previous experience with Shopify, Sitoo, iD Cloud, Omnium & Qlik is meriting.

  • Proficient in Office Tools; Excel, PPT/Keynote, Google Docs & Google Drive

  • Excellent organizational and numerical skills.

  • Fluent in spoken and written English.

About You

  • A strong understanding of luxury retail and the fashion industry.

  • Service minded, excellent communication skills with a mindset of that nothing is impossible.

  • Commercial mindset, best-in-class operational skills, and a high attention to service and detail.

  • Comfortable in navigating a complex tech- & IT infrastructure.

  • Proficient in finding, analyzing & visualizing data from different sources.

  • A proven ability to set clear goals and translate them into actionable roadmaps,

  • always focused on solutions.

  • Excellent project management skills with the ability to multitask, prioritize and

  • meet deadlines.

  • Ability to work independently and to adapt in a fast-paced, changing environment.


This is a full-time position based at our New York office, with a start date by agreement.

The monthly salary for this role ranges between 75,000 and 80,000 USD before tax and will be set according to the applicant’s profile, skills and previous experience.

TOTEME has teams in Stockholm, London, New York, and Shanghai, as well as retail spaces across Europe, North America, and Asia.

TOTEME has an internal career development and title framework and the job title used in the recruitment ad may differ from the title used internally at TOTEME.

Candidates progress through application, screening, interviews or case assessments, followed by final pre-employment checks and a formal offer before joining our team. We anonymize names and personal details in applications during the screening stage to support fair and skills-based hiring.

By applying to this position, I agree with TOTEME’s privacy policy and to be contacted about future job opportunities for up to two years from the submission date. Find out more about our privacy policy here.

title: Interest & Product Logic Specialist
description:
We are looking for a specialist who combines financial know-how with strong analytical skills to join our Core Ledger team. Our platform contain core ledger systems both fully developed internaly and developed and delivered by external suppliers. As an Interest & Product Logic Specialist, you will define and document the business rules behind interest, fees, and product configurations, and verify and test that what our internal- and external suppliers deliver matches our requirements and works correctly. A significant part of this role is providing hands-on internal support to colleagues across the business who have questions about interest, fees, and product behaviour — this is something all specialists in the team spend a lot of time on. Getting this right is essential — it directly affects how accurate and competitive our financial products are. You will join a small, close-knit team and have a great opportunity to make a direct impact early on and help shape it as it grows.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and document detailed requirements for interest calculation logic, accrual rules, and fee structures, to be delivered by external suppliers

  • Translate financial product requirements from business and product teams into precise specifications for our suppliers

  • Verify and test the product configuration delivered by suppliers, ensuring it behaves correctly for all financial instruments

  • Ensure the mathematical and financial correctness of calculation logic delivered, including edge cases and regulatory requirements

  • Collaborate with finance, compliance, and product teams to validate that supplier deliveries meet business intent

  • Support audit and regulatory reporting by ensuring calculation logic is transparent, traceable, and well-documented

  • Investigate and resolve discrepancies in interest calculations, fee postings, or product behaviour, working with suppliers when needed

  • Define and execute test cases for financial calculation logic, including boundary and regression tests

  • Provide hands-on internal support to other teams (finance, customer service, operations, and others) on questions about interest, fees, and product behaviour — this is a significant and ongoing part of the role

Qualifications and Experience

Desired Skills

  • 4+ years of experience in a financial, analytical, or product role within financial services, lending, or payments

  • Strong understanding of interest calculation methodologies (e.g. effective interest rate, simple interest, compound interest, XIRR)

  • Experience writing detailed financial business rules and specifications for systems built by others

  • Solid grasp of product structures in consumer credit, BNPL, or instalment lending

  • Ability to work with complex financial specifications and verify that delivered systems implement them correctly

Nice to Have

  • Knowledge of Nordic or European consumer credit regulation (e.g. Consumer Credit Directive)

  • Experience with configuration-driven financial product frameworks delivered by suppliers

  • Background in actuarial science, quantitative finance, or financial mathematics

  • Familiarity with accounting principles as they relate to interest and fee recognition

Behaviours

  • Exceptional analytical rigour — comfortable with mathematical precision and financial logic

  • Service-minded and approachable — enjoys helping colleagues understand interest, fee, and product logic

  • Strong bridge-builder between finance/business language and supplier requirements

  • Thorough documenter — understands that correctness must be explainable and auditable

  • Curious and proactive in challenging assumptions and validating requirements

  • Collaborative, with an ability to work effectively with business stakeholders and external suppliers

Education

BSc or MSc in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.

Languages

Fluent English is required for this role. Swedish language is a plus.

What We Offer

  • A key role in building and shaping a modern, cloud-native core ledger platform

  • A collaborative, cross-functional team environment with short decision paths

  • Competitive salary and benefits package

  • Flexible working arrangements with hybrid options

  • Opportunities for professional development and continuous learning

  • A chance to make a real impact in the fintech space across the Nordic market

  • Modern office in central Hamburg with a strong team culture

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Software Engineer

Software Engineer – Dev Cloud, Platform & Container Enablement

About the Role
Software Engineers in this role focus on enabling developer productivity and platform reliability through strong cloud, container, and internal developer platform (IDP) capabilities. You will design, build, and support Dev Cloud solutions that power secure, scalable, and developer-friendly experiences across the organization. This role blends software engineering, platform engineering, and operational excellence, with a strong emphasis on Docker Desktop support, Backstage, AWS, and cloud-native tooling.
All team members are expected to demonstrate Mastercard Way cultural values—own it, simplify it, sense of urgency, thoughtful risk-taking, unlock potential, and be inclusive—with a relentless focus on developer experience and customer outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
Design, build, and operate Dev Cloud capabilities that enable engineers to develop, test, and deploy software efficiently and securely.
Own and support Docker Desktop and container-based development workflows, including troubleshooting developer issues and improving local-to-cloud parity.
Build and extend Backstage as an internal developer platform, including software templates, plugins, golden paths, and service catalogs.
Develop and maintain cloud-native services and automation on AWS, leveraging managed services and infrastructure-as-code practices.

Partner with application teams to onboard services to Dev Cloud and Backstage, improving consistency, discoverability, and operational readiness.
Automate and simplify developer workflows across build, test, deploy, and runtime operations.
Introduce new tools, patterns, and architectures aligned with enterprise engineering standards.
Drive reliability, observability, and security best practices across platform services.
Collaborate cross-functionally with security, infrastructure, and application teams to resolve complex, multi-service issues.
Mentor engineers and contribute to a culture of continuous improvement and platform excellence.
All about you-Technical Focus Areas

Dev Cloud & Platform Engineering: Internal platforms, self-service capabilities, golden paths, and developer experience optimization.
Containers & Local Development: Docker Desktop, container orchestration concepts, and developer tooling support.
Backstage: Software catalogs, templates, plugins, integrations, and adoption strategies.
Cloud (AWS): Core AWS services, IAM, networking, compute, storage, monitoring, and cost-aware design.
Automation & IaC: CI/CD, scripting, and infrastructure-as-code to enable repeatable, secure environments.
Observability & Operations: Logging, metrics, tracing, and operational readiness in cloud-native environments.

Corporate Security Responsibility


All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;

  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;

  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and

  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Stödpedagog sökes till Unika Kvicksund
description:
Om Unika

Verksamheten ligger belägen i Kvicksund och vänder sig till LSS personkrets 1.

Om rollen som Stödpedagog

I rollen som stödpedagog kommer du att ge stöd och service utifrån de boendes önskemål, både i hemmet och i fritidsaktiviteter. Du ska bidra till att den boende ges möjlighet till en aktiv och meningsfull tillvaro. Vidare ska du kunna se till varje persons individuella behov och kunna bedriva pedagogiskt arbete utifrån detta. Då behovet är olika bland brukarna är ingen dag sig lik vilket bidrar till ett utmanande och spännande jobb.

Du kommer att arbeta brukarnära tillsammans med dina kollegor och har ett stort ansvar att, i samråd med verksamhetschef, driva och utveckla det pedagogiska arbetet på verksamheten. Vissa delegerade hälso- och sjukvårdsuppgifter ingår i arbetet.

Om rollen som Stödassistent

Som stödassistent kommer du att arbeta med att ge individuellt anpassat stöd till personer med funktionsvariationer, med målet att främja deras självständighet och välbefinnande i vardagen. Du kommer att stödja brukarna i deras dagliga rutiner och aktiviteter, utifrån deras behov och förmågor, och alltid ha deras personliga genomförandeplan som utgångspunkt. Som stödassistent arbetar du utifrån tydliggörande pedagogik och lågaffektivt bemötande för att skapa trygghet och förutsägbarhet i brukarnas vardag. Samtidigt har vi ett starkt fokus på att arbeta professionellt och effektivt inom verksamhetens resurser och budget.

Kvalifikationer

Du är relationsskapande, lugn, stabil och kan hålla ett realistiskt perspektiv på situationer som uppkommer i det dagliga arbetet. Du har en god samarbetsförmåga och relaterar till andra på ett lyhört sätt samt trivs med att samverka med andra. Du är en självständig, ödmjuk och ansvarsfull person som brinner för omsorgsarbete och kan se individens behov.

Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.

Krav stödpedagog:

  • Examen från stödpedagogutbildningen 200 YH poäng eller 60 HP inom pedagogik.

  • Erfarenhet samt kunskap om målgruppen LSS vuxna personkrets 1.

  • Grundläggande kunskaper om hushållsarbete såsom kost och hygien.

  • Datorvana samt goda kunskaper i svenska språket, i tal och skrift.

Krav stödassistent:

  • Mycket god förmåga i svenska i tal såväl som skrift.

  • God datorvana, erfarenhet av dokumentation.

  • Erfarenhet av arbete inom vård och omsorg

  • Gymnasial utbildning som tex barn-och fritidsprogram, vård-och omsorgsprogram, eller eftergymnasial utbildning tex stödpedagog, socialpedagog eller högskoleutbildning inom relevant område.

Meriterande om du därtill har:

  • Erfarenhet av arbetsledning

  • Erfarenhet av att ha jobbat med människor med autism och utmanande beteende.

  • Erfarenhet av att arbeta med individer med komplex psykosocial beteendeproblematik.

  • Erfarenhet av tydliggörande kommunikation och lågaffektivt bemötande.

Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet.

Anställningsvillkor

Anställningsform: Tillsvidare (Provanställning 6 månader)
Tjänstgöringsgrad: %
Arbetstid: Dag, kväll och helg
Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse, vi ser positivt på om du kan tillträda inom kort.
Kollektivavtal: Vi är kollektivavtalsbundna till Vårdföretagarna/Kommunal, Bransch E.

Vad kan vi erbjuda dig?

Attendo är en trygg arbetsgivare som kan erbjuda dig som vill goda möjligheter att ta mer ansvar eller att gå vidare till nya roller. Vi erbjuder dig som medarbetare utbildningsmöjligheter, kollektivavtal, friskvård samt flera andra förmåner och personalrabatter via vår förmånsportal AttendoPlus, i samarbete med Benifex.

Om rekryteringsprocessen

Före eventuell anställning ska utdrag ur belastningsregistret tillsammans med giltig fotolegitimation visas upp. Om du blir kallad till en intervju behöver du kunna styrka att du har rätt att arbeta i Sverige, genom att uppvisa att du har medborgarskap inom EU/EES eller ett giltigt arbetstillstånd. Sista ansökningsdag 2026-08-31 men urval och intervjuer sker löpande, så vänta inte med din ansökan.

Om Attendo/Unika

Unika är en del av Attendo, som är ett av Nordens största omsorgsföretag och startades 1985. Vi är verksamma inom LSS. Våra värderingar omtanke, engagemang och kompetens vägleder oss dagligen i vårt arbete för att alla ska uppleva trivsel, delaktighet, gemenskap och arbetsglädje.

Välkommen med din ansökan!
Tillsammans för en meningsfull vardag!

Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta:

Herash.azizi@unika.se

title: School meal worker (Hot Drop) - Tullamore GAA
description:
A little bit about us

Looking for a job that actually makes a difference - and fits into your day-to-day routine?

At The Lunch Bag, we deliver healthy, hot school meals to children across Ireland. We're the country’s leading school meal provider, and we’re on a mission to help children build healthy habits for life.

We’re changing children’s lives, one lunch at a time. Are you flexible, bubbly, and keen to bring a little extra joy to the school day? We’re looking for people who want to help us keep driving forward and doing something great. Bonus? You’re off when the schools are!

So, what’s the job?

We’re on the hunt for School Lunch Operatives – reliable, cheerful people who take pride in doing things right and bring a bit of warmth to every school day.

Think of yourself as the final link in the lunch chain. All meals are prepared in our headquarters in Nenagh, Tipperary, and then delivered to the Hot Drop Locations. It’s from there that you’ll take over. Your duties will consist of heating the meals in the oven, loading them into vans and driving them to the schools.

If you’re a detail addict and thought this was a little vague, here’s a list for you:

  • Load pre-packaged school meals into ovens.

  • Turn on oven and heat meals to specific temperature according to HACCP guidelines.

  • Unload ovens and load into vans.

  • Drive the lunches in a company vehicle to the local schools.

  • Ensure adequate stock levels.

  • Follow and maintain work and cleaning schedule.

  • Maintain temperature and cleaning records.

  • HACCP and Manual Handling Training will be provided.

What we’re looking for

Since you’ll be representing The Lunch Bag in schools, we’re looking for doers - people who are sound, good-natured, and genuinely care about doing a good job. Our values matter to us, and to keep improving the lives of children across the country, we need people who share that same goal.

We’re not asking for bodybuilders, but you do need to be able to lift 14kg boxes as part of the job. You may also need to climb some stairs – not every facility in Ireland has a lift

Working for children is really rewarding – their cute little faces can brighten your day... just don’t let them get hungry or they’ll turn into tiny monsters! So we’re looking for someone who can work quickly, stay calm under pressure, and still do things properly.

All you need is the ability to read English, have full clean Irish driving license, and heat each lunch with care.

We’re looking for cheerful people who like being out and about. Teleportation would make life easier, but until that arrives, we need drivers who are happy to take a roughly one-hour trip to deliver meals to local schools.

I still have questions actually…

No problem! We’re here to answer them.

When will I work?

This job is offered on a part-time basis, 5 hours a day, Monday through Friday! Typical hours of work are 08:00 to 13:00 with some flexibility required as start and finish times may be a little earlier or later.

Will I have to work all year long?

No! As schools close for the summer, we follow the academic calendar. As a result, you will work with us approx 183 days of the year and will have the rest off to enjoy some well-deserved rest! Our staff sign on to Social Welfare as Educational Sector Workers during the summer months.

What benefits do I get by joining The Lunch Bag?

Well, for starters - a genuinely supportive team who’ve got your back.

The role’s flexible, ideal if you’re looking to ease back into work or just want something steady during school terms.

How much is it paid?

It’s THE question everybody is asking! The same way we take care of the children in the schools, we take care of our people. You will earn €14.65 per hour – 50c above minimum wage.

title: commissioning engineer
description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This position reports to:

Commissioning&Warranty Suport Specialist

__

Your role and responsibilities

In this role, you will have the opportunity to act as commissioning lead for projects with low to medium complexity. Each day, you will ensure completion of all commissioning activities efficiently and cost-effectively in accordance with contract specifications, quality standards, safety requirements, and cybersecurity policies and standards. You will also showcase your expertise by supporting continuous improvement of project process/product/system design.

The work model for the role is: {onsite}

You will be mainly accountable for:
  • Lead and coordinate pre-commissioning and commissioning activities for ABB MV and LV switchgear, including inspection readiness, test preparation, execution, punch list closure, energisation readiness, and handover documentation.
  • Plan, sequence, and control commissioning works in alignment with Project Manager, Commissioning Manager, Site Manager, Installation Supervisor, package engineers, contractors, customer representatives, and field service engineers.
  • Perform or coordinate electrical testing and commissioning activities, including functional checks, interlocking verification, protection and control interface checks, alarm/status verification, trip/close command checks, and system readiness verification as applicable to the project scope.
  • Lead IEC 61850-related communication testing with switchgear and associated protection/control systems, including signal mapping verification, GOOSE/MMS communication checks where applicable, communication interface troubleshooting, and coordination with automation/protection teams.
  • Ensure commissioning design changes, site modifications, red-line mark-ups, and as-built inputs are properly captured, reviewed, and submitted in line with project document control requirements.
  • Prepare commissioning method statements, inspection and test plans, commissioning procedures, check sheets, test records, progress reports, punch lists, and final handover dossiers as required by the project.
  • Drive proactive issue identification, technical clarification, escalation, and closure to avoid delays to energization, customer acceptance, or contractual milestones.
  • Qualifications for the role
  • Degree/Diploma in Electrical Engineering or equivalent
  • At least 5 years of Electrical testing & commissioning relevant experience
  • Electrical MV/LV Design Thinking
  • Fail-Safe Philosophy
  • Cyber Security
  • Agile Methodology
  • Planning & Prioritizing
  • Risk Management
  • Strong communication and site coordinate skills
  • Technical background on E-house Module (PMS, HVAC, UPS etc)
  • Advantage if have ECAP3 certificate

What’s in it for you?

We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day.

More about us

As a pivotal division within ABB Energy Industries, PAEN (Process Automation Energy Industries) leads the transformation of global energy and industrial sectors through cutting-edge automation, electrification, and digital solutions. We specialize in delivering integrated systems for process industries—spanning oil and gas, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy—to optimize efficiency, sustainability, and operational resilience. Our innovations, such as Ethernet-APL-enabled process automation platforms and AI-driven energy management systems, empower clients to achieve carbon neutrality and future-ready operations.

Call to Action

Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world.

Run What Runs the World.

Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.

Ready to make an impact?

Apply today or visit https://www.abb.com to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.
